lines.text: Well, it's not so much that you know, like I don't believe in it, you know, it's just...I don't know, lately I get the feeling that I'm not so much being pulled down as I am being pushed.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Uh-Oh. It's Isaac Newton, and he's pissed.

lines.speaker: MR. TREEGER
lines.text: There she is. And over there, that's the other one. This is Mr. Buddy Boyle, Mr. Heckles' attorney. He'd like to talk to you.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: What can we do for you?

lines.speaker: MR. BOYLE
lines.text: All right, kids. Here's the deal. According to my client's will, he wants to leave all his earthly possessions to "the noisy girls in the apartment above mine".

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, what about his family?

lines.speaker: MR. BOYLE
lines.text: He didn't have any.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, so let's talk money.

lines.speaker: MR. BOYLE
lines.text: All right, there was none. Let's talk signing. You be noisy girl number one, you be noisy girl number two.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I can't believe that this whole time we thought he hated us. I mean, isn't it amazing how much you can touch someone's life, without even knowing it?...Would you look at this dump? He hated us. This is his final revenge!

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Have you ever seen so much crap?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Actually, I think this apartment sullies the good name of crap

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Check this out. Can I have this?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: How can you not believe in evolution?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Just don't. Look at this funky shirt!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Pheebs, I have studied evolution my entire adult life. Ok, I can tell you, we have collected fossils from all over the world that actually show the evolution of different species, ok? You can literally see them evolving through time.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Really? You can actually see it?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You bet. In the U.S., China, Africa, all over.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: See, I didn't know that.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, there you go.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Huh. So now, the real question is, who put those fossils there, and why?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey, look at this. "My Big Book of Grievances."

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, there's me! April 17th. Excessive noise. Italian guy comes homes with a date. Hey Chandler, look, you're in here too.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: April 18th, excessive noise. Italian guy's gay roommate comes home with the dry-cleaning. Well that's excellent.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Monica, Monica, look at this lamp. Is this tacky or what? We have to have this.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Rache, I think we have enough regular lamps.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What? Come on, it's not like I'm asking for this girly clock or anything, which, by the way, I also think is very cool.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: It doesn't go with any of my stuff.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, what about my stuff?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You don't have any stuff.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You still think of it as your apartment, don't you?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yes you do. You think of it as your apartment, and I'm just somebody who rents a room.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Mmmmm.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, while you "mmm" on it for awhile, I'm gonna go find a place for my new lamp.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, Pheebs. See how I'm making these little toys move? Opposable thumbs. Without evolution, how do you explain opposable thumbs?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Maybe the overlords needed them to steer their spacecrafts.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Please tell me you're joking.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Look, can't we just say that you believe in something, and I don't.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, no, Pheebs, we can't, ok, because--

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: What is this obsessive need you have to make everyone agree with you? No, what's that all about? I think, I think maybe it's time you put Ross under the microscope.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Is there blood coming out of my ears?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Check it out, check it out. Heckles' high school yearbook.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Wow, he looks so normal.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: He's even kind of cute.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: "Heckles, you crack me up in science class. You're the funniest kid in school.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Funniest? Heckles?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: That's what it says.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Wow, Heckles was voted class clown, and so was I. He was right. Would you listen to that?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I'd call that excessive.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Whoa!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Heckles played clarinet in band, and I played clarinet. And he was in the scale modeler's club, and I was, well, there was no club, but I sure thought they were cool.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: So, you were both dorks. Big deal.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I just think it's weird, you know? Heckles and me, Heckles, and me, me and Heckles...Would you knock it off?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Have you been here all night?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Look at this. Pictures of all the women that Heckles went out with. Look what he wrote on them. Vivian, too tall. Madge, big gums. Too loud, too smart, makes noise when she eats. This is, this is me. This is what I do. I'm gonna end up alone, just like he did.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Chandler, Heckles was a nut case.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Our trains are on the same track, ok? Yeah, sure, I'm coming up 30 years behind him, but the stops are all the same. Bitter Town. Aloneville. Hermit Junction.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: All right, you know what we gotta do? We gotta get you outta here. Come on, I'll buy you breakfast, let's go.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: What if I never find someone? Or worse, what if I've found her, but I dumped her because she pronounced it "supposably"?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Chandler, come on, you're gonna find somebody.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: How do you know that? How?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I don't know, I'm just tryin' to help you out.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You'll see, you guys are all gonna go off and get married, and I'm gonna end up alone. Will you promise me something? When you're married, will you invite me over for holidays?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, I don't know. I don't know what we're gonna be doin'. I mean, what if we're at her folks' place?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, I understand.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: You can come over and watch the Super Bowl. Every year, all right?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You know what? I'm not gonna end up like this. I'll see you man.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Supposably. Supposably. Did they go to the zoo? Supposably.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hi, it's me.

lines.speaker: JANICE
lines.text: Oh, my, god.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Janice? You called Janice?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yes, Janice. Why is that so difficult for you to comprehend?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You remember Janice, right?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yes. She was smart, she was pretty, and she honestly cared about me. Janice is my last chance to have somebody.

lines.speaker: JANICE
lines.text: Helloo!!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, my, god!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Geez, look how fat she got.

lines.speaker: JANICE
lines.text: Hey, it's everybody.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Janice, you're--

lines.speaker: JANICE
lines.text: Yes, I am.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Is it--?

lines.speaker: JANICE
lines.text: Is it yours? Ha! You wish, Chandler Bing. You are looking at a married lady now.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Congratulations.

lines.speaker: JANICE
lines.text: Oh, sweetie, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You couldn't have told me about this on the phone?

lines.speaker: JANICE
lines.text: And what? Missed the expression on your face? Janice likes to have her fun.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey, Rache. You know what we haven't played in a while?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hide the Lamp.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Monica, let it go.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Did you know I was allergic to shellfish?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, then, you'll just have to eat the other lamps.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Uh-oh. It's Scary Scientist Man.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, Phoebe, this is it. In this briefcase I carry actual scientific facts. A briefcase of facts, if you will. Some of these fossils are over 200 million years old.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, look, before you even start, I'm not denying evolution, ok, I'm just saying that it's one of the possibilities.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: It's the only possibility, Phoebe.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, Ross, could you just open your mind like this much, ok? Wasn't there a time when the brightest minds in the world believed that the world was flat? And, up until like what, 50 years ago, you all thought the atom was the smallest thing, until you split it open, and this like, whole mess of crap came out. Now, are you telling me that you are so unbelievably arrogant that you can't admit that there's a teeny tiny possibility that you could be wrong about this?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: There might be, a teeny, tiny, possibility.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I can't believe you caved.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: You just abandoned your whole belief system. I mean, before, I didn't agree with you, but at least I respected you. How, how, how are you going to go into work tomorrow? How, how are you going to face the other science guys? How, how are you going to face yourself? Oh! That was fun. So who's hungry?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I am. Let me just get my coat.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, all right. It was an accident, I swear, all right. I was putting on my jacket, and the thing, and the lamp, and it broke.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, please, Monica. You've always hated my lamp, and then, all of a sudden, it's just magically broken?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Phoebe, tell her!

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, I didn't see it, because I was putting on my jacket, but I uh want to believe you.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey Chandler. Monica just broke my seashell lamp.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Neat. I'm gonna die alone.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, you win.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Chandler, you're not gonna die alone.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Janice was my safety net, ok? And now I have to get a snake.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Uh huh. Why is that?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: If I'm gonna be an old, lonely man, I'm gonna need a thing, you know, a hook, like that guy on the subway who eats his own face. So I figure I'll be Crazy Man with a Snake, y=know. Crazy Snake Man. And I'll get more snakes, call them my babies, kids will walk past my place, they will run. "Run away from Crazy Snake Man," they'll shout!

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You have got to get over this. You're not gonna end up alone.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Of course I am. I reject anyone who's crazy enough to actually go out with me, and then I bitch about the fact that there aren't any great women out there.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Chandler, you have just described virtually every man that we have ever gone out with.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You are not a freak. You're a guy.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: She's right. She's right. You are no different than the rest of them.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Wait a minute, wait a minute. Yes he is. You are totally different.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: In a bad way?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No, honey, in a wonderful way. You know what you want now. Most guys don't even have a clue. You are ready to take risks, you are ready to be vulnerable, and intimate with someone.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah. You're not gonna end up alone.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Chandler, you called Janice! That's how much you wanted to be with someone!

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You made it!

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: You're there!

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You are ready to make a commitment!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Whoa! Don't know about that.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What you got there? Something else that's not yours that you can break?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No. Um, I know you like this, and I want you to have it. I think it'll look good in our apartment.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: That's fine.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey. Well, you will all be pleased to know that I have a date tomorrow night. This woman, Alison, from work. She's great. She's pretty, she's smart. And uh, I've been holding off on asking her out in the past, because she has an unusually large head. But, I'm not gonna let that stuff hang me up anymore. Look at me. I'm growing.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, uh, you can't recycle yearbooks, can you?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I'll take that.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: You want his yearbook?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. Some people said some nice things about him. I think somebody should have it.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, gosh, this is so weird. I mean, his whole life was in this apartment, and now it's gone. You know, I think it would be nice if we just took a few moments, for Mr. Heckles. I mean, he was kind of a pain, he was, but, he was a person. You're all going to hell.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: It's really not that big!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Takin' that with you, huh?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh, yeah.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You comin'?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, jus' second. Good-bye Mr. Heckles. We'll try to keep it down.

lines.speaker: ALISON
lines.text: Oh, my major was totally useless. I mean, how often do you look in the classifieds and see "Philosopher wanted"?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Sure. (My god, that's a big head! It didn't look this big in the office. Maybe it's the lighting. My head must look like a golf ball at work. All right, don't get

lines.speaker: hung up on it, quick, quick, list five things you like about her
lines.text: Nice smile, good dresser...Big head, big head, big head!)

title: The One With Phoebe's Husband

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Mom, would you relax. That was 10 blocks from here and, the, the woman was walking alone at night, I would never do that. Mom, c'mon, stop worrying. This is a safe street, this is a safe building, there's nothing [a pigeon flies in the window and lands on the table] OH MY GOOOD, oh my God, oh I gotta go, I gotta go, I gotta go. [hangs up] OK, that's fine, you just read the paper, I'm gonna get a pot, it's not for you. [grabs a pot and lid] OK, that's fine, read the Family Circus, enjoy the gentle comedy. [puts pot over the pigeon] Aaahh, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, aaaaahh. [knock at the door] It's open you guys. [a stranger enters with flowers]

lines.text: Hi.

lines.text: Hi, hi can I help you?

lines.speaker: STRANGER
lines.text: Yeah, I'm looking for Phoebe, does she still live here?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Uh, no she doesn't but I can, I can get a message to her.

lines.speaker: STRANGER
lines.text: Great. Uhh, just tell her her husband stopped by. [leaves flowers on bar]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What? [in surprise she forgets she has the pigeon in the pot and lets it get away]

lines.speaker: STRANGER
lines.text: Hey, how, how did you do that? OPENING TITLES

lines.text: What!

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Wait a minute, who told you? [turns to Chandler who's looking sheepish] You are dead meat.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I didn't know it was a big secret.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh it's not big, not at all, you know, kinda the same lines as, say, oh I don't know, having a third nipple.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You bitch.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I don't know, you see somethin', you hear a word, I thought that's what it was. Let me see it again.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Yeah, show it. Show it. The nubbin, the nubbin, the nubbin.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Huuh.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, no. For your information I'm going to see him so I can put all those feeling behind me. OK, and the reason I'm dressed like this is because I think it's nice to look nice for your gay husband.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh I see, I see, because of the third nipple thing. Ha ha ha ha. . .

lines.text: Bye-bye Julie. [Julie leaves] [Rachel is still cleaning, Ross is laying on the couch. Ross kicks Rachel in the butt.]

lines.text: No, no no no, don't need to know the details.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: It's just, it's, it's me. You, you know I've only been with one woman my whole life and she turned out to be a lesbian. So now I've got myself all psyched out, you know, and it's become, like this, this thing and I. . . Well, you just must think I'm weird.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No, no, no, no I don't think it's weird, I think, I think umm, in fact, in fact you know what I think?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I think it's sexy.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Sexy?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Let me tell you something. As a woman there is nothing sexier than a man who does not want to have sex.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No kidding?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh yeah. In fact you know what I'd do?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I'd wait.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You'd wait?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yes, absolutely. I would wait and wait. . . then I'd wait some more.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh yeah, I don't care how much she tells you she wants it, I don't care if begs, she pleads, she tells you she, she's gonna have sex with, with another man. That just means it's working.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Women really want this?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: More than jewelry. [Rachel struts off, extremely pleased with herself]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ta-da.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Ahh, look at you, you look great.

lines.text: OK.

lines.text: Um, now. Phoebs.

lines.text: Oh, right, OK. Ole.

lines.text: Nice work my friend.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Thank you. Wait wait wait wait, you see me again. Hang on, the guy's butt's blockin' me. There I am, there I am, there I am, there I am, there I am. . .

scenes.location: Madison Square Garden

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: So um, so what's up, you came to see me yesterday.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Oh, yeah, um, alright, I kinda need a divorce.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ohh. . .K. How come?

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Umm, actually, I'm getting married again.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Oh God, I don't know how to tell you this. I'm straight.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Huuh.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I, I don't, I don't understand, how can you be straight? I mean, you're, you're so smart and funny and you throw such great Academy Award parties.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: I know, that's what I kept telling myself but you just reach a point where you can't live a lie anymore.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: So how long have you known?

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Well I guess on some level I always knew I was straight. I though I was supposed to be something else, you know, I'm an ice dancer, all my friends are gay, I was just tryin' to fit in.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: And um, and there's actually a, a woman?

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Her name's Debra.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh. Well is she, is she the first that you've been with?

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: Well, I've never told you this but, there were one or two times, back in college, when I'd get really drunk, go to a straight bar and wake up with a woman next to me. But I, I, I told myself it was the liquor and e-everyone experiments in college.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: But now I know I don't have a choice about this, I was born this way.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I, I don't know what to say. I mean, you know, you're married to someone for six years and you think you know him and then one day says, 'Oh, I'm not gay.'

lines.speaker: DUNCAN
lines.text: I'm, I'm still me.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Why couldn't you have just figured this out six years ago?

lines.text: No, no, I mean, no, c'mon you guys, I mean, c'mon look it's only eleven thirty. Let's just talk, we never just hang out and talk anymore.

lines.text: Maybe that's all we do, what about Julie?

lines.text: What about Julie?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: So. I mean, who here does not have the time to get to know Julie?

lines.text: I got time.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: OK Julie, so now let's start with your childhood, what was that like?

lines.speaker: JULIE
lines.text: Well, in a nutshell. . .

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Nah, uh, uh, uh, uh.

You know what, I just have one more question, um, if you had figured this out sooner and um, I had been around, do you think that I would have been the one who. . . no, um, I'm sorry, don't tell me, I don't th ink either answer would make me feel better.

lines.text: I love you Phoebe. [they hug and kiss]

lines.text: OK, now I'm nervous.

lines.text: No, no, I don't wanna put it off, I just, God I just, I spent last year being so unbelievably miserable, ya know, and now, now I'm actually happy. You know, I mean, really happy. I just, I just don't wanna, I don't wanna mess it up, ya know.

lines.text: I know, yeah, sorry.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What, it's not your fault.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Maybe it, maybe it doesn't have to be this tough. I mean, maybe you were on the right track with this whole, you know, spontaneous thing. I mean, women really like that.

lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah, I mean, you know it, I mean, if it were me I, I, you know, I'd want you to, I don't know, like catch me off guard, you know, with like a really good kiss, you know really, sort of um, soft at first, then maybe um brush the hair away from my face, and look far into my eyes in a way that let's me know that something amazing is about to happen.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [being drawn in by her talk] Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: And then, I don't know, I mean you'd pull me really close to you so that, so that I'd be pressed up, you know, right against you. And, um, it would get kind of sweaty and uh, and blurry, and then it's just happening.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ohh. . . Thanks Rach, goodnight. [goes back in apartment]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ohh, God.

lines.text: Well, you know, there's no one way really, it's just, you know, whatever it takes so that you can finally say to him, "I'm over you."

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Closure, that's what it is. Closure. [she looks around the restaurant, spotting a guy with a cellular phone] Hello, excuse me. Excuse me, hel. . . woo [she almost falls out of her chair]

lines.speaker: GUY
lines.text: Hang on.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Hello, excuse me.

lines.speaker: GUY
lines.text: What.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Hi, I'm sorry, I need to borrow your phone for just one minute.

lines.speaker: GUY
lines.text: I'm talkin'!

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: I can see that. I... just one phone call, I'll be very quick, I'll even pay for it myself. [man is still reluctant] OK, you're bein' a little weird about your phone.

lines.speaker: GUY
lines.text: Alright, fine. [on the phone] I'll call you back. [hands the phone to her]

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Thank you. OK. [dials] [to Michael] Machine. Just waiting for the beep.

lines.speaker: MICH
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: [on phone] Ross, hi, it's Rachel. I'm just calling to say that um, everything's fine and I'm really happy for you and your cat who, by the way, I think you should name Michael. And, you know, ya see there I'm thinking of names so obviously, I am over you. I am over you and that, my friend, is what they call closure. [hangs up and tosses phone in the ice bucket]

scenes.scene_description: Back in the restraunt. Rachel pours the last of the champange bottle in her glass.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler is answering the door in his robe

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: No, no, no, no, no, no [opens door to Monica] No. Monica, it's Sunday morning. I'm not running on a Sunday.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Because it's Sunday. It's God's day.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: OK, if you say stop, then we stop.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: OK, stop.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: No, c'mon, we can't stop, c'mon, we've got three more pounds to go. I am the energy train and you are on board. Woo-woo, woo-woo, woo-woo [Chandler walks out of the apartment, leaving Monica] Woo.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler is answering the door in his robe.

scenes.location: Rachel and Monica's apartment. Rachel is taking asprin. Ross enters

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey Rach.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Ahhhh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh. And how was the date?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Umm, I think there was a restaurant... I know there was wine. . . [Rachel looks at Ross as though she remembers something, but can't place what it is.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Wow, well uh, uh, actually, Julie's downstairs getting a cab, I just need the cat toy, did Monica say. . . What? Why, why are you looking at me like that?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: I don't know, I, I feel like I had a dream about you last night but I, I don't remember.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: OK. Oh, oh, oh. [runs over and picks up the cat toy]

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Did we speak on the phone last night? Did you call me?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, I stayed at Julie's last night.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, actually I haven't even been home yet. Do you mind if I check my messages?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh yeah, go ahead. [Rachel walks in her room. Ross picks up the phone and dials his machine to check his messages.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Rach, I got a message from you. [pauses] Who's Michael? [Rachel comes out of her room, suddenly she remembers leaving the message.]

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh my God. Oh my God Ross, no, hang up the phone, give me the phone Ross, give me the phone, give me the phone, give me the. . . [jumps the couch and lands on Ross's back, finally getting the phone from him. Ross has a confused expression on his face.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You're over me?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Ohhhhhhhh God. [climbs off his back]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Wha... you're uh, you're, you're over me?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Ohh, ohh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: When, when were you... under me? Rach. Rachel do you, I mean, were you, uh. . . What?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Ohh, OK, OK, OK, well, basically, lately, I've uh, I've uh, sort of had feelings for you.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You've had feelings for me?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Yeah, what, so? You had feelings for me first.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Woah. Huh. You know about my, I mean, you know I had... you know?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Chandler told me.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Chandler. When did he... when did he... when did he?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: When you were in China.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: China.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Meeting Julie.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Julie. Julie. That. Oh God. Julie, right. OK, I need to lie down. No, ya know, I'm gonna stand. I'm gonna stand, I'm gonna walk, I'm walkin' and I am standing. OK so you uh, and now wha... and now, now, now you're over me?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Are you over me? [A moment of silence.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [doorbell buzzes] That's, that's Julie. Ju... Julie, Julie. [talks on intercom] Hi Julie.

lines.speaker: JULIE
lines.text: [over intercom] Hi honey, I've got a cab waiting.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [perky] I'll be right down.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Wait, so, you're going?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, OK, I uh, I have to. I can't deal with this right now. I mean, I've uh, y'know, I've got a cab, I've got a girlfriend, I'm... I'm gonna go get a cat.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: OK, OK.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Cat. [leaves]

scenes.scene_description: Rachel and Monica's apartment. Rachel is taking asprin. Ross enters.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Joey is watching a rabbi play an electric guitar on TV. Phoebe enters

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Hey Joey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey Phoebs.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: How come you're watching a rabbi play electric guitar?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I can't find the remote. [Phoebe turns off the TV] Thank you.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: So, Scott asked me to come over for lunch today and I did.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: And we did.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: All right Phoebs, way to go.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Yay me.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: So, so how did it happen?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Well, I finally took your advice and asked him what was going on.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: And what did he say?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: He said that, um, he understands how sex can be like, a very emotional thing for a woman and he was just afraid that I was gonna get all, y'know, like, 'ohh, is he gonna call me the next day' and, y'know, 'where is this going' and, ya know, blah-la-la-la-la. So he said he wanted to hold off until he was prepared to be really serious.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Yeah, so I said, "OK, relax please," y'know, I mean, sex can be just about two people right there in the moment, y'know, it's, if he wants to see me again he can call and if not, that's fine too. So after a looooot of talking. . . I convinced him.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Let me get this straight. He got you to beg to sleep with him, he got you to say he never has to call you again, and he got you thinking this was a great idea.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Um-hum.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: This man is my God.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Joey is watching a rabbi play an electric guitar on TV. Phoebe enters.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Rachel is closing up and Ross comes in. Get your Kleenex

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I didn't get a cat.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh, that's um, interesting.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, no it's not interesting. OK, it's very, very not interesting. In fact it's actually 100 percent completely opposite of interesting.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Alright, I got it Ross.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You had no right to tell me you ever had feelings for me.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: [hurt] What?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I was doing great with Julie before I found out about you.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Hey, I was doin' great before I found out about you. You think it's easy for me to see you with Julie?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Then you should have said something before I met her.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: I didn't know then. And how come you never said anything to me.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: There was never a good time.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Right, you, you only had a year. We only hung out every night.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Not, not, not every night . You know, and... and it's not like I didn't try, Rachel, but things got in the way, y'know? Like, like Italian guys or ex-fiances or, or, or Italian guys.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Hey, there was one Italian guy, OK, and do you even have a point?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: The point is I... I don't need this right now, OK. It, it's too late, I'm with somebody else, I'm happy. This ship has sailed.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Yeah, what're you saying, you just sort of put away feelings or whatever the hell it was you felt for me?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, I've been doin' it since the ninth grade, I've gotten pretty damn good at it.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Alright, fine, you go ahead and you do that, alright Ross.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: 'Cause I don't need your stupid ship.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Good. [Ross leaves]

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: And ya know what, now I've got closure. [Rachel slams the door and locks it. She sits down, visibly upset. She puts her head in her hands and begins to cry. Ross comes back and is standing outside the window. When Rachel regroups and gets back up to finish closing, she sees him. She smiles. She goes to open the door and can't get the lock undone.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Try the bottom one. [She opens the door and they kiss.]

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Rachel is closing up and Ross comes in. Get your Kleenex.

lines.text: Monica, it's 6:30 in the morning. We're not working out, it's over.

lines.text: No way, with one pound to go, c'mon. We're workin', we're movin', we're in the zone we're groovin'.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: OK, I don't, I don't mind the last pound. OK, in fact I kind of like the last pound. OK, so don't make me do anything that I'll regret.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Ooh, what'cha gonna do, fat boy, huh? What?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Nothing, except tell you, uh, I think it's wonderful how much energy you have.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Well, thanks.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: I mean, especially considering how tough it's been for you to find work.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Well, you know.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: You know, I mean, you can't tell your parents you were fired because they'd be disappointed.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: [sad] Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: And it's not as if you have a boyfriend's shoulder to cry on.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Well no, but um.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: I mean, if it were me, I think I'd have difficulty just getting out of bed at all.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Y'know, I try to stay positive. . .

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: So, you feel like goin' for a run?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Because, you know, you don't have to. If you want, you could just take a nap right here.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: OK. Just for a little while.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: OK. [Puts an afghan over her and dances into his room]

lines.text: Oh my god, oh my god, oh my god!

lines.text: Oh my god, oh my god, oh my god!

lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: PHOE/MNCA
lines.text: Ohhhh.

lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Hey, oh, so, um...how'd you make out last night?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That, that is funny. That is painfully funny. No, wait. Wait, yeah, that's just painful

lines.text: Wait a minute. I thought last night was great.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, it was, but...I get home, ok, and I see Julie's saline solution on my night table. And I'm thinking to myself, oh my god, what the hell am I doing? I mean, here I am, I am with Julie, this incredible, great woman, who I care about and who cares about me, and I'm like, what, am I just gonna throw all that away?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: You got all that from saline solution?

lines.speaker: MNCA

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Believe me, I've been dreaming about me and Rachel for ten years now. But now, I'm with Julie, so it's like me and Julie, me and Rachel, me and Julie, me and... [Rachel enters, carrying a tray]... Rachel. Rachel, Rachel.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: How are you?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Good. How are you?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Good.

lines.text: Hi, honey.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi, Julie. [nervous] Hi, Julie. Julie, um, how are you?

lines.speaker: JULIE
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [uncomfortable] Good, so everybody's here. Everybody's good. So, were you gonna play something, Phoebe?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Oh, well, actually.

lines.speaker: ROSS

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Ok, all right.

lines.text: What?

lines.text: [to Joey] Ssshh.

lines.text: Ok, um, hi, hello, hi, ok, so, um, this is a song about a love triangle between three people that I made up. Um, it's called, um, "Two of Them Kissed Last Night".

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: [singing] There was a girl, we'll call her Betty, and a guy let's call him Neil. Now I can't stress this point too strongly, this story isn't real. Now our Neil must decide, who will be the girl that he casts aside. Will Betty be the one who he loves truly? Or will it be the one who we'll call Ju...Loolie? He must decide, he must decide, even though I made him up, he must decide!

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: This is a nice resume. Nice, nice, nice. Muy impressivo.

lines.text: So, Mr. Rastatter, what exactly does this job entail? The ad wasn't too clear.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Mockolate.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: I'm sorry?

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Mockolate. It's a completely synthetic chocolate substitute.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Ohh.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Go ahead. Try a piece. Yeah, we think that Mockolate is even better than chocolate.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: All right. Mmm-mmm.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: [disgusted, trying not to show it] I love how it crumbles. Now see, your chocolate doesn't do that.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: No, ma'am. Well, anyhoo, we should be getting our F.D.A. approval any day now, hopefully, in time for Thanksgiving. See, the way we look at it, chocolate already

lines.speaker: dominates most of your major food-preparation holidays
lines.text: Easter, Christmas, what have you.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: [still chewing] Mmm-mmm.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: But, we're thinking, given the right marketing, we can make Thanksgiving the Mockolate holiday.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Aren't you going to swallow that?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Just waiting for it to stop bubbling.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Yeah, isn't that great?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: [with false enthusiasm] Mmm.

lines.text: Well, anyhoo, um, we are looking for a couple of chefs who can create some Thanksgiving-themed recipes. You think you might be interested?

lines.text: Abso...[swallows hard]...lutely. See, I love creating new recipes. I love Thanksgiving. And, well, now, I love Mockolate.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Especially the after taste, you know, I'll tell ya, that'll last ya till Christmas.

lines.text: Oh, I know. This must be so hard. Oh, no. Two women love me. They're both gorgeous and sexy. My wallet's too small for my fifties, and my diamond shoes are too tight.

lines.text: Ok, all right, look. Let's get logical about this, ok? We'll make a list. Rachel and Julie, pros and cons. Oh. We'll put their names in bold, with different fonts, and I can use different colors for each column.

lines.text: Ok, let's start with the cons, 'cause they're more fun. All right, Rachel first.

lines.text: [in phone] So, Spock actually hugs his father?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Hey, do you guys have...[sees Ross, pauses]...hi.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: [sees his coat on] Where you goin'?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I uh, I just got back from uh, from Julie's.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: [dejected] Oh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, no, uh, it's not what you think. It's um the other thing.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Well, what's the other thing, what do I think?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, uh.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: He broke up with Julie. Well, go hug her, for god's sakes.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Really. It's always been you, Rach.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh, god.

lines.speaker: JOEY/CHAN
lines.text: Ohhh.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh, oh, this is good, this is really good.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I know, I know, it's, it's almost...[turns around, sees Chandler and Joey] What do you say we go take a walk, just us, not them?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Let me get my coat.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok. No, hey, whoa, whoa, I'll get your coat.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Ok, he's goin' to get my coat. He's goin' to get my coat. Oh my god, you guys. I can't believe this. This is unbelievable. [notices Chandler's computer screen] What's that?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: [nervous] What? Nothing.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: What's that? What? I saw my name. What is it?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: No, no, see? See? [the printer starts to run] Hey, it's printing. [to Joey, rattled] Hey, it's printing!

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Well what is it? Let me see.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, someone order a coat?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Ross, Chandler wrote something about me on his computer and he won't let me see.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: He won't? [remembers what it is] He won't! Because, isn't that, isn't that the, the short story you were writing?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Yes, yes it is, short story, that I was writing.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: And I'm in it? Then let me read it.

lines.speaker: CHAN, JOEY, ROSS
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Come on.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, uh, why don't you read it to her?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: [through gritted teeth] Alright. [clears his throat] "It was summer, and it was hot. Rachel was there. A lonely gray couch. 'Oh, look,' cried Ned, and then the kingdom was his forever. The end."

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That's it? That's all you wrote? You're the worst writer in the whole world.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: All right, you know what? This isn't funny anymore. There's something about me on that piece of paper and I want to see it.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, you don't.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: All right, you know what, that's fine. If you guys want to be children about this, that's fine. I do not need to see it. [Rachel grabs the paper and runs across the room, reading it to herself.]

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: What is this? Ross, what is this?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, just, just remember how crazy I am about you, ok?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Kind of ditzy? Too into her looks? Spoiled?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Now that's a little spoiled. He was supposed to type "little", the idiot.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Just a waitress?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, that, that was, I mean, as opposed to uh, the uh, ok. Is this over yet Rach?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh! I do not have chubby ankles!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, no, wait, ok, ok, look at the other side. Look at Julie's column.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: She is not Rachem . What the hell's a Rachem? Is that some stupid paleontology word that I wouldn't know because I'm just a waitress.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, Rach, come on. Rach! Rach, no, no! She's not Rachel, she is, she is not, Ra--Rachel?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler is on the phone with a computer hotline.

scenes.location: Central Perk . Chandler

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: My diary! My diary, that's brilliant. I should have told her it was my diary, she never would have made me read her my diary.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: You know, that's true. You'd be a great person to have around the day after an emergency.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: I... I cannot believe Ross even made this list. What a dinkus.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, cut him some slack. It was Chandler's idea.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Oh good, I was hoping that would come up.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: This was your idea?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: What were you thinking?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: [squirming] All right, let's get some perspective here, ok? These things, they happen for a reason.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Yeah. You!

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: All right, Pheebs, back me up here, ok? You believe in that karma crap, don't you?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Yeah, by the way, good luck in your next life as a dung beetle.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk . Chandler, Monica, Joey, and Phoebe are there.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Rachel is sitting on the couch

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Rach! Whoops! Rach, hey, open up, please!

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: [coldly] When somebody does not buzz you in, Ross, that means go away. That doesn't mean please climb up the fire escape.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I just wanna read something. It's your pro list.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Not interested.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [reading his list] Ok, ok, number one: The way you cry at game shows. Number two:

lines.speaker: how much you love your friends. Number three
lines.text: the way you play with your hair when you're

lines.speaker: nervous. Number four
lines.text: how brave you are for starting your life over. Number five: how great you are with Ben.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Number six: the way you smell.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [opens the drapes] Hey, Ross! What are you doin'?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, Joey. You wanna open the window?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh, yeah, I do.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: What are you doing out there?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I am, uh, I am...

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Oh, you must be freezing. You know what you need? How about a nice steaming cup of hot Mockolate?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Rach, come on, open up. Rach, come on, come on, Rach. You got to give me another chance.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: That's what I said.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Look, maybe we should go?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: No, you guys, you really don't have to go, we're done talking.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Rach, come on, look, I know how you must feel.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: [near tears] No, you don't, Ross. Imagine the worst things you think about yourself. Now, how would you feel if the one person that you trusted the most in the world not only thinks them too, but actually uses them as reasons not to be with you.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, but, but I wanna be with you in spite of all those things.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh, well, that's, that's mighty big of you, Ross. [to the others] I said don't go!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You know what? You know what? If, things were the other way around, there's nothing you could put on a list that would ever make me not want to be with you.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Well, then, I guess that's the difference between us. See, I'd never make a list.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [quietly] I never know how long you're supposed to wait in this type of a situation before you can talk again, you know? [Ross stares blankly at him] Maybe a little longer.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Rachel is sitting on the couch, eating candy. It is raining out. Ross climbs up the fire escape and is knocking on the window.

scenes.location: Mr. Ratstatter's office. Monica is there

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Now, in some of these recipes, the quantities may seem just a little unusual, uh, like these coconut mockolate holiday nut bars. I've indicated four cups of coconut, and four cups of crushed nut, and only, uh, one tablespoon of mockolate.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Doesn't matter.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Our FDA approval didn't come through. Something about laboratory rats.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Oh, gosh, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Yeah, well, anyhoo, here is your check. [hands it to her] Thank you for all the trouble you went through. Um, listen, you didn't eat a lot of it while you were cooking, did you?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Well, uh, I ate some.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Oh, some, that's fine. Some is fine. Some is not a lot. So, it doesn't burn when you pee, does it?

scenes.scene_description: Mr. Ratstatter's office. Monica is there.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Rachel are there
scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Rachel are there.

scenes.location: phone rings

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: [to Monica] Is that him again? Tell him I'd come to the phone, but my ankles are weighin' me down.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: [to Ross] Listen, I... I don't think this is the best time.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Look, can, can you do something for me?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Sure, what? Ok, ok. [hangs up the phone] [to Rachel] Music?

lines.speaker: RADIO
lines.text: The next one's dedicated to Rachel from Ross. Rachel, he wants you to know he's deeply sorry for what he did and he hopes you can find it in your heart to forgive him.

lines.speaker: RADIO
lines.text: Uh, we've just gotten a call from Rachel, and she told us what Ross did. It's pretty appalling, and Ross, if you're listening, I don't wanna play your song anymore. Why don't we devote our time to a couple that stands a chance? Avery, Michelle's sorry she hit you with her car and she hopes you two will work it out.

scenes.scene_description: phone rings

scenes.location: Mr. Ratstatter's office. Monica is there

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Hi, thanks for coming in again.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Oh, not at all. I have no morals and I need the cash.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: It's like I'm lookin' in a mirror. Anyway, they're called "fishtachios". They taste exactly like pistachios, but they're made primarily of reconstituted fish bits. Here, try one. You're not allergic to anything, are you?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Cat hair.

lines.speaker: RTST
lines.text: Oh, sorry.

scenes.scene_description: Mr. Ratstatter's office. Monica is there.

title: The One With Phoebe's Dad

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ooh, ooh, ooh, ooh, ooh. Ugly Naked Guy is decorating his tree. Oh my God, you should see the size of his Christmas Balls. [Chandler and Joey enter.]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, how much did you guys tip the super this year?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, we were gonna give fifty, but if you guys gave more, we don't wanna look bad.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, actually this year we just made him homemade cookies.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: And twenty-five it is.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: You gave him cookies?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Money is so impersonal. Cookies says someone really cares. . . Alright, we're broke, but cookies do say that.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I can see that. A plate of brownies once told me a limerick.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Phoebs, let me ask you something, were, were these, uh, funny brownies?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Not especially. But you know what, I think they had pot in them.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: So you guys, who else did you tip with cookies?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Uhh, the mailman, the super. [There's a bang at the door.]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, and the newspaper delivery guy. [Joey opens the door and picks up the remnants of the newspaper.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uhhh, I don't think you're gonna like this. [Joey shows them the torn-up newspaper.]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ooh, goooosh, ooh, these are cookies smashed in the sports section.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh look, and he did my crossword puzzle.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, but not very well, unless 14-across, 'Gershwin musical' actually is bitemebitemebitemebiteme. OPENING TITLES

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica, Rachel, Ross, and Phoebe are there. Phoebe is looking out the window.

scenes.location: Central Perk . Monica

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I can't believe it's Christmas already. Ya know, I mean, one day your eatin' turkey, the next thing ya know, your lords are a-leapin' and you geese are a-layin'.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Which is why geese are so relaxed this time of year. [Ross enters with several bags from shopping.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey guys.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER, MONICA, and JOEY
lines.text: Hey. [Ross approaches Rachel at counter.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey Rach. I, uh, got you a little present. [Rachel is not impressed]. . I'll open it. It's a Slinky! Remember, huh. [sings] Walks down stairs, alone or in pairs, everyone knows it's . . . just a big spring. Alright, you still mad at me becuase of the whole. . .

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Horrible and degrading list of reasons not to be with me?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: How 'bout from now on we just call it the 'unfortunate incident'? [Rachel walks off] Hey Gunther, you got stairs in your place?

lines.speaker: GUNTHER
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Here, go nuts. [gives him the Slinky and goes and sits with others at the couches]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey guys.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER, MONICA, and JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: What's in the bag?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Um, just some presents.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: C'mon show us what you bought. . . You know you want to.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [childishly] OK. OK, this is a picture frame from Ben to my parents, huh.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Cute.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I got some, uh, hers and hers towels for Susan and Carol. And, uh, I got this blouse for mom. [Ross holds up the blouse. It is extremely tacky, with sewn-on medals hanging off of it.]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ross, that is gorgeous!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Look at these authentic fake medals. I tell ya, mom's gonna be voted best dressed at the make-believe military academy. [Phoebe enters.]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: GANG
lines.text: Hey. Hi Phoebe.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Happy Christmas Eve Eve. [sees Ross's picture frame] Oh my God, where did you get this?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh, Macy's, third floor, home furnishings.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: This is my father, this is a picture of my dad.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Nah, Phoebs, that's the guy that comes in the frame.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No it isn't, this is my dad, alright, I'll show you.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Phoebe, I thought your dad was in prison.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No, that's my stepdad. My real dad's the one that ran out on us before I was born.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: How have you never been on Oprah?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [showing her pictures] OK, look, see, this is him. My mother gave me this picture before she died, same guy.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Honey, uh, this is a picture of the frame guy posing in front of a bright blue screen with a collie.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: It's not a blue screen... it's just, maybe it was just really clear that day. OK, I have to talk to my grandmother. [turns to leave]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, wait a minute honey.

lines.speaker: GANG
lines.text: Phoebs. [Phoebe leaves]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: So anyway, I'm trying to get my boss's ex-wife to sleep with me. . .

lines.speaker: GANG
lines.text: Joey!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh, but when Phoebe has a problem, everyone's all ears!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk . Monica, Chandler, and Joey are seated at couches. Rachel is working behind the counter.

scenes.location: Phoebe's grandmother's place. Phoebe's grandmother is sitting at the table

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Esther Livingston. [scratches out name] Gone. [Phoebe enters.]

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Hi, Phoe.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hi Gram. Whatcha doin'?

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Oh, just updating the phonebook.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Um, gram, um, can I see the pictures of my dad again?

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: [nervously] Oh. Oh, sure, sure, uh, uh, how come?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Just, you know, to see... um.

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Oh, sure, yeah. [gets a box with the pictures] This is the one of you father in a meadow, and, uh, helping a little boy fly a kite, and here he is at a graduation. . . another graduation. . . another graduation.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, is this really my father?

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Is it really your fa--I can't... well of course it is.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, I smell smoke. Maybe that's 'cause someone's pants are on fire.

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Look, I. . .

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ya know, in all the years that we have been grandmother and granddaughter, you have never lied to me.

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Alright, that is not your father, that's just a picture of a guy in a frame.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh God.

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: It was your mother's idea. Ya know, she didn't want you to know your real father because it hurt her so much when he left, and, I didn't want to go along with it, but, well then she died and, and it was harder to argue with her. Not impossible, but harder.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Alright, so, what, he's not a famous tree surgeon? And then, I guess, OK, he doesn't live in a hut in Burma where there's no phones?

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Last I heard, he was a pharmacist somewhere upstate.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, that makes no sense. Why would the villagers worship a pharmacist?

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Honey.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [realizes] Oh.

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Anyway, that's all I know. That, and this. [pulls apart a frame and pulls a picture out] This is the real him.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe's grandmother's place. Phoebe's grandmother is sitting at the table, reading the obituaries, and crossing out names in the phonebook.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Rachel

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ya know I remember my father, all dressed up in the red suit, the big black boots, and the patent leather belt, sneakin around downstairs. He didn't want anybody to see him but he'd be drunk so he'd stumble, crash into something and wake everybody up.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, that doesn't sound like a very merry Christmas.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Who said anything about Christmas? [Monica and Ross enter.]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, anyone hear from Phoebe yet?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No, nothin'.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I hope she's OK.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, I know exactly what she's goin' through.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: How do you know exactly what she's going through?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: She told us.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So whaddya got there Monica?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Just some stuff for the party.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, what're you guys doin' here, aren't you supposed to be Christmas shopping?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You guys haven't gotten your presents yet? Tomorrow's Christmas Eve, what're ya gonna do?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Don't you have to be Claymation to say stuff like that?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, by the way Mon, I don't think the mailman liked your cookies. Here are the ornaments your mom sent. [hands her a smashed box]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, maybe the mailman liked the cookies, we just didn't give him enough.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Monica, pigeons learn faster that you. [Ross approaches Rachel, away from everyone else.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, Rach, you know what? I think, I think I know what'll make you feel better. How 'bout you make a list about me.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Wha... forget it Ross, no, I am not gonna stand here and make a list of. . .

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: C'mon Rachel.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: OK, you're whiney, you are, you're obsessive, you are insecure, you're, you're gutless, you know, you don't ever, you don't just sort of seize the day, you know. You like me for what, a year, you didn't do anything about it. And, uh, oh, you wear too much of that gel in your hair.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: See there, you uhh, alright, ya, you did what I said.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah, and you know what? You're right, I do feel better, thank you Ross. [she walks off and Ross puts his hand to his hair]

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Rachel, Chandler, and Joey are decorating the Christmas tree.

scenes.location: Back at Phoebe's. She is on the phone

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, um, in Albany, can I have the number of Frank Buffay. . . OK, um, in Ithica. . . alright, um, Saratoga. . . Oneonta. Alright, you know what, you shouldn't call youself information. [hangs up] [Phoebe's grandmother enters]

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hello grandma, if that is in fact your real name.

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: C'mon now Phoe, don't still be mad at me. How's it going?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Well, not so good. Upstate's pretty big, he's pretty small, you do the math.

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Well, I think you're better off without him. Oh honey, I know he's your daddy but, but to me he's still the irresponsible creep who knocked up your mom and stole her Gremlin.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No I just, just wanted to know who he was, ya know.

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: I know. OK, I wasn't completely honest with you when I told you that, uh, I didn't know exactly where he lived.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Whattaya mean?

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: He lives at 74 Laurel Drive in Middletown. If you hit the Dairy Queen, you've gone too far. You can take my cab.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Wow. Thank you.

lines.speaker: GRANDMOTHER
lines.text: Now, remember, nobody else drives that cab.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Uh-huh, got it. Ooh, I'm gonna see my dad. Wish me luck, Grandpa! [blows a kiss to a picture of Einstein]

scenes.scene_description: Back at Phoebe's. She is on the phone

scenes.location: Chandler is standing on a street corner waiting for Phoebe in the cab. Joey walks up

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Phoebe here with the cab yet?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, she, she brought the invisible cab. . . hop in.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well she better get here soon, the outlet stores close at 7.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey, don't worry. I figure it'll be 2 hours to Phoebe's dad's house, they'll meet, they'll chat, they'll swap life stories, we'll still have plenty of time. [Phoebe drives up in the cab]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, here she comes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey. [Phoebe runs over the curb. Joey gets in the back seat, Chandler in the front]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Can you believe this. In, like, two hours I'm gonna have a dad. Eeeshk.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Eeeshk.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, big stuff.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, let's go.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Alright, here, you have to hold this. [hands Chandler a piece of paper]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: OK. [reads paper] Brake left, gas right?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Uh-huh, yeah, that's my cheat sheet.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [grabs for seat belt] Where's my seat belt?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, no no, that side doesn't have one, the paramedics had to cut through it. [Chandler jumps out of the car]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [Chandler gets in the back seat] Hey!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey. [Phoebe takes off, Joey and Chandler are thrown back in the seat]

scenes.scene_description: Chandler is standing on a street corner waiting for Phoebe in the cab. Joey walks up.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica is preparing for the party with Ross questioning her

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: C'mon, just tell me, please, please.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: For the sixteenth time, no... I do not think you're obsessive. [Rachel enters from her room]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, gosh, it's hot in here.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Rach, get the heat. [Rachel holds up her hand with wet fingernail polish] Ross, could you turn the heat down please?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Sure. By the way, there's a difference between being obsessive and. . .

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ross, the heat!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Fine, OK! Heat, heat, heat, and I'm the obsessive one. [goes to the radiator and starts turning the knob] OK, this way is on, so this is. . . [breaks off the knob] off.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Did you just break the radiator?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, no, I was turnin' the knob and, and. . . here it is.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well put it back.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: It uhh, it won't go back.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I'll call the super.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Here, let me try.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, oh that's right, I forgot about your ability to fuse metal.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey, it's Funny's cousin, Not Funny.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: [on phone] Hi, Mr. Treeger. Hi, it's Rachel Green from upstairs. Yes, somebody , uh, broke our knob on the radiator and it's really hot in here. Yes, it's, it's hot enough to bake cookies. Well, do you think we could have a new one by 6? Wha t, no, no, Tuesday, we can't wait until Tuesday, we're having a party tonight.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: OK, tip the man.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No, if he doesn't like our cookies, too bad, I am not gonna be blackmailed. Look if worse comes to worse, it gets a little warm, we'll call it a theme party.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, here's a theme: Come on in, live like bacon.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica is preparing for the party with Ross questioning her.

scenes.location: Outside Phoebe's dad's house. The cab pulls up

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ooh, this is it, 74. [screeches to a halt, Joey and Chandler are thrown into the plexiglass wall in the cab]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, so that's what this is for.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Wow, this is it, I'm gonna meet my dad. This is like the biggest thing ever, huh.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Sure is.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, here I go. I'm goin' in.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Good luck Phoebs.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, here I go. . . here I go. . . I'm goin'. [she just sits in the cab]

scenes.scene_description: Outside Phoebe's dad's house. The cab pulls up.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. They are having their party. All the guests are stripped down because of the heat

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: [answers door] Hi, welcome to our tropical Christmas party. You can put your coats and sweaters and pants and shirts in the bedroom.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [sitting at table talking to a girl] It's hard to tell because I'm sweating, but I use exactly what the gel bottle says, an amount about the size of a pea. How, how can that be too much?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [carrying an ice cube tray] Ice, ice, ice squares anyone? Take a napkin. Alright.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Monica, Monica, your guest are turning into jerky, OK.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Really? I'm perfectly comfortable. [one of the guest opens the refrigerator] Hey, hey, hey, get in line buddy, I was next. [she opens the refrigerator and leans into it]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: [answering the door] Mr. Treeger.

lines.speaker: MR. TREEGER
lines.text: Uhh, you said there was a party.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, yeah, well hey, welcome to our sauna.

lines.speaker: MR. TREEGER
lines.text: Ahh, is it hot? My body always stays cool, probably 'cause I have so much skin. Hey, cheese! [Ross is speaking to Monica and Rachel about tipping the super.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Alright, alright, here's the chance. Monica give him cash, Rachel give him your earrings. Something, now, anything.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No, I will not cave.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah, I'm with Mon.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Alright, alright, you know how you say I never seize the day? Well, alright, even though he's your super, I'm seizing. [approaches Mr. Treeger] Mr. Treeger, here is 50 bucks, merry Christmas. [Gives him the cash.]

lines.speaker: MR. TREEGER
lines.text: Oh wow, I didn't get you anything. Here's five back.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No no, no, that, that's your Christmas tip, alright. Oh, hey, do you think there's a chance you could fix that radiator now?

lines.speaker: MR. TREEGER
lines.text: No can do, like I told the girl, I can't get a new knob until Thursday.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ross.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [to Ross] Looks like he's playin' baseball.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You mean hardball?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Whatever.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What'cha gonna' do?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Excuse me, I'm seizing. Mr. Treeger, here's another 50, happy Hanukkah. Will uh, will this help with the knob getting?

lines.speaker: MR. TREEGER
lines.text: No, the place is not open 'till Tuesday. Am I not saying it right.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So, wait, you really did like my cookies?

lines.speaker: MR. TREEGER
lines.text: Oh, yeah, they were so personal, really showed you cared.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Nice seizing. . . gel boy.

lines.speaker: MR. TREEGER
lines.text: [to Rachel who is standing under mistletoe] So, uh, is this, uh, mistletoe?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Huh-huh, no act--no, uhh, that, that is basil.

lines.speaker: MR. TREEGER
lines.text: Ahh, if it was mistletoe, I was gonna kiss ya.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Huh-hoo, yeah, no, it's still basil.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. They are having their party. All the guests are stripped down because of the heat.

scenes.location: Outside Phoebe's dad's house. Phoebe is running back to the cab

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: How far'd ya get?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Mailbox.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Alright, we're gettin' closer.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Phoebs, what's goin' on?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No, it's just like, ya know, it's a whole mess of stuff, ya know. It's like, yesterday, ya know, my dad was this, like, famous Burma tree surgeon guy and, ya know, now he's a, a pharmacist guy and. . .

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, maybe he's, maybe he's this really cool pharmacist guy.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, maybe, yeah. You know, and, and I'll knock on the door and, and he'll hug me and I'll have a dad. Ya know and I'll, I'll go to his pharmacy and everyone will be really nice to me 'cause, you know, I'm Franks daughter.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, so why not go knock?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Well, 'cause, I mean, what if, what if he's not this great dad guy? I mean, what if, what if he's just still the dirtbag who ran out on my mom and us? You know what? I've already lost a fake dad this week and I don't think I'm ready to lose a real one.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Phoebs, that's OK. You took a big step today.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, and someday when you're ready, you'll make it past the hedges.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, and when you do, he'll be lucky to have you.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: You guys. I'm sorry about your shopping.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, that's OK, we'll figure something out.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uh, listen Phoebs, I know you're not goin' in there but do you think it'd be alright if I went in and used his bathroom? Oh, that's fine, never mind. Cool, snow, kinda like a blank canvas.

scenes.scene_description: Outside Phoebe's dad's house. Phoebe is running back to the cab.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Ross

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ho, ho, ho, holy crap is it hot in here!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Really, hey, you mind if I turn the heat down?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey, we could have used that kind of thinkin' earlier.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, Phoebs, how'd it go.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, I couldn't go in.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Honey, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Are you OK?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, no it's OK 'cause, I mean, I know he's there, so, that's enough for now.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey, guys, it's after midnight, merry Christmas everyone. [Ross and Phoebe hug, Monica and Rachel hug, Chandler is left standing]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, Monica, the knob was broken so I just turned it off from underneath, I hope that's alright. CLOSING TITLES

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Ross, Monica, and Rachel are sitting around after the party. Chandler, Joey, and Phoebe enter.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Joey and Chandler are giving out their Christmas presents out of a cardboard box from a case of motor oil

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Rach, these are for you.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Wiper blades. I don't even have a car.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, but with this new car smell, you'll think you do.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: OK, Phoebs, your turn.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ahh, toilet seat covers! Is that what you were doing while I was getting gas?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: You guuuyys.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: And for Ross, Mr. Sweet-tooth.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You got me a cola drink?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: And, a lemon lime.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well this, this is too much, I feel like I should get you another sweater.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: And last but not least. [Chandler and Joey give Monica a pack of condoms.]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: They're ribbed for your pleasure. [Ross and Monica trade their gifts.]

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Joey and Chandler are giving out their Christmas presents out of a cardboard box from a case of motor oil.

title: The One With Russ

scenes.location: Unknown

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Joey, would you slow down? They're not gonna be sold out of papers at one o'clock in the morning.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I'm excited! I've never gotten reviewed before.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: You were so amazing as the king. I was really impressed, I was.

lines.speaker: PHOE
