lines.text: Although, you know what? You might want to consider wearing underwear next time. Yeah, cause when you sat down on your throne you could kind of see your... royal subject.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Here it is, here it is. [reading from newspaper] The only thing worse than the mindless, adolescent direction was Joseph Tribbiani's disturbingly unskilled portrayal of the king.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: OK, look, that is one guy's opinion, alright. Phoebs, read yours.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: OK. [reading] The only thing worse than the mindless, adolescent direction...

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Does anyone have one from a different paper? Ross, read yours.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I don't want to.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Joey, honey, they don't know what they're talking about.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Maybe they do. I've been doin' this ten years and I haven't gotten anywhere. There's gotta be a reason.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh c'mon. Maybe you're just, uhhh... paying your dues.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, no, no, it.. it's too hard. It's not worth it. I quit.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait one minute. Wait a minute. I believe this will change your mind. [Reads from paper] In a mediocre play, Joseph Tribbiana was able to achieve brilliant new levels of... continued on page 153...[turns it] sucking.

scenes.location: Chandler

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: When I was little, I wanted to be a veteranarian, but then I found out you had to put your hands into cows and stuff.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [sullenly] Hiiiiii.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Are... are you OK?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, just a tough day at work. A stegosaurus fell over and trapped a kid. Whoa, whoa, I know this jacket, this is, th--Fun Bobby's jacket! Where is he, what. He, he's here, isn't he?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Maybe.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Don't toy with me.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Geller!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, Fun Bobby!

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Hey. Whoa, hey, you've been working out, huh?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Not at all! I love this guy. Hey, I was so psyched to hear you're back with my sister!

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: You and me both.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Hey, so what'd I miss, what'd I miss, c'mon?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Oh, we were just trying to make Joey feel better.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Hey, do you need me to pick you up?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, I'm alright man. Really.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: No, I'm picking you up.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey no, seriously, I don't need you to pick me... [Fun Bobby picks Joey up off the ground, bounces him. Joey laughs.] Alright! It still works.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: OK, now before I go, does anybody else need to be picked up? [everyone raises their hands] I'm still gonna go.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: OK, I'll see you later babe.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Uh, public display of affection coming up. You can avert your eyes. [kisses Monica]

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: See ya. [exits]

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Bye! See you later!

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Fun Bobby is so great.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Oh, isn't he? Oh, you know, I really think this time it may work with him. I mean, he just makes me feel so good and I've been feeling so lousy this last couple of months, no job, no boyfriend. Well, at least my cup is half full.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Half full of looooovvvvve .

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: And for our two-week anniversary, he's gonna take me to his cousin's cabin for the weekend.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Cabin of loooooovvvvve .

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: We went through a lot of wine tonight, you guys. [walks over to table, holding five empty wine bottles]

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Really? I only had two glasses.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I just had a glass.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Two.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: I had one glass.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: I had about a mugful in this lovely 'I got boned at the Museum of Natural History' mug.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: OK, so that's... that's what, two bottles? And yet somehow we went through five?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oooooh.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [realizing what everyone else did a minute ago] Ooooooh.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: So what. So he drank a lot tonight.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah but, you know, now that I think about it, I don't think I've ever seen Fun Bobby without a... a drink in his hand.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Yeah. Oh, OOOH, yeah, you know, did you notice how he always starts his stories with, um, OK, 'I was soooo wasted,' or, 'Oh, we were soooo bombed,' or, ummm, ooh, ooh, 'So I wake up, and I'm in this dumpster in Connecticut.'

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Monica, have you ever been with him when he wasn't drinking?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Well, we just happen to go to alot of places where you might drink. I mean, how do you go to a wine tasting without having a drink? Or... or to a club, or to the... zoo.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler, Phoebe, Rachel, Monica comforting Joey at Monica and Rachel's apartment.

scenes.location: Monica

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Rach, does this have nonfat milk?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Ehhhummmm, I don't know, why don't you taste it.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: [takes a sip] Mmmm, no.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh well, too late, sorry, you already had some.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: [pulls out a flask] Whattaya say we make these, uh, coffees Irish?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Um, cake.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Yeah, we're gonna... we're gonna get some cake. [Phoebe and Rachel go to counter.]

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: You know what? It seems like you've been making an awful lot of stuff Irish lately.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Well, I would make them Belgian, but the waffles are hard to get into that flask.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Bobby.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Yeah, OK.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Look, maybe this is none of my business, or maybe it is, I don't know... but, uh, I'm kind of worried about you.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: OK, look, this isn't the first time somebody's said something to me about this, but, I don't know... I always made excuses about it, like... uhhh... 'I'm just a social drinker,' or, 'C'mon, it's Flag Day.'

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: So, what are you saying now?

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: I guess I'm saying, I'll try and quit. I kinda like that you worry about me. [they hug]

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: [comes back to couch, with cake] Sooo, what's goin' on, huh?

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: I am gonna try and quit drinking.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: [sad] Ooohh, why?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Guess who's back in show business.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Ohh, ohh, Lorne Green?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: No, no, Phoebs. You know why? Cause he's dead.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Oh, no.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: OK, I guess this is gonna seem kinda bittersweet now, but... Joey, that's who.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, my agent just called me with an audition for Days of Our Lives !

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Oh, well, we have to celebrate. You know what we should do? We should do, like, a soap opera theme.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Hey, yeah... we could all sleep together and then one of us could get amnesia.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Hey Rach, what time do you get off? We're all gonna do something tonight.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Ummmm.... well, actually I'm already done, but I...I kinda got plans.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: [gasps] You have other friends?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Yeah... I, uhh... I have a... I have a date.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: With a man?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: What? What is so strange about me having a date?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What about Ross? I mean, are you still mad at him cause he made that list about you?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Noooo, no, I'm not mad at him. I'm.. I'm not really anything at him anymore.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: I don't know. Whatever I was feeling, I'm... not.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: But you guys came so close.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh, I know, I'm sorry you guys. You're just gonna have to get used to the fact that I will not be dating Ross.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Here he is. Hi. Guys, this is Russ.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: [sounding like Ross] Hhhhiiiii.

scenes.scene_description: Monica, Fun Bobby, and Phoebe sitting in Central Perk Rachel is serving them. She brings a mug to Monica.

scenes.location: Estelle Leonard Talent Agency . Estelle (ESTL

lines.speaker: ESTL
lines.text: Stop saying you're not talented, you're very talented. It's just with the bird dead and all, there's very little act left. Oh, honey, give me a break, will ya? [a knock on the door] Oooh, ooh, I'll talk to you later.

lines.speaker: ESTL
lines.text: Well, there's my favorite client. So tell me darling, how was the audition?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, I think it went pretty well. I.. I got a callback for Thursday.

lines.speaker: ESTL
lines.text: Joey, have you ever seen me ecstatic?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: ESTL
lines.text: Well, here it is. [She almost smiles.]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: OK, uh.... listen, there's something I want to talk to you about. The network casting lady...

lines.speaker: ESTL
lines.text: Oh, isn't Lori a doll?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh yeah, yeah, she's great, but... I kinda got the feeling that she was sort of... coming on to me. And I definitely would get the part if I would've... you know... if I would have sent the Little General in.

lines.speaker: ESTL
lines.text: Oh, I see. Well, I'm just gonna put in a call here and we'll find out what's goin' on and straighten it out. [picks up the phone] Yeah, hi, Lori please. [pause] Hi darling. So how 'bout Joey Tribbiani for the part of the cab driver, isn't he terrific? [pause] Uh-huuuuh. [pause] Uh-huuuuh. OK, doll. Talk to you later. [hangs up] [to Joey] Yeah, you're gonna have to sleep with her.

scenes.scene_description: Estelle Leonard Talent Agency . Estelle (ESTL) is speaking on the phone.

scenes.location: Central Perk . Monica and Rachel at counter

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: It's Fun Bobby.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: What, isn't he sober?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Oh, he's sober alright. Just turns out that Fun Bobby was fun for a reason.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Ohhh, OK.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Alright, here you go, sweetie. [hands Fun Bobby his coffee]

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Thanks. You wanna hear something funny?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Oh God, yes!

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: There are no hardware stores open past midnight in the Village.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: That is funny.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: I needed to buy a hammer the other night, and I'm out walkin' around the neighborhood but apparently there are no hardware stores open past midnight in the Village.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Ahhh, hey honey? Don't you have to be at your interview now?

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Oh yeah. See you guys. [leaves]

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Bye..... ridiculously dull Bobby.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Oh.... my... God.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: It's not that bad.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Not that bad? Did you hear the hammer story?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: OK, OK, don't get all squinky.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Maybe it was just the kind of story where you have to be there.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: But I'm gonna be there... for the rest of my life. I mean, I can't break up with him. I'm the one who made him quit drinking. He's dull because of me.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Alright, don't say that. He's probably always been dull. You just, you know, set it free.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: [turning around] Hey Ross.... bahhhh!

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Hi Russ, I've just got two more tables to clean and then we'll go, OK?

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: OK, I'll just sit here and... uh... chat with your, uh.... friend-type....people.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Rachel? Um, hi.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: OK, so, you know what you're doing, right?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Uhh.... waitressing?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Well, yeah, but... no. I mean, umm... doesn't.... doesn't Russ just remind you of someone?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: [looks at him] Huh, Bob Saget?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: [looks at Russ] Oh, yeah! No, no, no, no, oh, oh.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Oh, my, oh!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What? What's wrong?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: She's just upset because she, uh, she buttered a spider into her toast this morning.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: [to Phoebe] Listen, Phoebs, this is gonna be OK. [introducing Russ and Ross] Ross, Russ. Russ, Ross.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Are you a, uh, friend of Rachel's?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yes, yes I am. Are you a, uh, a friend of Rachel's?

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Actually, I'm a... kind of a.... you know, a... date-type... thing... of Rachel's.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: A date.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Yeah, I'm her date.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, oh, you're... uh... you're, oh you're the date.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: You know, this is actually good, because if we ever lose Ross, we have a spare.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Oh, you are the, uh... paleontologist.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yes, yes I am. And you are a....

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Periodontist.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: See? They're as different as night and... later that night.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, I am going to, uh... get a beverage. It was nice, nice... uh... meeting you.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Ditto.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I, uh, well... I... I met Russ.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, I didn't know we were, uh, seeing other people.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Well, we're not seeing each other, so....

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, uh, for your information, there's a woman at the museum, who's curator of moths and other... uh... winged things... who's, uh, let it be known that she is drawn to me much like a... well, you know. But so far I've been keeping her at bay, but, uh, if this is the deal...

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Well, yeah, this is the deal.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: OK, well, um, have a nice evening.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Um, Russ, you ready?

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [upset] She's dating. She's dating.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Yes, yes, but did you see who she was dating?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Do you not see it?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: See what? I don't know what she sees in... innn that goober. And it takes him, what? Like... like... I don't know, uhh... uhhh, hello.... a... week, to get out a sentence.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Yeah, it's annoying, isn't it?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk . Monica and Rachel at counter, Phoebe, Chandler, and Fun Bobby at the couch.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Joey is making marinara sauce and filling every container in sight. Chandler enters

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, so I'm guessing you didn't get the part, or... uh, Italy called and said it was hungry.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, the part's mine if I want it.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, if I'm willing to sleep with the casting lady.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: [not knowing how to react] Oh my... God?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ten years I've been waiting for a break like this Chandler, ten years! I mean, Days of Our Lives . That's actually on television.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: So, what're you gonna do?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, I guess I could sleep with her... I mean, how could I do that?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Well, I... I've got a pop-up book that told me everything I need to know.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I've never slept with someone for a part.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Well is she... [reaches into the cookie jar for a cookie, takes his hand out, covered with pasta sauce]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: It's alright. Is she good-looking?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, she's totally good looking. I mean, if I met her in a bar, or something, I'd be buying her breakfast. [pause] You know, after having slept with her.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Y'know, maybe this isn't such a big deal. Y'know, I mean, the way that I see it is you get a great job and you get to have sex. Y'know, I mean, throw in a tree and a fat guy and you've got Christmas.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I just... I just don't think that I want it that way though, y'know? I mean, let's say I do make it, alright? I'm always gonna look back and wonder if it was because of my talent or because of.. y'know, the Little General.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Didn't you used to call it the Little Major?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, but after Denise DeMarco, I had to promote it.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Joey is making marinara sauce and filling every container in sight. Chandler enters.

scenes.location: A restaurant. Fun Bobby and Monica are ordering

lines.speaker: WAITER
lines.text: Can I get you something from the bar?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Yes, I would like something. [looks at Fun Bobby, changes her mind] No, no thank you.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: If... if you want to drink, it's OK with me, I've got to get used to it.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: No, no really. I.. I wouldn't feel right about it. [to waiter] Just some water.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: So the light went out in my refrigerator...

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: [grabs waiter as he's leaving] I'd like a scotch on the rocks with a twist.

scenes.scene_description: A restaurant. Fun Bobby and Monica are ordering.

scenes.location: Central Perk . Chandler is sitting on the couch between Russ and Ross

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Hey, we're having some fun now, huh, Ross? Wanna do another one, huh Russ? OK... eleven letters, atomic element number 101... ends in ium .

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Dysprosium.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [condescendingly] Dysprosium? Try mendelevium.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: And weenie number two has it. Unless, of course, nine-down, Knights in White Satin was sung by the Doody Blues.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: You don't see it? You actually don't see it?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: OK honey, you're dating Ross.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: No, Phoebs. I'm dating Russ .

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Russ is Ross. Russ... Ross!

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Steve... sleeve!

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: OK, noone is named Sleeve.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Phoebe, what the hell are you talking about? Other than their names being similar, I'm sorry, I do not see what you're seeing.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [to Russ] For your information, it's a card sharp, not a card shark.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: You could not be more wrong. You could try... but you would not be successful.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: OK, I'm gonna get some more coffee before the pinching and eye-poking begins.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: I know what your problem is.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh you do, do you?

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Um-hum, you're jealous.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Of... of what?

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: You're jealous because I'm a real doctor.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, you're a doctor of gums . That's the smallest body part you can major in. It's like day one, floss. Day two, here's your diploma.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Hey, you listen.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, no, let me finish.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: No, let me finish.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, you let me fini...

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Ewww, ewww, ewww, ewww! [turns away]

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk . Chandler is sitting on the couch between Russ and Ross, doing a crossword puzzle.

scenes.location: Ross

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Did Joey say what he was gonna go when he left?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: No, I don't even think he knew. Hey, would you sleep with somebody to get a great job?

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: I don't know. Who would I have to sleep with?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Me.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Why would I have to sleep with you?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: It's my game. You want the job or not?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Morning.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Where ya goin'?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Bobby and I are going away for the weekend, remember?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ooooohhhh.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: What's with all the bottles of liquor?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What's going on, is... uh, Bobby drinking again?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Oh no no, this is not for him, this is for me. That way he's still sober but I find his stories about shoelaces much more amusing.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: Oh God, even his knock is boring.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Hi. I'll be ready in just a second.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Uh, can I talk to you a minute?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: This is really hard for me to say.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Oh God, you fell off the wagon.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Oh, no, no, it's about you.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: What about me?

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: I think you may have a drinking problem.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: What these? [holding up liquor bottles] Oh, these are, um, for.. cuts and scrapes.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Look, I am just not strong enough to be in a codependent relationship right now, OK?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Oh... shoot.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: Well, anyway, I hope we can be friends.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Take care.

lines.speaker: FBOB
lines.text: You too.

lines.speaker: RACH
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: Well we... we kinda broke up.

lines.speaker: GANG
lines.text: Awwwwwwww.

lines.speaker: MNCA
lines.text: [holding bottles] Does anybody want these?

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: I'll take one. Sometimes I like to hold stuff like this and pretend I'm a giant.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: GANG
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: How'd the callback go?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: It was unbelievable! I walked in there and she was all over me.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: So what'd you do?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, I couldn't do it. I told her I didn't want to get the part that way.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Good for you.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: But wait, wait, wait. Then, after I left her office, she caught up with me at the elevator and offered me an even bigger part.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: So... and?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Soooooo... you are now looking at Dr. Drake Ramore, neurosurgeon, recurring in at least four episodes!

lines.speaker: GANG
lines.text: Allright!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Alright... I've got to go shower. [leaves]

scenes.scene_description: Ross, Phoebe, Rachel, and Chandler at Monica and Rachel's apartment.

scenes.location: Central Perk . Russ enters. Chandler and Phoebe are sitting on the couch

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Oh, hey.

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: I guess you guys heard, Rachel dumped me.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Yeah, I'm sorry man.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Oh, all she said was that I remind her too much of somebody. You have any idea who she's talking about?

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Oh I do, it's.... it's Bob Saget. She hates him.

lines.speaker: RUSS
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: JULIE
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: CHAN
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: PHOE
lines.text: Hey, Julie! Hey, how are you doing?

lines.speaker: JULIE
lines.text: Um, oh, I don't know. I mean, it's definitely weird not being with Ross, but I guess I'm doing OK. Actually I've got some of his stuff that he, um....

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk . Russ enters. Chandler and Phoebe are sitting on the couch.

title: The One With the Lesbian Wedding

scenes.location: Ross's

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok. Here's his diaper bag, and his uh, Mr. Winky, and uh...oh, him. Hi!

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: So how did everything go?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, great. Great. There was a projectile, uh, throwing up incident, but he started it.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Well, we've gotta go.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: [clears her throat]

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Oh, right. Um, I've got some news. It's about us.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, you and me?

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Uh, no, Susan and me.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: The other us.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: We're uh, we're getting married.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: As in, "I now pronounce you wife and wife" married?

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Anyway, we'd like you to come, but we totally understand if you don't want to.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Why wouldn't I want to come? I had fun at the first wedding.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Look I just thought that...

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No no no, I mean, hey, why shouldn't I be happy for you? What would it say about me if I couldn't revel in your joy? I'm revelling baby, believe me!

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: Is your finger caught in that chair?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Mmm hmmm.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Want us to go?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh-huh.

scenes.scene_description: Ross's. Carol and Susan are picking Ben up

scenes.location: Rachel and Monica's

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: This is so cool. You're actually gonna be on television.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: It really hit me last night. I'm gonna be on Days of our Lives. And then I started thinkin' about all of u, and how these are the days of our lives..

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Yes! Carol and Susan's caterer had a mountain bike accident this weekend, and she's in a full body cast.

lines.speaker: ROSS, CHANDLER & JOEY
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: They want me to do it, which is really cool, seeing as I've never catered before, and I really need the money, and this isn't a problem for you, is it?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Would it matter?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, you are so great! [kisses him] Thank you!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Are you really not going?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I am really not going. I don't get it. They already live together, why do they need to get married?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: They love each other, and they wanna celebrate that love with the people that are close with them.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: If you wanna call that a reason.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [singing to the tune of Mister Rogers' Neighborhood] Who's the bitterest man in the living room, the bitterest man in the living room? Hi, neighbor.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ross, I thought you were over this.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Look, that has nothing to do with this, ok? She's my ex-wife. If she were marrying a guy, none of you'd expect me to be there.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, if she were marrying a guy, she'd be like the worst lesbian ever.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: [entering hurriedly] Did I miss it? Did I miss it?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, I'm on right after this guy shoots himself.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Whoa, she's pretty.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, and she's really nice too. She taught me all about how to work the cameras, and smell-the-fart acting.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I'm sorry, what?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: It's like, you got so many lines to learn so fast, that sometimes you need a minute to remember your next one. So while you're thinkin' of it, you take this big pause where you look all intense, you know, like this.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, ok.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: There's my scene, there's my scene. [Joey on tv] "Mrs. Wallace, I'm Dr. Drake Ramoray, your sister's neurosurgeon.

lines.speaker: MRS. WALLACE
lines.text: Is she gonna be all right?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I'm afraid the situation is much worse than we expected. Your sister is suffering from a..subcranial hematoma. Perhaps we can discuss this over coffee.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Nice!

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: That's great!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Excellent!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: For a minute there I thought you were actually tryin' to smell something.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That is so good! Do it again!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: All right, all right. "Damnit Braverman, it's right there on the chart!"

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: That's great. All right, I gotta get to work, I got a big dinosaur bone to inspect.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No no, that's me.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, yeah.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, hello.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, thanks. I couldn't uh...

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Is everything ok?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Um, no, huh-uh. One of my clients died on the massage table today.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh my god.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: That's a little more relaxed than you want them to get.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, um, she was 82 years old. Her name was um, Mrs. Adelman.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, honey.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, it's just so strange. I mean, she probably woke up today and thought, "ok, I'll have some breakfast, and then I'll take a little walk, and then I'll have my massage." Little did she know God was thinking, "Ok, but that's it." Oh, but the weirdest thing was, ok, I was cleansing her aura when she died, and when the spirit left her body, I don't think it went very far.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I think it went into me.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: God, this is so hard. I can't decide between lamb or duck.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, of course, lambs are scarier. Otherwise the movie would've been called Silence of the Ducks.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, who ordered what?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, I believe I had the half-drunk cappuccino with the lipstick on the rim.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yes, and this with the cigarette butt in it, is that decaf?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh god.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I can't believe you're so uptight about your mom comin'.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I know, but it's just it's the first time, and I just don't want her to think that because I didn't marry Barry, that my life is total crap, you know?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [Mrs. Adelman's voice] Talk about crap. Try listening to Stella Niedman tell the story of her and Rod Steiger for the hundredth time.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uh, Pheebs, how long do you think this lady'll be with us?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I don't know. I mean, she obviously has some kind of unfinished business. [Mrs. Adelman's voice] Sit up!

lines.speaker: MRS. GREEN
lines.text: [entering] There she is.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Mom!

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: Sweetie! So this is where you work? Oh, it's wonderful! Is it a living room? Is it a restaurant? Who can tell? But I guess that's the fun.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Pretty much.

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: Monica! You look gorgeous! Last time I saw you, it was eat or be eaten.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: This is Joey, and Phoebe, and this is Chandler, and you remember Ross.

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: Oh hello, Ross.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi, Mrs. Green. [He gets up to shake her hand, but she ignores him.]

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: So, what do you think of my daughter in the apron with the big job?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh Mom!

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: If you didn't pour the coffee, no one would have anything to drink.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Believe me, sometimes that happens.

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: This is just so exciting. You know, I never worked. I went straight from my father's house to the sorority house to my husband's house. I am just so proud of you.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I know who it is you remind me of. Evelyn Dermer. 'Course, that's before she got the lousy face lift. Now she looks like Soupy Sales.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Pheebs, who's Evelyn Dermer?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I don't know. Who's Soupy Sales?

scenes.scene_description: Rachel and Monica's

scenes.location: Rachel and Monica's

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: Oh my god, there's an unattractive nude man playing the cello.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah, well just be glad he's not playing a smaller instrument.

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: [laughing] You have some life here, sweetie.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I know. And Mom, I realize you and Daddy were upset when I didn't marry Barry and get the big house in the suburbs with all the security and everything, but this is just so much better for me, you know?

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: I do. You didn't love Barry. And I've never seen you this happy. I look at you and I think, oh, this is what I want.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: For...me.

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: Well, not just for you.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, what do you mean?

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: I'm uh, considering leaving your father.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [entering] All right. Tell me if this is too cute. Lesbian wedding, chicken breasts.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh god. I think I'm gonna be sick.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Why? It's not like I'm putting little nipples on them.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: And you had no idea they weren't getting along?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: None.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: They didn't fight a lot?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No! They didn't even talk to each other. God, how was I supposed to know they were having problems?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [Mrs. Adelman's voice] In my day, divorce was not an option.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, look who's up.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I just can't believe this is happening. I mean, when I was little, everybody's parents were getting divorced. I just figured as a grownup I wouldn't have to worry about this.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Is there any chance that you can look at this as flattering? I mean, she's doing it because she wants to be more like you.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, then, you know, couldn't she have just copied my haircut?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You know, it's funny when my parents got divorced, they sent me to this shrink, and she told me that all kids have a tendency to blame themselves. But in your case it's actually kinda true.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: That's him.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Damn. My mail order grandfather hasn't come yet.

lines.speaker: MR A
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yes, hi, Mr. Adelman. Thanks for meeting me.

lines.speaker: MR A
lines.text: Oh, that's all right, although you did cut into my busy day of sitting.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Um, do you wanna sit?

lines.speaker: MR A
lines.text: Oh, no, please, I spent most of mid-morning trying to stand up. Now uh, what can I do for you, my dear?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I don't know how to say this, but I think when your wife's spirit left her body, it um, kind of stuck around in me.

lines.speaker: MR A
lines.text: You're saying, my wife is in you?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah. Ok, you don't have to believe me but um, can you think of any unfinished business she might have had, like any reason she'd be hanging around?

lines.speaker: MR A
lines.text: Well, I don't know what to tell you dear. The only thing I can think of is that she always used to say that before she died, she wanted to see everything.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Everything?

lines.speaker: MR A
lines.text: Everything.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Whoa, that's a lot of stuff.

lines.speaker: MR A
lines.text: Oh, wait, I remember, she also said she wanted to sleep with me one last time.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I'm sorry, there's laughing in my head.

lines.speaker: MR A
lines.text: [to Joey] Worth a shot, huh?

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: Look at this.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: These are from Halloween three years ago.

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: Oh, look, here's Barry. Did he have to come straight from the office?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No, that was his costume. See, he's actually an orthodontist, but he came as a regular dentist.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Um, you guys, you know when I said before, "thank you, but I don't really need your help"?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Actually, what I think you said was, "don't touch that, and get the hell out of my kitchen."

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Really? Weird. Anyway, see, I planned everything really well. I planned and I planned and I planned. It just turns out, I don't think I planned enough time to actually do it.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey, Mon, you want some help?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: If you want.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [enters] Hey. What a day. I took her everywhere. The Museum of Modern Art, Rockefeller Center, Statue of Liberty.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: She's still with you?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah. I guess she hasn't seen everything yet. I'll be right back, she has to go to the bathroom again. [Takes Mrs. Green's chin in her hand and says, in Mrs. Adelman's voice] Oh, such a pretty face.

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: This is so much fun, just the girls. You know what we should do? Does anybody have any marijuana?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: God!

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: All right, look, nobody's smoking pot around all this food.

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: That's fine. I never did it. I just thought I might. So, what's new in sex?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh! What's new in sex?

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: The only man I've ever been with is your father.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I'm dicing, I'm dicing, I don't hear anything.

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: I mean, this is no offense to your dad, sweetie, but I was thinking there might be more.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. You know what? I cannot have this conversation with you. I mean, god, you just come in here, and drop this bomb on me, before you even tell Daddy. What? What do you want? Do you want my blessing?

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You want me to talk you out of it?

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Then what? What do you want?

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: I guess I just figured of all people you would understand this.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Why on earth would I understand this?

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: You didn't marry your Barry. I did.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: All right people, we're in trouble here. We've only got 12 hours and 36 minutes left. Move, move, move!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Monica, I feel like you should have German subtitles.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Joey, speed it up!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I'm sorry, it's the pigs. they're reluctant to get in the blankets!

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Monica, how did this happen? I thought you had this all planned out.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Do you want me to cry? Is that what you want? Do you wanna see me cry?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Sir! No sir!

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [to Ross] All right, you!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No. Look, I told you I am not a part of this thing.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: All right, look, Ross. I realize that you have issues with Carol and Susan, and I feel for you, I do. But if you don't help me cook, I'm gonna take a bunch of those little hot dogs, and I'm gonna create a new appetizer called "pigs in Ross". All right, ball the melon.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey! How come I'm stuck dicing, when he gets to ball the melon.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: How's it going?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: It's goin' great. Right on schedule. Got my little happy helpers. [everyone groans]

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Fine, whatever.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Nothing. Ok, everything. I think we're calling off the wedding.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You're still gonna pay me, right? Or something a little less selfish.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Carol, what's the matter? What happened?

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: My parents called this afternoon to say they weren't coming.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh my god.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: I mean, I knew they were having trouble with this whole thing, but they're my parents. They're supposed to give me away and everything.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: It's ok. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: And then Susan and I got in this big fight because I said maybe we should call off the wedding, and she said we weren't doing it for them, we were doing it for us, and if I couldn't see that, then maybe we should call off the wedding. I don't know what to do.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I uh can't believe I'm gonna say this, but I think Susan's right.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Look, do you love her? And you don't have to be too emphatic about this.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Of course I do.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well then that's it. And if George and Adelaide can't accept that, then the hell with them. Look, if my parents didn't want me to marry you, no way that would have stopped me. Look, this is your wedding. Do it.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: You're right. Of course you're right.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So we're back on?

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: We're back on.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You heard the woman. Peel, chop, devil! I can't believe I lost 2 minutes.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel and Monica's

scenes.location: the wedding

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: It just seems so futile, you know ? All these women, and nothing. I feel like Superman without my powers, you know? I have the cape, and yet I cannot fly.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well now you understand how I feel every single day, ok? The world is my lesbian wedding.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [Mrs. Adelman's voice] Butterscotch? No one? All right, you'll be sorry later.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Any time. [He doesn't want to let her go]

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Ross. [He lets her go]

lines.speaker: MINISTER
lines.text: You know, nothing makes God happier than when two people, any two people, come together in love. Friends, family, we're gathered here today to join Carol and Susan in holy matrimony.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [Mrs. Adelman's voice] Oh my god. Now I've seen everything! [Phoebe's voice] Whoa, she's gone. She's gone. She's gone! Go ahead, get married. Go, go.

scenes.scene_description: the wedding

scenes.location: the reception

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Would you look at them?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, can't help but.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [to a wedding guest] How's that pig-in-the-blanket workin' out for you? [the guy nods] I wrapped those bad boys.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I miss Rose.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, yeah?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I know it's kind of weird, but I mean, she was a big part of my life there, you know, and now I just feel kind of alone.

lines.speaker: WOMAN
lines.text: You know, I uh, I couldn't help but overhear what you just said, and I think it's time for you to forget about Rose, move on with your life...how 'bout we go get you a drink?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, that's so nice.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [to an attractive woman] I shouldn't even bother coming up with a line, right? [The woman walks away]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey, Mom? Having fun?

lines.speaker: MRS GREEN
lines.text: Oh, am I! I just danced with a wonderfully large woman. And three other girls made eyes at me over the buffet. Oh, I'm not saying it's something I wanna pursue, but it's nice to know I have options.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: There's more alcohol, right?

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: How you doin'?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: You did a good thing today.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: You wanna dance?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, that's fine.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: Come on. I'll let you lead.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [to the woman who just rejected him] All right look. Penis schmenis. We're all people. [She walks away again.]

scenes.scene_description: the reception, Monica and Ross watch Carol and Susan getting their picture taken.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, which one of us do you think is gonna be the first one to get married?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, Mon, I was married.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, me, too, technically.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I had a wedding.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: All right, just trying to start an interesting discussion.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I got one. Which one of us do you think will be the last to get married? [They all look at Chandler]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Isn't Ben in this?

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Oh, yeah!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's

title: The One After the Superbowl

scenes.location: In a TV commercial that the gang is watching at Monica and Rachel's

lines.speaker: COMMERCIAL VOICEOVER
lines.text: Can't get the monkey off your back? Then put it in your mouth... [A monkey jumps on the desk and hands the guy a beer. He opens the beer and is suddenly on the beach, in a hammock, with beautiful women all around.]

lines.speaker: COMMERCIAL VOICEOVER
lines.text: ...With MonkeyShine Beer. [MonkeyShine theme] MonkeyShine Beer, 'cause it's a jungle out there.] [Camera pans back from the TV to show the gang watching.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That commercial always makes me so sad.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, but then the guy opens his beer and those girls run at him, so, everything seems to work out OK.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I meant because the monkey in it reminds me of Marcel.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I can see that, 'cause they both have those big brown eyes and, ya know, the little pouty chin.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: And the fact that they're both monkeys.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Sometimes I wonder if I did the right thing, ya know, giving him away.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, Ross, you had to, I mean, he was humping everything in sight. I mean, I have a Malibu Barbi that will no longer be wearing white to her wedding.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Remember when sometimes he'd borrow your hat, and, and when you got it back there'd be little monkey raisins in it.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, well sure, when he did it, it was funny. When I did it to my boss's hat. . . all of the sudden I have this big attitude problem. OPENING TITLES

scenes.scene_description: In a TV commercial that the gang is watching at Monica and Rachel's.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Chandler

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, hey, check it out, guess what I got.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Rhythm?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, my first fan mail.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Alright!

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [reading] 'Dear Dr. Remore, know that I love you and would do anything to have you.' Gosh. 'Your not-so-secretive admirer, Erica Ford.' Ooh wait, 'PS enclosed please find 14 of my eyelashes.'

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ya know, in crazy world, that means you're married.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: This wasn't addressed to Days of Our Lives , this is, this came to your apartment. There's no stamp on it, this woman was in our building.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh my god, I got my very own stalker. [Ross enters with a suitcase]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey guys.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ooh, where are you off to, Travelin' Jake?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, there's this, uh, paleontology conference in L.A. so I figured I'd go and then drive down to the zoo and surprise Marcel.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You know I think he will be surprised, 'till he realizes he's a monkey, and uh, you know, isn't capable of that emotion. [Rob enters]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, Phoebe, that really cute guy is here again.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, oh, OK, so everyone, pretend like I'm telling you a story, OK. And, and it's really funny. So everyone just laugh, now. [Everyone laughs]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I know, I know. [to Rob] Hello.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: Hi. I'm Rob Dohnen.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hi Rob Dohnen.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: I don't know anything about music, but I think you're really, really great.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, wow.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: Anyway, I schedule performers for the childrens libraries around the city and I was just thinking, have you ever thought about playing your songs for kids?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, I would love to have kids. . . you're, you're the, you're, me play the songs that I will write for them.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Chandler, Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe are at the couch.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler and Joey are standing around in the kitchen

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, whaddya wanna do for dinner?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well we could just stay in and cook for ourselves. [both laugh hysterically] [door buzzer goes off]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: It's Erica.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ah, the stalker.

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Never mind, it's open.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [Joey grabs a frying pan] Yes, hitting her with a frying pan's a good idea. We might wanna have a backup plan, though, just in case she isn't a cartoon.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Let's get out of here. [They run out and knock on Monica and Rachel's door]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: The one time they're not home.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: OK, we'll just leave, and when we pass her on the stairs, she won't know it's me 'cause we've never met.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: That's how radio stars escape stalkers.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: She's comin'. [Both run back in their apartment. There's a knock at the door.]

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: It's me.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uhh, this is it, this is how we're gonna die. Ready?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait. [Opens the top of the dish soap he's holding] [Joey opens the door and sees Erica . Joey gets a huge smile and Chandler squeezed the dish soap in the air.]

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Erica.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler and Joey are standing around in the kitchen.

scenes.location: San Diego Zoo. Ross is at the Monkey cages

lines.speaker: LIPSON
lines.text: Hi, Dean Lipson, zoo administrator. I was told you had a question.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, I uh, I can't seem to find the monkey I donated last year. He's a capuchan, answers to the name Marcel.

lines.speaker: LIPSON
lines.text: Ahh, I'm afraid I have some bad news. Marcel has passed on.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh my God, what happened?

lines.speaker: LIPSON
lines.text: Well he got sick, and then he got sicker, and then he got a little better but then he died.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I can't believe this.

lines.speaker: LIPSON
lines.text: I'm sorry Mr. Geller. But ya know, there's an old saying, 'Sometimes monkeys die.' It's not a great saying but it certainly is fitting today.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, ya know, someone should have called me.

lines.speaker: LIPSON
lines.text: I'm sorry. Look, I know this can't bring him back but here, it's just a gesture.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Zoo dollars?

lines.speaker: LIPSON
lines.text: Yes, and come see the bird show at 4. The macaws wear hats. Well it's a lot cuter if your monkey hasn't just died.

scenes.scene_description: San Diego Zoo. Ross is at the Monkey cages.

scenes.location: Library. Phoebe is getting ready to sing for the kids. Chandler

lines.speaker: KIDS
lines.text: Ooohhh.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I can't believe Joey's having lunch with his stalker. What i-, what is she like.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, you remember Cathy Bates in Misery ?

lines.speaker: RACHEL AND MONICA
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, she looks the exact opposite of that.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: And she's not crazy?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh no no no, she's a total wack job. Yeah, she thinks that Joey is actually Dr. Drake Remore.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ah, and I mean, he's going out with her? He can not persue this.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey, just because this woman thinks she can actually see Joey through the magical box in her living room doesn't mean she's not a person. I mean, does she not deserve happiness, does she not deserve love? What're you lookin' at me for? He's the one who wants to boff the maniac.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: You OK?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No, uh-uh, I'm just, I'm nervous. So, you know what, maybe if I just, if I picture them all in their underwear.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: That's not a good idea, that's kinda the reason the last guy got fired.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I'm just, I'm, I'm, I'm used to playing for grown-ups. Ya know, they just, grown-ups drink their coffee and do their grown-up thing, ya know, and kids listen. This is a huge responsibility. What? Are you gonna kiss me?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I was thinkin' about it.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK. [they kiss] OK, alrighty, let's play some tunes. Hi everybody, I'm Phoebe

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Hi Phoebe.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, um, I'm gonna play, um, some songs about grandparents, OK. [singing] Now, grandma's a person who everyone likes, she bought you a train and a bright, shiny bike. But lately she hasn't been coming to dinner, And last time you saw her she looked so much thinner. Now, your mom and your dad said she moved to Peru, but the truth is she died and some day you will too. La-la-la la la-la-la la la-la-la la...

scenes.scene_description: Library. Phoebe is getting ready to sing for the kids. Chandler, Monica, and Rachel are there.

scenes.location: A nice restraunt. Joey and Erica are dining

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Oh, Drake, isn't it amazing?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah it is. . . what?

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Well, here we sit, devil may care, just a little while ago you were reattaching someone's spinal cord.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, that was a tricky one. In reality, that operation takes like, over 10 hours, but they only showed it for 2 minites.

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Who's they?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No one.

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Oh Drake, you are so talented, let me see those hands. Oh these hands, these beautiful hands, oh I could just eat them. . . but I won't.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Good, otherwise my watch would fall off. [laughs hysterically]

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: No, seriously. These hands. These miracle, magical, life-giving hands. Oh, just to be near them, touch them, maybe even lick one?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Alright, just one. [she licks his hands rather emphatically] Wow, you're good at that. [Some guy at another table starts choking]

lines.speaker: WAITER
lines.text: Oh my God! Someone, he's choking. Is anyone here a doctor?

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Well, yes, yes, the best doctor in all of Salem, Dr. Drake Remore.

scenes.scene_description: A nice restraunt. Joey and Erica are dining.

scenes.location: San Diege Zoo. Ross is still at the monkey cages. A janitor is sweeping

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: Meet me in the nocturnal house in 15 minutes.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uhh, hey look, I don't really enjoy being with other men that way. But, um, zoo dollars?

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: It's about your monkey. It's alive.

scenes.scene_description: San Diege Zoo. Ross is still at the monkey cages. A janitor is sweeping.

scenes.location: Restraunt. Joey and Erica are still there

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: I don't understand, why didn't you help that man?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uhh, cause, uhh, I'm a neurosurgeon and that was clearly a case of, uh, uh, foodal chokage. Alright, look, I got to tell you something.

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: No, no no no, you don't have to tell me anything. You don't have to explain yourself to me. Ooh, who am I to question the great Dr. Drake Remore?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: But that's what...

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: I should just be happy to be near you.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey I- [she cuts him off with a kiss]

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Hey what?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: That's it, just hey. Like at the end of a dance, HEY! [she starts nibbling his hand] Hey. He-hey.

scenes.scene_description: Restraunt. Joey and Erica are still there.

scenes.location: Library. Phoebe is singing

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [singing] There'll be times when you get older when you'll want to sleep with people just to make them like you. . . But don't. Cause that's another thing that you don't wanna do, everybody That's another thing that you don't wanna do.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Excellent!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Very informative!

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Not at all inappropriate!

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Thank you for coming everybody. There're cookies in the back.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: That was great, the kids loved you.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yay, I rock.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: And you know why? Because you told the truth, and nobody ever tells kids the truth.You were incredible.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: But.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: How did you know there was a but?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I sense these things. It was either but or butter.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: The thing is, I think some of the parents, they were kinda hopin' that you'd play more songs about like, barnyard animals.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I can do that.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: Because that would be fantastic. What? You wanna kiss me?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Thinkin' about it.

scenes.scene_description: Library. Phoebe is singing.

scenes.location: San Diego Zoo. Ross and the janitor are in the nocturnal house

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: Ahh, the bat. Ambassador of darkness, flitting out of his cave like a winged messenger, sightless spectre of the macabe.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Buddy, my monkey?

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: Oh, yeah, right. There was a break-in, few months back, inside job. Your monkey was taken.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh my God. But the zoo told me that my monkey was dead.

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: The zoo! Do you believe everything the zoo tells ya?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That, that's the only thing the zoo's ever told me.

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: Of course they're gonna say he's dead. They don't want the bad publicity. It's all a great big cover-up. Do you have any idea how high up this thing goes?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That guy Lipson?

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: Lipson knows. Do you have any idea who else knows?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, I, I only know Lipson.

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: Hmm, Lipson knows huh? Ahh, hello Mr. Opossum, enigma of the trees, upside-down denizen of the night, taunting gravity with...

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Buddy, my monkey, my monkey.

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: Word on the street - well, when I say street, I mean those little pretend streets they have here at the zoo.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: Your monkey found a new career, in the entertainment field. That's all I know.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: This is unbelievable.

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: So, what is this information worth to you, my friend?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Are you trying to get me to bribe you?

lines.speaker: JANITOR
lines.text: Maybe.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: But you already told me everything.

scenes.scene_description: San Diego Zoo. Ross and the janitor are in the nocturnal house.

scenes.location: Library. Ross show up with a MonkeyShine Beer poster

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Check it out, he actually is the MonkeyShine monkey.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, so what're you gonna do?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, I guess I'm gonna call the beer company and try to find out where he is.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: That's what I did when I lost my Clydesdales.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, hi again.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Hi Phoebe.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Today we're gonna start with some songs about barnyard animals. [singing] Oh, the cow in the meadow goes moo, Oh, the cow in the meadow goes moo. Then the farmer hits him on the head and grinds him up, And that's how we get hamburgers. Nooowww, chickens!

scenes.scene_description: Library. Ross show up with a MonkeyShine Beer poster.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Monica

lines.speaker: TV DOCTOR
lines.text: You're the only one who can save her Drake.

lines.speaker: JOEY ON TV
lines.text: Damnit, I'm a doctor, I'm not God.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, there goes my whole belief system. [knock at the door]

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: It's Erica.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh my God, quick turn off the TV.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No no no, wait, I wanna see what happens.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uh, I get Leslie out of the coma and then we make out.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well how can that be, you were just kissing Sabrina?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Rachel, it's a world where Joey is a neuro-surgeon.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey Erica, c'mon in.

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: How did you get here so fast, I just saw you in Salem?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Right, they uh, they choppered me in. What's up?

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Ohh, and I see you're having a little party too. Is she here, huh, huh?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Sabrina. I know about you two. I saw you today kissing in the doctor's lounge.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: It's not what you think, that was...

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: You told me I was the only one. [throws a glass of water in his face]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Alright look, that's it. I don't think we should see each other anymore, alright. Look, I know I should have told you this a long time ago but I am not Drake Remore, OK. I'm not even a doctor, I'm an actor. I just pretend to be a doctor.

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Oh my God. Do the people at the hospital know about this?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Somebody wanna help me out here?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, I know, I know. [Turns on the TV. Joey in on it.]

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: How, how can you be here and there.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: 'Cause it's a television show.

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Drake, what're you getting at?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I'm not Drake.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That's right, he's not Drake, he's Hans Remore, Drake's evil twin.

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Is this true?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yes, yes it is true. And I know this because, because he pretended to be Drake to, to sleep with me. [throws water in his face]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: And then he told me he would run away with me, and he didn't. [throws water in his face]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: And you left the toilet seat up, you bastard. [throws water in his face]

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Is all this true?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yes, I'm afraid it is. You deserve much better than me Erica. You deserve to be with the real Drake, he's the one you fell in love with. Go to Salem, find him, he's the guy for you.

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: Oh Hans. [They kiss]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hans...Hans...Yo evil twin.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Right. Goodbye Erica, good luck in Salem. Take care

lines.speaker: ERICA
lines.text: I'll never forget you Hans. [Joey shuts the door in her face]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: OK, alright, the people who threw the water.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Monica, Rachel, and the guys are watching Days of Our Lives .

scenes.location: Central Perk. Phoebe and Rob are sitting on the couch

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Fired! Why?

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: The library board has had a lot of complaints from parents about some of the stuff in your songs.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I can't believe it. Did you tell your board about how kids want to hear the truth?

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: Maybe if you just played some regular kiddie songs.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No. What do you, what do you want me to be, like some stupid, big, like, purple dinosaur?

lines.speaker: ROB
lines.text: I'm not saying you have to be Barney.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Who's Barney.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Phoebe and Rob are sitting on the couch.

scenes.location: Central Perk. The whole gang is there

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, I tracked down Marcel and get this, he's healthy, he's happy, and he's right here in New York filming Outbreak II - The Virus Takes Manhattan .

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You're kidding.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: This is amazing.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I finally get a part on TV and the monkey's makin' movies.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, Rachel, I'm ready.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: OK. [little kid enters]

lines.speaker: KID
lines.text: Excuse me. Is this where the singing lady is that tells the truth?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Um, yeah I guess that's me.

lines.speaker: KID
lines.text: [shouting out the door] She's here. [a rush of kids enter]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [singing] Sometimes men love women, sometimes men love men, and then there are bisexuals, though some just say they're kidding themselves. la la-la-la la-la-la-la-la-la-la...

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. The whole gang is there.

scenes.location: City street. The whole gang is walking up to the movie set

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: This is so exciting, I haven't seen my monkey in almost a year.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: What, you never look down in the shower? Oh please. I'm not allowed to make one joke in the monkey-is-penis genre?

lines.speaker: SECURITY GUARD
lines.text: C'mon people, back up please, back up, c'mon, c'mon, c'mon, c'mon.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh, excuse me, uh, where can we find the monkey?

lines.speaker: SECURITY GUARD
lines.text: I'm sorry guys, closed set.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh, I'm sorry, you don't understand, I'm, I'm, I'm a friend of his. We uh, we used to live together.

lines.speaker: SECURITY GUARD
lines.text: Yeah, and I have a time share in the Pocanos with Flipper.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ross, there he is.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, hey buddy, Marcel. Marcel. [Marcel doesn't react so Ross starts singing] In the jungle, the mighty jungle the lion sleeps tonight. [no reaction from Marcel, Monica and Joey urge him on] In the jungle, the mighty jungle the lion sleeps tonight. [Marcel looks over and everyone joins in] a-weema-way, a-weema-way..... [Marcel runs over and hops up on Ross's shoulder]

scenes.scene_description: City street. The whole gang is walking up to the movie set.

scenes.location: The next time at the movie set

lines.speaker: SECURITY GUARD
lines.text: Uh, excuse me folks this is a uhh...

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Closed set. We know but we're friends with the monkey. [guard lets them in]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Good morning. Hey pal, look who I brought. It's your old friend Harry Elefante. [Marcel grabs the elephant doll and throws it to the ground]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Woah, dude, burn.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I don't get it, he seemed so happy to see me yesterday.

lines.speaker: TRAINER
lines.text: Hey don't take it personal, he's under a lot of pressure, ya know, starring in a movie and all.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Now just how big of a star is Marcel?

lines.speaker: TRAINER
lines.text: In human terms, I'd say Cybill Shepard.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Woah.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [to guys wering yellow isolation suits] So, are you guys in the movie, or are you just really paranoid.

lines.speaker: DIRECTOR'S ASSISTANT
lines.text: Hey Sal, Jerry wants to know if the monkey's ready for the subway set?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uh, excuse me. Jerry is the director, which one's he?

lines.speaker: DIRECTOR'S ASSISTANT
lines.text: The one in the director's chair.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Gotcha. Phoebs, walk with me.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK. Um, how come I'm walking with you?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, we're, we're just goin' over here so that we can get away from the horrible flesh eating virus, for the love of God woman, listen to me. Is he lookin', is he lookin'?

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: We've got a problem.

lines.speaker: DIRECTOR'S ASSISTANT
lines.text: Tell me.

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: I can't do Chris's makeup. She refuses to acknowledge that she has a moustasche.

lines.speaker: DIRECTOR'S ASSISTANT
lines.text: Is it bad?

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: It looks like one of her eyebrows fell down. Now unless someone convinces her to let me bleach it, Jean-Claude Van Damme is gonna be making out with Gabe Kaplan.

lines.speaker: DIRECTOR'S ASSISTANT
lines.text: I'll talk to her.

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: I hate actors.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [to an extra in fatigues] Nice camoflauge man, for a minute there I almost didn't see ya.

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: Excuse me.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ahhhh.

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: Uh, is your name Chandler?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Uh, yes, yes it is.

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: Chandler Bing?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Do you know me or are you just really good at this game?

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: I'm Susie Moss. Fourth grade, glasses, I used to carry around a box of animal crackers like a purse.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Susie Moss, right, yeah, wow, you look. . . great job growing up.

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: It's nice to see you're not still wearing that denim cap with all the little mirrors on it.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, right, well yeah, I graduated fourth grade and realized I wasn't a pimp.

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: Remember the class play? You, you pulled up my skirt and the entire auditorium saw my underpants.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yes, back then I, uh, used humor as a defense mechanism. Thank God I don't do that anymore. [cut to Monica and Rachel walking through the set]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What what what what?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Jean-Claude Van Damme. I didn't know he was in this movie, he is so hot.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ya think?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: The muscles from Brussels, wham bam Van Damme, did you see Time Cop ?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No, was he any good in it?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Rachel, he like, totally changed time.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Wow, so why don't you go talk to him?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, yeah.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What, so you go over there, you tell him you think he's cute, what's the worst that could happen?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: He could hear me.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: OK, I'm doin' it for ya.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh Rachel don't, don't you dare, don't, don't . Tell him I cook.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Excuse me. Hi.

lines.speaker: VAN DAMME
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Um, this is gonna sound kinda goofy but uhhm, my friend over there, who cooks by the way, um, she thinks you're cute.

lines.speaker: VAN DAMME
lines.text: You don't think I'm cute?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I, I don't know, um, do you think you're cute? OK, we're kinda gettin' off the track here. Um, I was supposed to come here and tell you my friend thinks you're cute. So what should I tell her?

lines.speaker: VAN DAMME
lines.text: You can tell her I think her friend is cute. [back to Chandler and Susie]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No, Stephen Hurs was the kid who would eat anything for money, David Stein was the guy who had no elbows.

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: OK, well then who was the kid that got caught masturbating?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: OK that's not what he was doing. Alright, he was looking for his bus money. [a voice in the background calls for makeup]

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: Oh that's me, I gotta go.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh uh, o, OK.

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: Um, so listen, how many times am I gonna have to touch you on the arm before you ask me on a date.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, uh, let's try one more. . . there you go, say Ernie's, 8 o'clock.

lines.speaker: SUSIE
lines.text: I'll be there, and who knows, if things go well, maybe this time I'll get to see your underwear.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No one was around to hear that? [back to Rachel and Monica]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So what'd he say?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Agh, what a jerk. I kept talking about you and he kept asking me out. I mean, naturally, you know, I said no.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, thanks anyway.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: He just kept asking, and asking, and asking, and asking, and asking, and asking.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Rachel if you, if you want to go out with him, you can. Sound like a big jerk to me but if that's what you want to do...

lines.speaker: RACHEL
