scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica answers the door and lets her parents in. They are carrying boxes

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Hi darling.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So, what's this.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Some of your old stuff.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Well sweetie, we have a surprise for you. We're turning your room into a gym.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Wow, that is a surprise. Just one little question, uh, why not Ross's room?

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Gosh, we talked about that but your brother has so many science trophies and plaques and merit badges, well we didn't want to disturb them.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, God forbid. [Rachel enters with a laundry basket]

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Oh, hi Rachel.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Oh, we were so sorry to hear about your parents splitting up, dear.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, well, you know, they're just separated so, you know, never know, we'll see.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Well, I can't say any of us were surprised. Your parents have been unhappy ever since we've known them. Especially after that incident in Hawaii.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What, what incident?

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Uhh, naa, no no no, I, I must be thinking of someone else, uh, maybe me. Don't you have some folding to do? Go fold dear. Fold. You fold. [shuffles her into her room]

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica answers the door and lets her parents in. They are carrying boxes.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Chandler and Phoebe are sitting on couches. A beautiful woman is looking at Chandler

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Do you want a refill?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No, I'm alright, thanks.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK. Ooh, OK, you gotta give me a second, I wanna get this just right. [she sticks out her gut, clears her throat and sniffs her nose and then in her best male voice. . .] Dude, 11 o'clock, totally hot babe checkin' you out. That was really good, I think I'm ready for my penis now.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [walks over to the woman] I know what you're thinking, Dave Thomas, founder of Wendy's.

lines.speaker: GAIL
lines.text: I'm Gail.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Chandler. [waves his arm around, exposing the bracelet]

lines.speaker: GAIL
lines.text: I, I really have to be somewhere but it was nice meeting you.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: What? [realizes it was the bracelet] Oh this is excellent. You know he coulda gotten me a VCR, he coulda gotten me a set of golf clubs, but no, he has to get me the woman repeller, the eyesore from the Liberace house of crap.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: It's not that bad.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, yeah, easy for you to say, you don't have to walk around sporting some reject from the Mr. T collection. [Joey walks in behind Chandler]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Chandler, Chandler.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I pity the fool who puts on my jewelry, I do, I do. I pity the fool that. . . [turns around and sees Joey] Hi. Hey man, we were just doin' some uhh, impressions over here. Do your Marcel Marceau. [Joey turns around and walks out without saying anything] That's actually good.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Chandler and Phoebe are sitting on couches. A beautiful woman is looking at Chandler.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Rachel is talking to a man at the counter. Ross and Phoebe are sitting at the couches

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Would you look at that guy, I mean how long has he been talking to her. It's like, back off buddy she's a waitress not a geisha.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I think she's OK.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [Rachel, laughing, puts a hand on the guy's shoulder] Look at that, look at that, see how she's pushing him away and he won't budge. Alright, I'm gonna do something. [walks up in the middle of their conversation] Excuse me, are you Rachel?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I'm Ross Geller. Wha, I'm, God in your add you said you were pretty but wow.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What are you, what are you doin'?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, oh my God, is this the wrong day? I don't believe it, uh, well, hey, I guess if it works out we'll, we'll have something to tell the grandkids.

lines.speaker: MAN
lines.text: Sure will. I've uh, gotta go. Take care.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: OK, see ya later, nice meeting you. [man leaves] You're welcome.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I was saving you.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Saving, saving, saving me from the pleasant conversation with the interesting man, saving me?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, see from where I was sitting I uh. . .

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: OK, Ross, listen to me, I am not yours to save.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: But, you are.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh, uh, well you're, umm, you're my lobster.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: OK, you know what, are, are you being like, the blind date guy again?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No no, you're uh, you're my lobster. See um, lobsters, uhh, in the tank when, when they're old, uhh, they get with, uhh, they walk around holding the claws. In the tank, ya know, with, with the holding and. . . Uhh, Phoebs you wanna help me out with the, the whole lobster thing?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Do the claws again.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Rach. OK, forget, forget the lobsters OK. We're, let's talk, what about us?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ross, there is no us, OK.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, but. . .

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No, listen to me. I fell for you and I get clobbered. You then fall for me and I again, somehow, get clobbered. I'm tired of being clobbered, ya know, it's, it's just not worth it.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, but, but. . .

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: NO but Ross. We are never gonna happen, OK. Accept that.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: E-except, except that what?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No, no, ACC-cept that.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Rachel is talking to a man at the counter. Ross and Phoebe are sitting at the couches.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler is there. Joey enters

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey man, look it's my best bud. How ya doin? [Joey doesn't respond] Wow, you are really gettin' good at that Marcel Marceau thing. Hey, whaddya say uh, we play some ball, you and me, huh, whaddya say? [Throws a basketball to Joey. Joey doesn't move to catch it and the ball takes out a lamp] OK, that's my bad.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: If you hated the bracelet so much, Chandler, you should have just said so.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, doesn't the fact that I wore the bracelet even though I hated it say something about our friendship and how much it means to me?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, what about the fact that you insulted the bracelet and you made fun of me?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: OK, well that's the part where I'm a wank. But I was hoping we wouldn't focus on that. [Joey goes to his room and shuts the door] Hey, c'mon man, I said I was sorry like a hundred times, I promise I will never take it off my. . . [notices the bracelet is missing from his wrist] wrist. But if, if you want to stay in there and be mad, you know, you just uh, you stay in there. [he starts searching the room, lifting up the couch cushions]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: You know what the. . . [sees Chandler on his knees, holding the couch cushions]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I am here, on my knees, holding up these couch cushions as a symbol of my sorrow and regret, much like they did in biblical times. Though you may haveth anger now. . . [Joey returns to his room]

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler is there. Joey enters.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Mr. and Mrs. Geller are watching tennis on TV

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: You know, that Steffi Graf has quite a tush. I'm just saying, it's right there.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey guys.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Hi, darling. Where's my grandson, you didn't bring him?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, he's at uh, Carol's and Susan's today.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: A woman in my office is a lesiban. I'm just saying.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Oh, Jack look, there's that house paint commercial that cracks you up. [the Gellers return to watching TV and Ross goes over to Monica]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Where have you been?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Emotional hell. So, did they lend you the money yet?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No, but that's probably 'cause I haven't asked them yet.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: C'mon Monica, do it. Hey, you guys, um, Monica has some news.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Um, yeah, so uh, uhh, listen, I'm sorry I didn't tell you this before but umm, I, I'm no longer at my job, I, I had to leave it.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Because they made me.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: You were fired? What're you gonna do?

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Judy, Judy, relax, this is our little harmonica we're talking about. We taught her well. Ten percent of your paycheck, where does it go?

lines.speaker: MONICA and ROSS
lines.text: In the bank.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: There you go. So she dips into her savings, that's what it's there for. She's gonna be fine, and if you need a little extra, you know where to find it. [pulls a quarter from behind her ear]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Anything larger back there?

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Mr. and Mrs. Geller are watching tennis on TV, Monica is sitting at the table

scenes.location: Central Perk. Chandler and Phoebe enter

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I can't believe it.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Would you stop already? Get out of the bitter barn and play in the hay.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, you're right I, I should play in the hay. Forget about the fact that I just dropped 400 dollars to replace a bracelet that I hated to begin with. Bring on the hay. [sits down at the bar]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: [comes up from behind the bar and startles Chandler] Hey. I've got something that's gonna make you happy. Guess what Gunther found? [holds up Chandler's bracelet]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey now you have two. [Chandler looks annoyed] Oh, now you have two.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: What am I gonna do, huh? [Joey walks in behind him]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: How come you have two?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well this one's for you.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Get out.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No, I can't. No no, listen, I, I know how much this means to you and I also know that this is about more than just jewelry, [puts bracelet on Joey] it's about you and me and the fact that we're [reading bracelet] best buds.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Wow, is this friendship? I think so. Check it out, we're bracelet buddies.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: That's what they'll call us.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Chandler and Phoebe enter.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Ross are standing in the kitchen. Ross is filling out a check

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Here you go, you can pay me back whenever you like.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You have dinosaur checks?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, yeah I mean, you get your money and you learn a little something, what's wrong with that?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Nothin', nothin', hey you're a cheapasaurus. I'm kidding, I'm kidding, thank you, I'm very greatful.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [Standing in living room with Chandler and Joey. She pulls a huge bathing suit out of a box] Hey, Mon, what is this?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, um, that was my bathing suit from high school. I was uh, a little bigger then.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, I thought that's what they used to cover Connecticut when it rained.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [pulls out a VCR tape] Hey Monica, what's on this video tape?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey, you got me, put it in.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [Rachel enters] Oh.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Over here Jack. OK. I see, Rachel's coming up the path. Oh doesn't she look pretty. Jack, get this. [Rachel enters with a huge nose]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What is with your nose?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: They had to reduce it because of, of my deviated septum.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: OK, I was wrong, that's what they used to cover Connecticut.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You know what this is, this is us getting ready for the prom.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You know what, you guys, we don't have to watch this.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Oh yeah we do. C'mon.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Get a shot of Monica. Where's Monica.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Over here dad. [he pans over and we see a torso taking up the whole screen]

lines.speaker: MR, GELLER
lines.text: Wait, how do you zoom out? [zooms out and we see an extremely overweight Monica eating a big sandwich] There she is.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Some girl ate Monica.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Shut up, the camera adds ten pounds.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ahh, so how many cameras are actually on you?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, you look so great.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ahh, so do you, beautiful. [they hug]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oops.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Shoot, I think I got mayonaise on you.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, that's OK, it's just the shoulder, it's not my dress.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Everybody smile.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, dad, turn it off.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: It is off.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Dad, it is not. What's with the red light?

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: It's the off light. Right Ross? [pans over to see Ross with an afro and moustache]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Lookin' good Mr. Cotter.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You look pretty tonight.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, thanks. So, uh, what are you gonna do this summer?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, you know, I'm just gonna, I'm gonna hang out, work on my music.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: [the shoulders of her dress keep falling off her shoulders] Is my hook unhooked? These things keep falling down, I can't. . .

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh, hold, let me see, I don't know. So what're you gonna do. . . [doorbell rings]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, the guys are here.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: this summer?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Work on your music? [Ross is sitting on the stairs with a laptop keyboard playing 'Axel-F']

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh my God, look there's Roy Gublik.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ya know, Roy saw Star Wars 317 times. His name was in the paper.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Where's Chip, why isn't he here yet?

lines.speaker: ROY
lines.text: He'll be here OK, take a chill pill. [Chip pins Monica's corsage on, Monica then turns and whispers to Rachel]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I just told Rachel that Roy touched my boob.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I can't go to my own prom without a date, I can't, it's too late.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: If you're not going then I don't want to go either.

lines.speaker: ROY
lines.text: Oh, I'm gonna kick Chip's ass.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: [to Ross on the stairs] I have a wonderful idea. You should take Rachel to the prom.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Doubtful.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Jack, give me that. Talk to your son.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Your mother's right. Take her, you can wear my tux.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Dad, she won't want to go with me.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Of course she would, you're a college man.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Well, c'mon. Don't ya want to find out?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I can't believe I don't get to go to my own prom, this is so harsh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: OK. Hold my board.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Atta boy. [Ross scrambles upstairs to change]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: OK, you guys, ya know, I think we've seen enough, let's turn it off.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: No, no, no.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: OK, fine, well I'm not gonna watch, alright.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: C'mon kid, let's go.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Ahh, are you hadsome.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Let's show 'em.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh, just a sec dad. [to himself] OK, be cool, just be cool. [walks down the stairs and grabs the flowers out of the vase on the endtable] OK dad.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: [going downstairs] Rachel, ready or not, here comes your knight in shining. . . oh no. [Chip has shown up and the four are leaving]

lines.speaker: RACHEL, MONICA, ROY, and CHIP
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Oh, dear. Jack, how do I turn this off?

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Press the button.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Which one? Which button, Jack.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: The button, the button.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I can't believe you did that.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, well. [Rachel, seeing what he did for her, gets up, walks across the room, and kisses Ross]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: See, he's her lobster.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Ross are standing in the kitchen. Ross is filling out a check

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica is watching the rest of the tape

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Dance with him.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Mom, I'm hungry.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Dance with your father.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: I may not know any of your flash dances but I'm no slouch on the dance floor.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright. [the tape cuts to Monica's parents under the covers]

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Oh, Jack.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Oh, Judy. Oh, Judy.

lines.speaker: BOTH
lines.text: Oh, ohhhhh. [Monica is visibly upset]

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica is watching the rest of the tape

title: The One Where Ross and Rachel...You Know

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Joey and Chandler enter with Chandler covering his eyes and Joey leading him

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Alright, no peeking. No peeking, no peeking, no peeking.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Alright, alright, but you better be wearing clothes when I open my eyes.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Alright open your eyes. [opens his eyes to see two black leather recliners and a big screen TV]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Sweet mother of all that is good and pure.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Huh? Days of our Lives picked up my option.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Congratulations!

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I know. chandler : Now we can finally watch Green Acres the way it was meant to be seen.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So uh, which one is mine?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Whichever one you want, man. Whichever one you want. [Chandler starts to sit in one of the chairs] Not that one.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [sits down] Ohh yes.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [sits down] Ohh yeah, that's the stuff.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [reaches for the footrest lever] Do we dare?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: We dare.

lines.speaker: BOTH
lines.text: [both extend the footrests] Aaahhhh. [both recline their chairs] AAAAHHHHHH. OPENING TITLES

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Joey and Chandler enter with Chandler covering his eyes and Joey leading him.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler and Joey are sitting in their recliners watching TV. Monica

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I can't believe two cows made the ultimate sacrifice so you guys could watch TV with your feet up. CHANDLER : Well they were chair-shaped cows. They never would have survived in the wild.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: This screen is amazing, I mean Dick Van Dyke is practically life-size.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Woah!

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Rose Marie really belongs on a smaller screen, doesn't she? [Rachel enters]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi you guys.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey you.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey you. [they stand together in front of the TV.]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER and JOEY
lines.text: Woah, hey, yo. [Rachel and Ross move]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: So, uh, how was your day?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh you know, pretty much the usual, uh, sun shining, birds chirping.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Really? Mine too.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey cool, mine too.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [beeper goes off] Yeah, yeah, yeah. I got to get to the museum. So um, I'll see you tonight.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: OK. [they go to kiss but everyone's watching so Ross just kisses her on the top of her head and leaves]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Bye guys.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [walks up to Rachel in front of the TV] Tonight?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER and JOEY
lines.text: Hey, yo. [they move from out of in front of the TV]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: What's tonight?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: It is our first official date. Our first date.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Uh, hello.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Tonight you're supposed to waitress for me, my catering thing, any of those words trigger anything for you?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: God, oh God Monica, I forgot. This is our first date.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Yes but my mom got me this job.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, I can be a waitress, I can be a waitress.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, thank you, thank you, thank you, thank you. See Phoebe, Phoebe.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Really Phoebs? Because, you know, you'd have to be an actual waitress. This can't be like your 'I can be a bear cub' thing. PHOEBE : I can be a waitress. OK watch this. Um, gimme two number ones, 86 the bacon, one Adam and Eve on a raft and rick'em, la-la-la-la-la, la-la-la-la-la.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler and Joey are sitting in their recliners watching TV. Monica, Ross, and Phoebe are there.

scenes.location: Dr. Burke's apartment. Dr. Burke answers the door for Phoebe and Monica

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: It's James Bond.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Sorry we're late.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Ah, that's OK, come on in. Um, I'm sorry, is Monica Geller coming? I was told she was.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Dr. Burke, it, it's me.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Monica? My God you used to be so. . . I mean you, you, you, you must have lost like. . . You look great.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Thank you. This is my friend Phoebe. She's gonna be helping me tonight.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Hi Phoebe, nice to meet you. [Phoebe just giggles when they shake] So, how ya been?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I've been great, just great. How have you been? [tilting her head]

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Oh, well obviously you know Barbara and I split up, otherwise you wouldn't have done the head tilt.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: The head tilt?

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Yeah, since the divorce, when anybody asks me how I am, it's always with a sympathetic head tilt. [demonstrating] 'How ya doin'? You OK?'

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: No no, it, it's fine, believe me. I do it too. I always answer with the 'I'm OK' head bob. [demonstrates] 'I'm OK.' [tilts head] 'You sure?' [bobs head] 'Yeah, I'm fine.' Hey listen, I've got to set up the music. I got a new CD changer, of course the divorce only left me with 4 CD's to change.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [her and Phoebe tilt their heads] Oh, that's too bad.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: [bobbing his head] I'll survive.

scenes.scene_description: Dr. Burke's apartment. Dr. Burke answers the door for Phoebe and Monica.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. They're still in their chairs watching TV. Chandler is ordering a pizza

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Uh, two larges, extra cheese on both. But listen, don't ring the buzzer for 19, ring 20, Geller-Green, they'll let you in, OK. If you buzz our door, there's no tip for you. OK, thanks. Pizza's on the way. I told you we wouldn't have to get up.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What if we have to pee?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I'll cancel the sodas.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. They're still in their chairs watching TV. Chandler is ordering a pizza.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You've got to get back out there, it's your party.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: But they're so dull, they're all opthamologists.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You're an opthamologist.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Only because my parents wanted me to be, I wanted to be a sherrif.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [entering the kitchen from the party] That's funny, no. Cadillac, cataract, I get it, no I get it, you stay out there.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: See.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright, I'll tell you what. I'll come get you in 5 minutes with some sort of um, kabob emergency.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: OK. You better. Oh God, here we go. Hey wanna see 'em go nuts? Watch this. [grabbing some wine glasses and opening the door to the party] Who needs glasses? [everyone laughs]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: You are so smitten.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I am not.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, you are so much the smitten kitten. You should ask him out.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Dr. Burke? I don't think so. I mean, like, he's a grown up.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: So. You two are totally into each other.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Phoebe, he's a friend of my parents. He's like 20 years older than me.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, so what, you're just never gonna see him again?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Not never. I mean, I'm gonna see him tomorrow at my eye appointment.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Didn't you like, just get your eyes checked?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well yeah, but, you know, uh, 27 is a dangerous eye age.

scenes.scene_description: Dr. Burke's apartment. Monica and Dr. Burke are in the kitchen.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Ross and Rachel are returning from a movie

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: C'mon, I'm not saying it was a bad movie, I'm just saying, you know, it was a little. . . hard to follow.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I told you there was going to be sub-titles.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I know, I just didn't want to wear my glasses on my first date. [They start kissing.]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Monica.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: It would really help when I'm kissing you if you didn't shout out my sister's name.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Honey, I'm just checking.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Monica.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Mon.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Monica.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Mon. [Since they're alone they start kissing and Ross's hands work their way down until they're on Rachel's butt. Rachel starts laughing.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What, what.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I'm sorry. Oh God, I'm sorry, it's just that when you moved your hands down to my butt, it was like woah, Ross's hands are on my butt. Sorry.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: And that's, that's funny why?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well it's not, honey I'm sorry, I guess I'm just nervous. I mean, it's you, ya know, it's us. I mean, we're crossing that line, sort of a big thing.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I, I know it's big, I just didn't know it was uh, ha-ha big.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: OK. [start kissing again and Rachel starts lauging again]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: OK, my hands were no where near your butt.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I know, I know, I know, I know. I was just thinking about when they were there the last time, I'm sorry. I'm sorry, I'm sorry. OK, OK, look, woah, I promise, I'm good, I'm not gonna laugh anymore. OK put your hands back there.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No see now, now I can't because uh, I'm feeling too self conscious.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Just one cheek.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Nuh, uh, the moment's gone.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Alright, just put your hands out and I'll back up into them.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That's romantic.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: C'mon touch it.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, come on squeeze it.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Rub it.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, come on, would you just grab my ass.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Ross and Rachel are returning from a movie.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. They are watching a Miracle Wax info-mercial

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Wow, look at that. The car is on fire, yet somehow it's expensive paint job is protected by the Miracle Wax.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You got a Cheeto on your face man.[Joey removes the Cheeto and eats it] [Ross enters]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER and JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What're you doin' here? Aren't you supposed to be out with Rachel?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That was 14 hours ago.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So how'd it go?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh. Listen, have you ever been uh, you know, foolin' around with a girl and uh, she started laughin'?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, but uh, it was 1982 and my flock of seagulls haircut was tickling her chin.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: She laughed at you?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah. I don't know, I've been wanting this since like ninth grade typing, ya know. And I just want it to be perfect and right and. . . why isn't that laser beam cutting through the paint?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: It's the Miracle Wax.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: It certainly is a miracle. [Rachel enters]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi you guys.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER and JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi. Listen, I was um, thinkin' about. . .

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Listen can you guys uh, speak up, it's harder for us to hear you when you lower your voice. [Rachel and Ross go out in the hall]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: OK, listen, I'm sorry about last night and I really want to make it up to you.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, you, ya know there's no need to make it u. . . how?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, I was thinking maybe a um, a romantic dinner with um, candles and wine and then uh, maybe going back to my place for um, dessert.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Humm, that sounds, I don't, perfect. [there's a loud bang at the door so Ross opens it back up to find a shoe has been thrown at it]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What's this.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Could you get us a couple of beers?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. They are watching a Miracle Wax info-mercial.

scenes.location: Dr. Burke's office. Monica is there for her eye appointment

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: I'm going to look into your eyes now.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Really.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Yeah, that's my job. Alright, look up. . . look down, now open your eyes, now look down. That's right, look into the light. Now look at me. . . OK. Your eyes look good. Those are good eyes.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Good, they feel good, in my head.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: So, it's great to see ya.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You too.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: You too.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: OK, um. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Drops!

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Drops. Here, they're free.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Thanks. So, I guess I better be going.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Oh, OK, yeah. I'll see ya later.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Thanks again. [He kisses her on the cheek, she returns the kiss, then they embrace in a full on kiss]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER and JOEY
lines.text: Hey, woah, hey, woah.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: You know you should go outside and be with the three-dimensional people.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, inside good, outside bad.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: You guys are so pathetic, I, oh, OH, XANADU! OH.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi you guys.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER, JOEY, and PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey.

lines.text: Well we just wanted to stop by and uh, say goodnight.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER, JOEY, and PHOEBE

lines.text: Well, why're you all dressed up?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: You're not the only one who has a date tonight.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What? You have a date? Who with?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No one.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: C'mon, what's his name?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Come on, tell me.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright, but I'm very excited about this OK, so you gotta promise you won't get all big-brothery and judgmental.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, I promise, what.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: It's Richard Burke.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Who's Richard Burke? Doc, Doctor Burke? You have a date with Doctor Burke? Why, why, why should that bother me? I, I love that man, he's like a uh, brother to dad.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well for your information he happens to be one of the brightest, most sophisticated, sexiest men I've ever been with.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Doctor Burke is sexy?

lines.speaker: RACHEL and PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh God, absolutely.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [his beeper goes off] It's the museum again, can I, oh.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ya know, Dr. Burke kissed me once.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: When?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: When I was um, 7, I crashed my bike right out in front of his house and to stop me from crying he kissed me right here. [points to the tip of her nose]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh you are so lucky.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [on the phone] Woah, woah, woah australopithicus isn't supposed to be in that display. No. No. No, n, homo-habilus was erect, australopithicus was never fully erect.

lines.text: Well maybe he was nervous.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh look, I can't believe this. Look, homo-habilus hasn't even learned how to use tools yet and they've got him here wi, with clay pots. Why don't, why don't they just give him a microwave? I'm sorry, I'm sorry this is taking so long, ya know, I, I, it's just it's longer than I expected, we will have dinner.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: It's OK, it's fine.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: KARL! [Ross leaves to find Karl. Rachel takes a peek under the loincloth of one of the display models.]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Wow, is that Michelle?

lines.text: Yep.

lines.text: I've not seen her since high school graduation. Oh my God, that night she got so dru. . . motional.

lines.text: No no. Henry's almost two and he's talking and everyting. Here. You know, the other day he told me he liked me better than his other grandpa. Now in all fairness his other grandpa's a drunk but still. . .

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, you're a grandpa.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Yeah. Are we nuts here?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I don't know, maybe. I mean I'm dating a man who's pool I once peed in.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: I didn't need to know that. I guess 21 years is a lot. I mean, hell, I'm a whole person who can drink older than you.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: So.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So maybe we should just. . .

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, maybe.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Wow, this really sucks.

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: Yeah, it sure does. [they hug and it turns into a passionate kiss]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, we don't really have to decide anything right now, do we?

lines.speaker: DR. BURKE
lines.text: No, no, there's no rush or anything. [knock at the door]

lines.text: Pizza delivery.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, I'm gonna kill those guys.

lines.text: Rach.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: RACHEL

scenes.location: The museum planetarium. Ross and Rachel enter on stage

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What is this? What are we doing?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Shh. Do you want cran-apple or cran-grape?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Grape.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [spreads the pelt on the floor] OK, now, sit. OK. [he starts the music system]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, God. [The stereo system booms out 'Billions of years ago. . .'. Ross gets up and changes it to music.]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ah, so what are we looking at?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well uh, you see that, that little cluster of stars next to the big one? That is Ursa Major.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I've no idea, could be. Listen, I'm sorry I had to work tonight.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh it's OK. You were worth the wait, and I don't just mean tonight. [they kiss]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You're not laughing.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: This time it's not so funny. [They kiss and start undressing. As Rachel tries to pull off Ross's tie she catches it in his mouth. Then they roll across the fur rug.]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ah, oh God. Oh, honey, oh that's OK.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What. Oh no, you just rolled over the juice box.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, thank God.

title: The One Where Joey Moves Out

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Man this is weird. You ever realize Captain Crunch's eyebrows are actually on his hat?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: That's what's weird? Joey, the man's been captain of a cereal for the last 40 years. [Joey finishes his cereal, licks his spoon, and puts it back in the silverware drawer.]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Waaa-aaah.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: The spoon. You licked and-and you put. You licked and you put.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, so.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well don't you see how gross that is? I mean that's like you using my toothbrush. [Joey gets a sheepish look] You used my toothbrush?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, that was only 'cause I used the red one to unclog the drain.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Mine is the red one! Oh God. Can open, worms everywhere.

lines.text: Hey, why can't we use the same toothbrush, but we can use the same soap?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Because soap is soap. It's self-cleaning.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Alright, well next time you take a shower, think about the last thing I wash and the first thing you wash. OPENING TITLES

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: MONICA and PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.text: Ahh.

lines.text: Um, a chip. A tattoo, I'm getting a tattoo.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: A tattoo? Why, why would you want to do that? [to Rachel] Hi.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi. Well hey, you don't - you don't think they're kind of cool?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, sorry I don't. Tell me why would anyone pay someone to scar their body for life? What if it doesn't come out right Phoebe? Then it's like, I don't know, havin' a bad hair cut all the time. Why's everyone staring at me?

lines.text: Ross, come sign this birthday card for dad. Rich is gonna be here any minute.

lines.text: Oooh, Rich is goin' to the party too, huh?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No, I don't even know how serious he is about me. Until I do, I'm not telling them anything.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I don't know, I don't think mom and dad would mind. Remember when you were 9 and Richard was 30, how dad used to say, 'God I hope they get together.'

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Phoebe are sitting at the table, Joey and Chandler enter.

scenes.location: The Gellers' house. Monica

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Alright, shall we?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: OK, wait, wait, wait, wait. You know what? Ross, let's - let's switch places. You get in the middle. No un-, ya know, unless this looks like we're trying to cover something up.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Monica, Monica, you could come in straddling him, they still wouldn't believe it. [opens door] We're here.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Oh hi kids. Hi darling.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Happy birthday dad.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Oh thank you.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi ma.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Happy birthday.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Well, you kids thank Dr. Burke for the ride?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh, actually mom, I think Monica thanked him for the both of us.

scenes.scene_description: The Gellers' house. Monica, Ross, and Richard are arriving to Mr. Gellers birthday party.

scenes.location: The Gellers' kitchen. Monica

lines.speaker: FRIEND
lines.text: Well, you kids take the train in?

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: No, Richard Burke gave them a ride.

lines.speaker: FRIEND
lines.text: Oh. Speaking of whom, I hear he's got some 20-year-old twinkie in the city. [Monica sprays whipped cream all over the place]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Finger cramp. Oh God, sorry. Here, let me get that mom.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Sooo, Richard's shopping in the junior section.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Are we still on that?

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: We just know she's got the IQ of a napkin.

lines.speaker: FRIEND
lines.text: She's probably not even very pretty, just young enough so that everything is still pointing up. [Monica folds her arms over her breasts]

scenes.scene_description: The Gellers' kitchen. Monica, Mrs. Geller and one of Mrs. Geller's friends are preparing the cake.

scenes.location: Joey's co-star's apartment. Chandler and Joey are at the brunch

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Can you believe this place?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I know, this is a great apartment.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ah, I was just in the bathroom, and there's mirrors on both sides of you. So when you're in there it's like you're peein' with the Rockettes.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Wow, there's my fantasy come true. No, seriously.

lines.speaker: JOEY'S CO-STAR
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey! We were just sayin', great apartment man.

lines.speaker: JOEY'S CO-STAR
lines.text: Thanks. You want it?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: JOEY'S CO-STAR
lines.text: Yeah, I'm movin' to a bigger place. You should definitely take this one.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, can you see me in a place like this?

lines.speaker: JOEY'S CO-STAR
lines.text: Why not? You hate park views and high ceilings? C'mon I'll show you the kitchen.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [being left behind] Oh that's all right fellas, I saw a kitchen this morning - on TV. Stop talking. OK.

scenes.scene_description: Joey's co-star's apartment. Chandler and Joey are at the brunch.

scenes.location: Mr. Geller's party. Mr. Geller and a friend are questioning Richard while Ross observes

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: C'mon, tell us.

lines.speaker: FRIEND
lines.text: Yeah, is she really 20.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: I am not telling you guys anything.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: C'mon Rich, it's my birthday, let me live vicariously.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Dad, you really don't want to do that.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Ahh, what's a little mid-life crisis between friends?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Jack, would you let it go?

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Look, I know what you're going through. When I turned 50 I got the Porsche. You... you got your own little speedster.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Guys. Seriously, it is not like that.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Tell you what, maybe one of these weekends you can borrow the car and I cou. . .

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Dad, I beg you not to finish that sentence.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: What? I'm kidding. You know I'd never let him touch the Porsche.

scenes.scene_description: Mr. Geller's party. Mr. Geller and a friend are questioning Richard while Ross observes.

scenes.location: Tattoo parlor. Phoebe and Rachel are deciding on tattoos

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK Rach, which, which lily? This lily or that lily?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well I. . .

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I like this lily. It's more open, ya know, and that's like my mom. She had a more open, giving spirit. Ooh, Foghorn Leghorn, ooh.

lines.speaker: TATTOO ARTIST
lines.text: Alright, blonde girl, you're in room two, not so blonde girl, you're with me.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Here we go.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: [reluctantly] Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: You're not going?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: What? Is it - is this 'cause of what Ross said?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No. Well, yeah, maybe.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I don't believe this. Is this how this relationship's gonna work? Ross equals boss. I mean, c'mon what is this, 1922?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What's 1922?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Just, you know, long time ago. Well, when men used to tell women what to do - a lot. And then there was suffrage, which is a good thing but is sounds horrible. Do you want to get this tattoo?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yes I do, it's just that Ross is. . .

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, hey, HEY. Is your boyfriend the boss of you?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, who is the boss of you?!!

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No. You are the boss of you. Now you march your heinie in there and get that heart tattooed on your hip. GO !!

scenes.scene_description: Tattoo parlor. Phoebe and Rachel are deciding on tattoos.

scenes.location: Mr. Geller's birthday party. Monica is in the bathroom and Richard comes in

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: How ya doin'?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I'm a twinkie.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Really? I'm a hero.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, this is so hard.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Yeah, I know. I hate it too. Look, maybe we should just tell them.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Maybe we should just tell your parents first.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: My parents are dead.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: God, you are so lucky. I mean, I mean. . . you know what I mean.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: I know, I know. Just hang in there, OK. OK, I'll go out first, alright.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: [walks out of the bathroom and runs into Mrs. Geller who is going to the bathroom] Judy, going to the bathroom , good for you.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Thank you Richard, I appreciate the support. [Monica jumps in the shower. Right after Mrs. Geller enters the bathroom, Mr. Geller peeks his head in.]

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Honey. Honey, have you seen my Harmon Kilerbrew bat? Bob doesn't believe I have one.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: I have no idea. Did you know Richard has a twinkie in the city?

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: I know. He's like a new man. It's like a scene from Cocoon .

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: I just never would have pictured Richard with a bimbo.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Apparently, he told Johnny Shapiro that she's quite a girl. In fact, he told Johnny that he thinks he's falling in love with her.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Really.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: I tell you, I've never seen him this happy.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: So Jack, you ever think about trading me in for a younger model?

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Of course not. With you it's like I've got two 25-year-olds.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: [they start kissing] Oh Jack stop.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: C'mon, it's my birthday.

scenes.scene_description: Mr. Geller's birthday party. Monica is in the bathroom and Richard comes in.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler and Joey are returning from their brunch

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Can we drop this? I am not interested in the guy's apartment.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh please, I saw the way you were checking out his mouldings. You want it.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Why would I want another apartment, huh? I've already got an apartment that I love.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well it wouldn't kill you to say it once in a while.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Alright, you want the truth? I'm thinkin' about it.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I'm sorry. I'm 28 years old, I've never lived alone, and I'm finally at a place where I've got enough money that I don't need a roommate anymore.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Woah, woah, woah. I don't need a roommate either, OK? I can afford to live here by myself. Ya know, I may have to bring in somebody once a week to lick the silverware.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What're you gettin' so bent out of shape for, huh? It's not like we agreed to live together forever. We're not Bert and Ernie.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Look, you know what? If this is the way you feel, then maybe you should take it.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well that's how I feel.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well then maybe you should take it.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well then maybe I will.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Fine with me.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Great. Then you'll be able to spend more quality time with your real friends, the spoons.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler and Joey are returning from their brunch.

scenes.location: Mr. Geller's birthday party. Mr. and Mrs. Geller enter looking particularly refreshed. Monica follows looking rather pale

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Who's drink can I freshen?

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Almost time for cake.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Mon, Mon, are you OK?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You remember that video I found of mom and dad?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, I just caught the live show.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Eww.

scenes.scene_description: Mr. Geller's birthday party. Mr. and Mrs. Geller enter looking particularly refreshed. Monica follows looking rather pale.

scenes.location: Mr. Geller's birthday party. Monica and Richard are alone in the kitchen

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey there.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Nothing, I just heard something nice about you.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Humm, really? [Mrs. Geller and Ross both enter]

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Richard. Richard. Your son isn't seeing anyone is he?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Uhh, not that I know of.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Well, I was thinking, why doesn't he give Monica a call?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: That - that's an idea.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, actually, I'm already seeing someone.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ohh.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: She never tells us anything. Ross, did you know Monica's seeing someone?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Mom, there are so many people in my life. Some of them are seeing people and some of them aren't. Is that crystal?

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: So, who's the mystery man?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, uh, he's a doctor.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: A real doctor?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No, a doctor of meat. Of course he's a real doctor. And he's handsome, and he's sweet, and know you'd like him. [she puts her arm around Richard]

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Well that's wonderful. . . I

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Mom, it's OK.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: It is Judy.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Jack. Could you come in here for a moment? NOW!

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: [enters with his bat] Found it.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I'll take that dad. [grabs the bat]

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: It seems your daughter and Richard are something of an item.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: That's impossible, he's got a twinkie in the city.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Dad, I'm the twinkie.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: You're the twinkie?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: She's not a twinkie.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Al-alright, l-look you guys, this is the best relationship I've been in. . .

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Oh please, a relationship.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Yes, a relationship. For your information I am crazy about this man.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: Am I supposed to stand here and listen to this on my birthday?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Dad, dad this is a good thing for me. Ya know, and you even said yourself, you've never seen Richard happier.

lines.speaker: MR. GELLER
lines.text: When did I say that?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Upstairs in the bathroom right before you felt up mom. [Everyone else enters and all start singing Happy Birthday .]

scenes.scene_description: Mr. Geller's birthday party. Monica and Richard are alone in the kitchen.

scenes.location: Tattoo parlor. Rachel is showing Phoebe her tattoo

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh that looks so good, oh I love it.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I know, so do I. Oh Phoebe, I'm so glad you made me do this. OK, lemme se yours.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ahh. OK, let's see yours again.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Phoebe we just saw mine, let me see yours.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh OK. [pulls over her shirt and shows a bare shoulder] Oh no, oh it's gone, that's so weird, I don't know how-where it went.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You didn't get it?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Why didn't you get it?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Phoebe, how would you do this to me? This was all your idea.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I know, I know, and I was gonna get it but then he came in with this needle and uh, di-, did you know they do this with needles?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Really? You don't say, because mine was licked on by kittens.

scenes.scene_description: Tattoo parlor. Rachel is showing Phoebe her tattoo.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler is at the bar and Joey enters

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey listen, I'm sorry about what happened. . .

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah me too.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I know. Yeah.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah. So do we need to hug here or. . .

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, we're alright.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So I got ya something. [tosses Joey a bag of plastic spoons]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Plastic spoons. Great.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Lick away my man.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: These'll go great in my new place. You know, 'till I get real ones.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, I can't use these forever. I mean, let's face it, they're no friend to the environment.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No-no, I mean what, what's this about your new place?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I'm movin' out like we talked about.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well I didn't think that was serious. [grabs the spoons back] Ya know I thought that was just a fight.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, it was a fight. . . based on serious stuff, remember. About how I never lived alone or anything. I just think it would be good for me, ya know, help me to grow or. . . whatever.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, there you go.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, are you cool with this. I mean, I don't want to leave you high and dry.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey, no, I've never been lower or wetter. I'll be fine. I'll just turn your, uh, bedroom into a game room or somethin', you know, put the foosball table in there.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Woah. Why do you get to keep the table?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I did pay for half of it.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah. And uh, I paid for the other half.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Alright I'll tell you what, I'll play you for it.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Alright, you're on. I can take two minutes out of my day to kick your ass.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Your little men are gonna get scored on more times than your sister.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Woah, woah, woah, woah. Which sister?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler is at the bar and Joey enters.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Richard are setting the table

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So, are you sorry that I told them?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: No, it's been a long time since your dad and I went running. [Rachel and Phoebe enter]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh. Well did you get it? Let me see.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Is Ross here?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No he went out to get pizza.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh really, OK. [shows Monica her tattoo]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: That's great.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Very tasteful.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Wanna see mine, wanna see mine?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What? You didn't get one.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, well then what is this? [shows her bare shoulder]

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: What're we looking at? That blue freckle?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, that's my tattoo.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: That is not a tattoo, that is a nothing. I finally got her back in the chair, bairly touched her with a needle, she jumped up screaming, and that was it.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK, hi. For your information this is exactly what I wanted. This is a tattoo of the earth as seen from a great distance. It's the way my mother sees me from heaven.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, what a load of crap. That is a dot. Your mother is up in heaven going, 'Where the hell is my lily, you wuss?' OK, Phoebe, that is not a tattoo, this is a tattoo. [she bends over and bears her tattoo right when Ross returns]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You got a tattoo?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Maybe. But just a little one. Phoebe got the whole world.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Lemme see. [looks]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well it's really. . . sexy. I wouldn't have thought it would be but. . . wow.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, so uh, is it sore or can you do stuff?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I guess.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, save us some pizza. [they go off to Rachel's room]

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Richard are setting the table.

scenes.location: Chandler's apartment. Chandler and Joey are playing foosball for the table

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Get out of the corner. Pass it, pass it.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Stop talkin' to your men. [Joey scores]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yes! And the table is mine.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Congratulations. [Chandler leaves]

scenes.scene_description: Chandler's apartment. Chandler and Joey are playing foosball for the table.

scenes.location: Chandler's apartment. The whole gang is helping Joey pack

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, you guys are still gonna come visit me, right?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh yeah, you got the big TV. We'll be over there all the time. . . [Chandler gives him a look] except when we are here.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I know you're just moving uptown but I'm really gonna miss you.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I know, how can you not be accross the hall anymore.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah, who's gonna eat all our food, and tie up our phone lines, and - is that my bra? What the hell you doin' with my bra?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh no-no, it's uh, it's not what you think. We uh, we used it to, you know, fling water balloons off the roof. Remember that, those junior high kids couldn't even get theirs accross the street.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [quietly] Yeah, I remember.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, let's bring the rest of these down to the truck. [Everyone except Joey and Chandler leave.]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So, uhh, em, you want me to uh, give you a hand with the foosball table?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Naa, you keep it, you need the practice.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: So, I guess this is it.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, right, yeah, I guess so. [Joey walks to the door. He stops, turns around.]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Listen, uh, I don't know when I'm gonna see you again.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, I'm guessing uh, tonight at the coffee house.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Right, yeah. OK. Um, take care.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah. [Joey walks out and after a few seconds comes back in and gives Chandler a big hug. He then leaves for good and Chandler is left alone in his apartment.]

scenes.scene_description: Chandler's apartment. The whole gang is helping Joey pack.

title: The One Where Eddie Moves In

scenes.location: Joey's new apartment. Everyone but Chandler is there. Joey has decorated the place with tons of tacky stuff

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Huh? So whaddya think? Casa de Joey. Huh? I decorated it myself.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Get out.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [looking at some kind of glass sculpture thing] Wow Joey, this is, uhh...

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Art.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Art it is.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [looking at a glass table with a panther shaped base] Look, check this out. Is it a coffee table, is it a panther? There's no need to decide.

lines.speaker: RACHEL

lines.text: [looking at a water sculpture that looks like a window with rain running down it] Hey, excellent, excellent water-table thing.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Thanks, yeah. I love this but ya know what, it makes me wanna pee.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, well me too, yeah. I think that's the challenge.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, how come, uhh, Chandler didn't come?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well uh, it's cause he had a thing with, wi-, with the thing.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Right, I go-, I got it.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: So why don't ya show us the rest of your casa?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah. Uh, oh, OH, the best part, c'mon. [leads them to the bathroom, gestures towards toilet, everyone stares, uncomprehending] Heh?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey, nice toilet.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No no no, behind it.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Wha-, you have a phone in here?

lines.text: Joey, promise me something.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: MONICA

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: OK, here we go. Honey, I'm sorry, they were all out of apple pie, someone just got the last piece.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God. You are not gonna believe this. I have just been discovered.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Now wait a minute, I claimed you in the name of France four years ago.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Anyway, OK, now promise you won't like, freak out and say how great this is until I'm done, OK.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: OK. I just met this producer of this like, teeny record company, who said that I have a very fresh, offbeat sound and she wants to do a demo of

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: [congradulating her and celebrating]

lines.text: I told you not to do that yet. And, she wants to do a video.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: [celebrating more]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I'm not done yet, OK. God. OK, if that goes well, they may even want to make an album.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I'm done now.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: [celebrating]

lines.text: Oh God. Ross, OK, if you care about me at all, you will get the pie out of the man's hood.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Pie in the hood, pie in the hood. Go.

lines.speaker: GUY
lines.text: What're you doing?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I'm sorry, my pie was, was in your hood. Now I just have to get the coffee out of that guy's pants and I'll be back in the hospital by 7. [swats at an imaginary insect by his head, guy leaves promptly]

lines.text: So, whaddya say boys, should I call him? [squeezes the ear of one of the slippers and it barks] Well, ya know what they say. Ask your slippers a question... you're going crazy.

lines.text: Hey, hey, they're runnin'

lines.text: See, this is the brilliance of the show. I say always keep them running. All the time, running. Run. Run Yasmine, run like the wind.

lines.text: But I thought you wanted to live by yourself.

lines.text: You really think he'd take me? I mean, we had a pretty good talk last night but, when I moved out, I hurt him bad.

lines.text: I promise you, he would definitely want you back.

lines.text: But we had one of the greatest talks we ever had last night. I mean it was, it was like when we first started living together.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No. You're just gonna have to accept the fact that you're just friends now, OK, you're not... rommmates anymore.

He just comes in here, Mr. Jonny Neweggs, with his, his, his movin' the mail and his, his 'see ya pals'. And now there's no juice. There's no juice f or the people who need the juice and want the juice. I need the juice.

lines.text: There's another carton right over there.

lines.text: Hey, this isn't about juice anymore, alright man.

lines.speaker: JOEY

lines.text: Well what's the difference? Your eggs aren't here anymore, are they? You took your eggs and you left. You really expect me to never find new eggs?

lines.text: There's something I never told you Amber. I'm actually your half- brother.

lines.text: God that is good TV.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Everyone except Ross is there watching Days of Our Lives .

scenes.location: Chandler and Eddie's apartment. Chandler is at the foosball table trying to get Phoebe to play a game with him

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Phoebs, play with meeee.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No. This game is grotesque. Twenty armless guys joined at the waist by a steel bar, forced to play soccer forever. Ahh, hello, human-rights violation.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ya know Phoebs, don't feel so bad for 'em. After they're done playing, I break out the little plastic women and everybody has a pretty good time.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Why don't you play with your roommate?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ah he's a, he's not a big fan of foosball.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Uh oh, ooh, are we not getting along with the new boy?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No he's, he's alright, just uh, he spends most of his time in his room.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Maybe that's because you haven't taken the time to get to know him. Let's remedy that, shall we?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: We don't need to remedy that.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh yeah, it'll be fun. [throws a tennis ball at Eddie's bedroom door]

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hi, um, I just thought that it would be fun if the three of us had some beers and got to know each other.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Yeah alright, that sounds alright.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh good, ok. Oh nooo, I have to go because I'm late for my um, Green Eggs and Ham discussion group. Um tonight it's why he would not eat them on a train. Have fun bye.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: That was so lame.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I know, yeah. Ok, talk to him. [leaves]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So, you uh, you think that Speed Racer guy gets a lot of tickets er?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Eddie's apartment. Chandler is at the foosball table trying to get Phoebe to play a game with him.

scenes.location: Chandler and Eddie's apartment. Chandler and Eddie are talking

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: That's good, that's good. So, so, so who broke up with who?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: What're you kidding? I broke up with her. She actually thought that Sean Penn was the capital of Cambodia.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: That's good man, when everybody knows that the uh, the capital of Cambodia is uh...

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well it's not Sean Penn.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Not Sean Penn. Alright, I, I've got a funny one, alright. My last girlfriend Tilly. Ok, we're eating breakfast, right, and I made all these pancakes, there was like 50 pancakes right. And all of the sudden she turns to me, alright, and she says, 'Eddie.' I say, 'yeah,' she says, 'Eddie, I don't want to see you anymore.' And it was literally like she had reached into my chest, ripped out my heart, and smeared it all over my life, ya know. And now there's like this incredible abyss, ya know, and I'm falling and I keep falling and I don't think I'm ever gonna stop. [finishes laughing] That uh, wasn't such a funny story, was it?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Eddie's apartment. Chandler and Eddie are talking.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Phoebe is singing. Monica

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Phoebe's got another job, right?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Great set tonight Phoebs.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, we should probably get going.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Um, we should go too, I got patients at 8 in the moring.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ya know, I was thinking. Ya know how we always stay at your apartment? Well, I thought maybe tonight we'd stay at my place.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: I don't know, I don't have my jammies.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, maybe you don't need them.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: My baby sister, ladies and gentlemen.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Shut up, I'm happy.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, this is so nice. Alright I have to make a speech. I just wanna say that of all the guys that Monica has been with, and that is a lot, I like you the best.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Oh, thank you Phoebs. That's very sweet.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Hear that? She likes me best, and apparently there've been a lot.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Not a lot, Phoebe's kidding, Phoebe's crazy.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Phoebe's dead.
