scenes.location: Chandler and Eddie's apartment. Chandler is there. There's a knock at the door. He answers it to see a young woman holding a fishtank

lines.speaker: TILLY
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: TILLY
lines.text: I'm looking for Eddie Minowick.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, uh, he's not here right now, uh, I'm Chandler, can I take a message, or, or a fishtank?

lines.speaker: TILLY
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh, oh, c'mon in.

lines.speaker: TILLY
lines.text: I'm Tilly.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: TILLY
lines.text: I gather by that oh that he told you about me.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh yeah, your uh, name came up in a uh, conversation that terrified me to my very soul.

lines.speaker: TILLY
lines.text: He's kind of intense huh?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yes. Hey, can I ask you, is Eddie a little...

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: [walks around corner] A little what?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Bit country? C'mon in here you roomie.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Hello Tilly.

lines.speaker: TILLY
lines.text: Eddie, I just came by to drop off your tank.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: That's very thoughtful of you. It's very thougtful.

lines.speaker: TILLY
lines.text: Well, ok then. I'm gonna go. Bye.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Bye-bye.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So, we gettin' a fish?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: You had sex with her didn't you?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Eddie's apartment. Chandler is there. There's a knock at the door. He answers it to see a young woman holding a fishtank.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Joey enters with several magazines and runs up to Phoebe

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Phoebs, check it out, check it out, check it out, check it out.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, ooh, Soap Opera Digest, oh that's one of my favorite digests.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Page 42, page 42, page 42.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, ok, ok. Ooh, hey 'new doc on the block, Days of Our Lives' Joey Tribbiani.' Ooh, cool picture.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ooh, I look good.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey is this true, that you write a lot of your own lines?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uh, well, kinda yeah. Like, remember last week when Alex was in the accident? Well the line in the script was, 'If we don't get this woman to a hospital, she's going to die.' But I made it, ' If this woman doesn't get to a hospital, she's not gonna live.'

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ohh, ok, I see what you did there. Aren't you afraid though, that the writers are gonna be kinda mad when they read this?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Huh? Never really thought about the writers. The scripts just kinda come to my house. But you know what? This makes me look good, which makes the show look good, which makes the writers look good so how could they be mad about that?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Joey enters with several magazines and runs up to Phoebe.

scenes.location: At a writer's desk. The writer is working on a script for Days of Our Lives

lines.speaker: WRITER
lines.text: Makes up most of his lines. Son-of-a-. Yeah, well, write this jerkweed.

scenes.scene_description: At a writer's desk. The writer is working on a script for Days of Our Lives.

scenes.location: Joey's apartment. The next script is being delivered

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I fall down an elevator shaft? What the hell does this mean, I fall down an elevator shaft?

lines.speaker: DELIVERY GUY
lines.text: Uhh, I don't know, I just bring the scripts.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: They can't kill me, I'm Francesca's long lost son.

lines.speaker: DELIVERY GUY
lines.text: Right. Could you sign this?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No. No way, I'm not signing that.

lines.speaker: DELIVERY GUY
lines.text: I don't think that's gonna affect the plot of the show.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: How can they do this to me?

lines.speaker: DELIVERY GUY
lines.text: Er, uh, I'm just gonna go. Sorry.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well it wasn't that many guys. I mean, if you consider how many guys there actually are, it's a very small percentage.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey, it's not that big a deal, I was just curious.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: G'night.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Night Richard. Good luck Mon.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright, before I tell you, uh, why don't you tell me how many women you've been with.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Two.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Two? TWO? How is that possible? I mean, have you seen you?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Well, I mean what can I say? I, I was married to Barbara for 30 years. She was my high school sweetheart, now you, that's two.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Two it is. Ok, time for bed, I'm gonna go brush my teeth. [goes in the bathroom]

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Woah, woah, no wait a minute now. C'mon it's your turn. Oh c'mon. Ya know, I don't need the actual number, just a ballpark.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, it is definitely less than a ballpark.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Wow, I am so glad I'm not Monica right now.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Tell me about it. So what, what's your magic number?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Uhhhooo.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: C'mon, you know everyone I've been with. All, both of them.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, there's you.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Better not be doin' these in order.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, uh, Billy Dreskin, Pete Carney, Barry, and uh, oh, Paolo.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh yes, the weenie from Torrini.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh honey, are you jealous of Paolo? Oh, c'mon, I'm so much happier with you than I ever was with him.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh please. That Paolo thing was barely a relationship. All it really was was just, ya know, meaningless animal sex. Ok, ya know, that sounded soooo much better in my head.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Eddie, I didn't sleep with your ex-girlfriend.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: That's very interesting, ya know, 'cause that's exactly what someone who slept with her would say.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: This is nuts. This is crazy. She came over for like two minutes, dropped off a fish tank, and left, end of story.

lines.text: Where's Buddy?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Buddy?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: My fish, Buddy.

lines.text: There was no fish when she dropped it off.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Oh, this is, this is unbelievable. I mean, first you sleep with my ex-girlfriend then you insult my inteligenct by lying about it and then you kill my fish, my Buddy?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey I didn't kill your fish. Look Eddie...[puts his hand on Eddie's shoulder] Would you look at what I'm doin' here. That can't be smart. So we're just gonna take this guy right off ya and put him here in Mr. Pocket. Tangellon? [picks up the fruit an tosses it to Eddie, it hits Eddie in the chest and falls]

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: That's it? That's the giant number you were afraid to tell me?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well yeah.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Well, that's not bad at all. I mean, you had me thinkin it was like a fleet.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You really ok with it?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Oh honey, I'm fine.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, yay. Ok about that two.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: What? Alright, what about my two?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, it just seems like a really small number.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Right, and...

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: And, well, don't you have a lot of wild oats to sew? Or is that what you're doing with me? Oh my God, am I an oat?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Honey, you are not an oat. I, I mean I don't know, I, I guess I'm just not an oat guy. I've only slept with women I've been in love with.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: But you've only slept with two people.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Wow. Oh wow. You know I love you too, right.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Now I do. [they kiss and fall to the bed]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ross, Ross, please listen to me. Ross, you are so much better for me than Paolo ever was. I mean you care about me, you're loving, you make me laugh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, hey, if I make you laugh, here's an idea, why don't you invite Paulo over and have a little romp in the sack and I'll just stand in the corner and tell knock-knock jokes.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: God, Ross, look, what you and I have is special, all Paolo and I ever had was...

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Animal sex, animal sex? So what're you saying, I mean, you're saying that like, there's nothing between us animal at all. I mean there's not even like, uhm, a little animal, not even, not even like, like chipmunk sex?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, Ross, try to hear me. Ok, I, hey, I'm not gonna lie to you. Ok, it was good with Paolo.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Knock-knock.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: But, what you and I have is so much better. Ok, we have tenderness, we have intimacy, we connect. Ya know, I swear, this is the best I have ever had.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Until now. [jumps on Rachel on the bed]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, hi.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hi. Richard just told me he loves me.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh my God, honey that's great.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I know. I just can't find...

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh they're in the top drawer. Hurry.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You need one too?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ooooh yeah.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: There's only one.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Monica.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hi. Uh, we'll be right there, we're just trying to decide something. [shuts the bathroom door]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [comes out of the bedroom] Rachel. [growls then sees Richard standing there] Hey.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Hey. They're just trying to decide somehting.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Good, good, good. So, is uh, was your moustache, did, used to be different?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh. How do you uh, ya know, keep it so neat?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: I have a little comb.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh. And what do you call that?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: A moustache comb.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, I, I will do your laundry for one month.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, ok, ok, I will, I will, I, hey, I will clean the apartment for two months.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright, I tell you what, I'll give this to you now if you can tell me where we keep the dustpan.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Agghhh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: So were you in Nam?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Rock-paper-scissors?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: RACHEL and MONICA
lines.text: One two three. [Rachel picks rock, Monica picks scissors]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeesss.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Fine, go have sex.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: No. You have got it completely wrong. John Savage was deerhunter, no legs, John Voit was coming home, couldn't feel his legs.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, no way. You've got it totally the other way around my friend. John Voit was...

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Honey.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What, what oh....[Ross and Rachel go into her room]

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Shall we?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: It's not gonna happen. They're doing it tonight, we can do it tomorrow.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Uh, in the future, if I could see the schedule beforehand...

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So, when I woke up this morning, he'd stolen all the insoles out of my shoes.

lines.text: Because he thinks I slept with his ex-girlfriend and killed his fish.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Because sometimes, Phoebe after you sleep with someone, you have to kill the fish.

lines.text: Yeah.

lines.text: Alright.

lines.text: Oh Drake.

lines.text: Some guys are just lucky I guess.

lines.speaker: INTERCOM
lines.text: Dr. Remore, report to first floor emergency, stat.

lines.speaker: DR. REMORE
lines.text: Well then, uh, I uhh, guess that's me. Anyone else need to go on the elevator? Dr. Horton, Dr. Wong?

lines.speaker: DR. HORTON
lines.text: No, no, they only said you.

lines.speaker: DR. REMORE
lines.text: Oh, ok. Alright.

lines.speaker: AMGER
lines.text: I love you Drake.

lines.speaker: DR. REMORE
lines.text: Yeah, whatever. Oh no.

lines.speaker: AMBER
lines.text: Drake, look out.

lines.speaker: DR. REMORE
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Did they just kill off Joey?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No. [sound of Dr. Remore's body hitting the bottom of the shaft] Now maybe.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: C'mon.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Open up. We want to talk to you.

lines.text: I don't feel like talkin.

lines.text: Oh c'mon Joey, we care about you.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: We're worried about you.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: And some of us really have to pee.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Sorry Joey [runs to the bathroom]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Listen, sorry about your death, that really sucks.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: We came over as soon as we saw.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: How could you not tell us?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I don't know, I was kinda hopin' no one would ever find out.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, maybe they can find a way to bring you back.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Naa, they said that when they found my body, my brain was so smashed in that the only doctor that could have saved me was me. Supposed to be some kind of irony or somethin.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: But Joey, you're gonna be fine. You don't need that show, it was just a dumb soap opera.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Phoebe, this was the greatest thing that ever happened to me.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yes, I was going to incorporate that. Oh good, here's Monica, she'll have something nice to say.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Um, I straightened out your shower curtain so you won't get mildew. What? To me that's nice.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: It's gonna be ok. You know that?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, I don't. It's like, ya know, you work your whole life for somethin' and you think that when you get it it's never gonna be as good as you thought it would be. But this so was. Ya know, it changed everything. Like the other day, I got this credit card application, and I was pre- approved. Huh? I've never been pre-approved for anything in my life.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I'm sorry man.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah, Joey honey, I don't know if this'll mean anything to you but you'll always be pre-approved with us.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, that means nothin to me.

lines.text: Uhhhaahh.

title: The One Where Eddie Won't Go

scenes.location: Chandler's bedroom. Chandler is sleeping and Eddie is there watching him

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey Eddie. Daahh!! What're you doin' here?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Nothin' roomie, just watchin' you sleep.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Makes me feel um, peaceful, heh-heh, please.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I can't sleep now.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: You want me to sing?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No, look, that's it, it's over, I want you out, I want you out of the apartment now.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Woah, woah, woah, what're, what're you talkin' about man.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hannibal Lecter...better roommate than you.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: No. See now I don't think you're being fair. I mean one night you see me and you get scared, I mean, what about all the other nights when you don't see me, huh? What about last night when you went and got a drink of water and I was nice enough to hide behind the door, what's that about, huh?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I didn't realize that.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: GET OUT NOW!!

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Ok, you really want me out?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Ok, then I want to hear you say it, I, I want to hear you say you want me out.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I want you out.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: No no no, I wanna hear it from your lips.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Where did you hear it from before?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Oh, right, all right, you know what pallie I understand, consider me gone, you know what, I'll be out by the time you get home from work tomorrow.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: I heard that.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler's bedroom. Chandler is sleeping and Eddie is there watching him.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Monica and Rachel are there

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey. Whe-ell, look at you, finally got that time machine workin' huh?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Seriously, you like it? This guy was sellin' them on 8th avenue and I looked at 'em and I though, you know what I don't have?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: A mirror?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Fine, make fun. I think it's jaunty.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Wow, for a guy who's recently lost his job, you're in an awfully good mood.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, I'll be alright. I mean it's not like I'm starting from sqare one. I was Dr. Drake Remoray on

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Cache? Jaunty?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Chandler gave me word of the day toilet paper. I'm gonna get some coffee.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oooh, so so so, did you read the book?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh my God, it was incredible.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Didn't it like totally speak to you?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Woah, woah, woah, what book is this?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Rachel you have to read this book. It's called

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah and oh, and but there's, there's wind and the wind can make us Goddesses. But you know who takes out wind? Men, they just take it.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Men just take out wind?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ya-huh, all the time, cause they are the lightning bearers.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well that sounds kinda cool, kinda like

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: It is nothing like the Hobbit. It's like reading about every relationship I've ever had, except for Richard.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh yes, no, Richard would never steal your wind.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No, 'cause he's yummy.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Yes. But all the other ones.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh yes. Oh and, the part about how they're always like drinking from out pool of inner power, but God forbid we should take a sip.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Anybody want a croan.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, this is a typical lightning-bearer thing. Right there, it's like, um, 'Hello, who wants one of my fallic shaped man cakes?'

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Monica and Rachel are there, Joey enters wearing an old looking hat.

scenes.location: Estelle Leonard Talent Agency.Joey is there

lines.speaker: ESTELLE
lines.text: Don't worry about it already. Things happen.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: So, you're not mad at me for getting fired and everything?

lines.speaker: ESTELLE
lines.text: Joey, look at me, look at me. Do I have lipstick on my teeth?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, can we get back to me?

lines.speaker: ESTELLE
lines.text: Look honey, people get fired left and right in this business. I already got you an audition for

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Alright. Cab driver number two?

lines.speaker: ESTELLE
lines.text: You're welcome.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: But I was Dr. Drake Remoray. How can I go from bein' a neurosurgeon to drivin' a cab?

lines.speaker: ESTELLE
lines.text: Things change, roll with em.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: But this is a two line part, it's like takin' a step backwards. I'm not gonna do this.

lines.speaker: ESTELLE
lines.text: Joey, I'm gonna tell you the same thing I told Al Minser and his pyramid of dogs. Take any job you can get and don't make on the floor.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I'm sorry. See ya.

scenes.scene_description: Estelle Leonard Talent Agency.Joey is there.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Monica

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, God, oh, God, I mean it's just so.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Isn't it.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Uhh, I mean this is like reading about my own life. I mean this book could have been called 'Be Your Own Windkeeper Rachel'.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I don't think it would have sold a million copies but it would have made a nice gift for you.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey you guys.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh, sweetie we've gotta go.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: NO!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No, why do we always have to do everything according to your time table?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Actually it's the movie theatre that has the time schedule. So you don't miss the beginning.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No, see this isn't about the movie theatre, this is about you stealing my wind.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You go girl. I can't pull that off can I?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Excuse me, your, your, your wind?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yes, my wind. How do you expect me to grow if you won't let me blow?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You, you know I, I don't, have a- have a problem with that.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, I just, I just really need to be with myself right now. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Um-um, um-um.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You're right, I don't have to apologize. Sorry. Damnit!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Monica, Phoebe, and Rachel are there. Rachel has just finished reading the book.

scenes.location: Joey's apartment. Joey and Ross enter

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I, I don't know, it's got all this stuff about wind and trees and there's some kind of sacred pool in it. I mean, I don't really get it but she's, she's pretty upset about it.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: See, this is why I don't date women who read. Uh-oh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What, what's that?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: It's my VISA bill. Envelope one of two. That can't be good.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Open it, open in.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Woah.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Look at this, how did I spend so much money?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh Joey, that's just the minumum amount due, that's your total due.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ahh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What, woah, woah, $3500 at porcelain safari?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: My animals. Hey the guy said they suited me, he spoke with an accent, I was all confused. I don't know what I'm gonna do.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well I guess you can start by drivin a cab on

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That audition.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: That's a two line part.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Joey, you owe $1100 at I Love Lucite.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: So what.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: So suck it up man, it's a job, it's money.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, look, I don't need you getting all judgemental and condescending and pedantic.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Toilet paper?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Look, I'm not being any of those things, ok, I'm just being realistic.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well knock it off, you're supposed to be my friend.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I am your friend.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well then tell me things like, 'Joey you'll be fine,' and, 'Hang in there,' and, and, 'Somethin' big's fonna come along, I know it.'

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: But I don't know it. What I do know is that you owe $2300 at Isn't it Chromantic.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey Ross, I'm aware of what I owe.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, well then get some sense. I mean it took you what, 10 years to get that job, who knows how long it's gonna be till you get another.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Look, I don't wanna hear this right now.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Huh, I'm just saying...

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well don't just say.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ya know, maybe, maybe I should just go.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok. I'll see ya later. Just think about it, ok.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I don't need to think about it. I was Dr. Drake Remoray. That was huge. Big things are gonna happen, you'll see. Ross, you still there?

scenes.scene_description: Joey's apartment. Joey and Ross enter.

scenes.location: Chandler's apartment. Chandler peeks in the door. He doesn't see Eddie so he enters

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Hey pal.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ahhhh-gaaaahhh. Eddie what're you still doin' here?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Ah, just some basic dehydrating of a few fruits and vegetables. MAN ALIVE this thing's fantastic!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Look Eddie, aren't you forgetting anything?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Oh yeah, that's right, look I got us a new goldfish. He's a lot fiestier that the last one.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Maybe 'cause the last one was made by Pepperidge Farm. Look Eddie, isn't there something else you're supposed to be doing right now?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Well, not unless it's got something to do with dehydrating my man because right now I'm a dehydrating maniac!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Look you have to help me out here. I thought we had a deal. I thought by the time...

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Ah-ah-ah, you know what that is?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Your last roommate's kidney?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: That's a tomato. This one definitely goes in the display.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler's apartment. Chandler peeks in the door. He doesn't see Eddie so he enters, breathing a sigh of relief. Eddie pops up from behind the bar.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Joey goes up to the bar to order

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey Gunther, let me get a lemonade to go.

lines.speaker: GUNTHER
lines.text: Lemonade? You ok man?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ah, it's career stuff. I don't know if you heard but they killed off my character on the show.

lines.speaker: GUNTHER
lines.text: Oh, that's too bad. How'd they do it?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I fell down an elevator shaft.

lines.speaker: GUNTHER
lines.text: That sucks. I was buried in an avalanche.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: GUNTHER
lines.text: I used to be Bryce on

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Joey goes up to the bar to order.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Chandler is sleeping on the couch. Monica walks by and starts watching him

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Daaahhhh!

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Aaahhhhhhh! Aaahhhh!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Why must everybody watch me sleep? There'll be no more watching me sleep, no more watching.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I wa-

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Uuuh.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Chandler is sleeping on the couch. Monica walks by and starts watching him.

scenes.location: Chandler's apartment. Chandler is returning from Monica and Rachel's with his bedding. Eddie is standing at the bar with his dehydrator and loads of fruit

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Hey man, check it out, I got some great stuff to dehydrate here. I got some grapes, got some apricots, I thought it would be really cool to see what happens with these water balloons.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Get out. Get out, get out, get out, get out, get out, get out.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You, move out. Take your fruit, your stupid small fruit and GET OUT!

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: You, you want, you want me to move out?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: I uh, I gotta tell you man, I mean, that's uh, it's kinda out of the blue, I mean don't you think?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: This is not out of the blue, this is smack dab in the middle of the blue.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Ohhhh. Relax, take it easy buddy. Tell me twice, you want me to go? Alright, alright, guess I'll be back for my stuff. [walks out the door and after a pause comes back in] But if you think for one second I'm leaving you alone with my fish, you're insane Jack!

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You want some help.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: No help required Chico. [reaches into the tank and grabs the fish and puts it in his pocket]

scenes.scene_description: Chandler's apartment. Chandler is returning from Monica and Rachel's with his bedding. Eddie is standing at the bar with his dehydrator and loads of fruit.

scenes.location: Joey is at the cab driver interview

lines.speaker: CASTING GUY
lines.text: Excuse me, that's 50 bucks.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: CASTING GUY
lines.text: Five oh dollars.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ohh, you know what it is? It's smudgy 'cause they're fax pages. Now when I was on

lines.speaker: CASTING GUY
lines.text: That's great.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: And, and just so you know, if you wanted to expand this scene like, like have the cab crash or somethin', I could attend to the victims 'cause I have a background in medical acting.

lines.speaker: CASTING GUY
lines.text: Ok, listen, thanks for coming in.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No no, uh, don't thank me for comin' in. Uh, at least let me finish.

scenes.scene_description: Joey is at the cab driver interview.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, question number 28, have you ever allowed a lighning bearer to take your wind? I would have to say no.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: And I would have to say pah-huh.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Do you not remember the puppet guy?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah you like totally let him wash his feet in the pool of your inner power.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: And his puppet too.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah ok, well at least I didn't let some guy into the forest of my righteous truth on the first date.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Paul.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, ok, ok, moving on, moving on, next question. Ok number 29, have you ever betrayed another goddess for a lightning bearer? Ok, number 30.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Woah, woah, woah, let's go back to 29.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Not uh, not to my recollection.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Huuh, alright, Danny Arshak, ninth grade. Oh, c'mon Rach, you know the bottle was totally pointing at me.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Only 'cause you took up half the circle.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Listen to you two. It's so sad. Looks like I'm gonna be going to the goddess meetings alone.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well not when they find out you slept with Jason Hurley an hour after he broke up with Monica.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: One hour? You are such a leaf blower.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe are sitting around the coffee table.

scenes.location: Joey's apartment. Joey is watching movers take all his stuff away

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh hey uh, be careful with that 3-D last supper, Judas is a little loose.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [enters] Oh my God, what's goin' on?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: They're takin all my stuff back. I guess you were right.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No look I wasn''t right, that's what I came here to tell you. I was totally hung up on, on my own stuff. Listen, I'm someone who needs the whole security thing, ya know. To know exactly where my next paycheck is coming from buy you, you don't need that and that's amazing to me. I could never do what you do Joey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Thanks Ross.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah. And you should hold out for something bigger. I can't tell you how much respect I have for you not going to that stupid cab driver audition.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I went.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Great, how did it go?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I didn't get it.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Good for you.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You're livin' the dream.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: All right then.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [movers removing a glass parrot] Oh, not my parrot.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I can't watch this.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [approaching the mover holding the parrot] Hey hold on, hold on. How much for the uh, how much to save the bird?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Dollars? You spent $1200 dollars on a plastic bird?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uhhh, I was an impulse buyer, near the register.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Go ahead, go ahead with the bird. Ok, do you have anything for around 200?

lines.speaker: MOVER
lines.text: Uh, the dog. [points to a big poecelain greyhound]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: MOVER
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I'll take it. My gift to you man.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Thanks Ross. I really like that bird though...I'll take the dog though.

scenes.scene_description: Joey's apartment. Joey is watching movers take all his stuff away.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Monica and Phoebe are sitting ignoring each other. Rachel walks up with two pieces of cake

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Here are your cakes.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: We didn't order cake.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No, I know, they're from me. Look you guys this is not good. I mean we have enough trouble with guys stealing our wind without taking it from each other.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You're right.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You know.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I love you goddesses. I don't ever want to suck your wind again.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Thank you. So are we good?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: We're good.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: We're good?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, let me take these cakes back 'cause they're gonna take that out of my paycheck.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [enters] Ding dong, the psycho's gone.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Are you sure this time?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yes, yes I actually saw him leave. I mean that guy is standing in the window holding a human head. He is STANDING IN THE WINDOW HOLDING A HUMAN HEAD!

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: [enters] Check it out man, I tore it off some mannaquin in the alley behind Macy's.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: There is no alley behind Macy's.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: So I got it in the junior miss department, big diff. Anyway check it out man, it's gonna make a hell of a conversation piece at out next cocktail party, huh pal?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Our next cocktail party?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Yeah, you know, put chips in it, we'll make like a chip chick.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Eddie, do you remember yesterday?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Uh yes, I think I vaguely recall it.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Do you remember talking to me yesterday?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Uh, yes.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So what happened?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: We took a road trip to Las Vegas man.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh sweet Moses.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So on this road trip, did you guys win any money?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Naah, I crapped out, but Mr. 21 over here he cleans up, 300 bucks, check it out he buys me these new shoes, sweet huh?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Nice.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Yeah. Well see ya upstairs. See ya pals.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Is anyone else starting to really like him?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Monica and Phoebe are sitting ignoring each other. Rachel walks up with two pieces of cake.

scenes.location: Hallway outside Chandler and Joey's apartment. Eddie walks up

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Why doesn't my key work and what's all my stuff doin' downstairs?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, I'm, I'm sorry...[Eddie forces his head in the door] Ahhh. Have we met?

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: It's Eddie you freak, your roommate.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I, I'm sorry, I uh [unchains the door and opens it all the way] I already have a roommate. [Joey turns around in the leather recliner]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, he's lived here for years, I don't, I don't know what you're talking about man.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: No he, he moved out and I moved in.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well I, I think we'd remember something like that.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I know I would.

lines.speaker: EDDIE
lines.text: Well that's uh, that's a good point. Um ok, well, uh, I guess I got the wrong apartment then. I, I'm, look, I'm, ya know, I'm sorry, I'm terriably sorry.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey no problem.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: See ya. [shuts the door] Goodbuy you fruit drying psychopath. So you want me to help you unpack your stuff?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Na, na I'm ok. Oh and uh, just so you know, I'm not movin' back in 'cause I have to. Well, I mean, I do have to. It's just that that place wasn't really, I mean, this is...

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Welcome home man. [they hug and jump around]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: A little foos?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What happened to the foosball?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ah that's a cantelope.

scenes.scene_description: Hallway outside Chandler and Joey's apartment. Eddie walks up.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. They are hauling out the porcelain dog from Joey's room. Chandler is holding the dog by the rear in a rather interesting position

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey look, are we gonna have to bring this out every time Ross comes over?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: He paid a lot of money for it.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I'm gonna hold him a different way. Look I don't understand, if you hated it so much, why did you buy it in the first place?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, I had a whole ceramic zoo thing goin' over there but now, without the other ones, it just looks tacky.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So is he housetrained or is he gonna leave little bathroom tiles all over the place? Stay. Good, STAY! Good fake dog.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. They are hauling out the porcelain dog from Joey's room. Chandler is holding the dog by the rear in a rather interesting position.

scenes.location: Chandler's bedroom. Chandler is sleeping and Eddie is there watching him
scenes.scene_description: Chandler's bedroom. Chandler is sleeping and Eddie is there watching him.

title: The One Where Old Yeller Dies

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Phoebe enters to see Monica

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey. Watcha guys doin?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Monica's making us watch

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Why are you guys so upset? It's

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What're you talkin' about?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: C'mon, happy family gets a dog, frontier fun.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah but Phoebs, what about the end?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: What when Yeller saves saves the family from the wolf and everyone's happy?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: That's not the end.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yu-huh. That's when my mother would shut off the TV and say 'The end'.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: What about the part where he has rabies?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: He doesn't have rabies, he has babies. That's what my mom said.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Uh, Phoebe, I don't think your mom would want you to see what's about to happen.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: What, what's about to happen? [starts watching] I've never seen this part before. Hey, Travis, watcha doin' with that gun? Oh no, no no Travis, put down the gun. No no no no, he he's your buddy, he's your Yeller, no, no no, the end, THE END. [hear the gunshot from the TV]

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Phoebe enters to see Monica, Richard, Ross, and Rachel tearfully watching TV.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Richard is on the balcony smoking and Monica is on the phone

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey, have you guys eaten, because uh, Richard and I just finished and we've got leftovers... Chicken and potatoes... What am I wearing?...Actually, nothing but rubber gloves.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ya know, one of these times you're gonna really be naked and we're not gonna come over.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright, I've got a leg, three breasts and a wing.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, how do you find clothes that fit?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh, hey, Monica, we've got a question.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright, for the bizillionth time, yes I see other women in shower at the gym, and no I don't look.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No, not that one. We're trying to figure out who to bring to the Knicks game tonight, we have an extra ticket.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, Ross can't go so it's between my friend Eric Prower who has breath issues and Dan with the poking. [starts poking Monica in the shoulder] 'Did you see that play? Do you want some more beer? Is that Spike Lee?'

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok. [Richard walks in] Hey, why don't you ask Richard?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ok, uh, hey Richard, if you had an extra ticket to the Knicks game and you had to choose between a friend who smells and one who bruises you who would you pick?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Wow. Well being a huge Knicks fan myself, I think you should take someone who's a huge Knicks fan.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok, that's Eric.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Glad to be of help. Matches. [walks out to the balcony]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I meant, why don't you take Richard to the game? What?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: C'mon. Keeps his fingers to himself and he's always minty fresh.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I don't know, Richard's really nice and everything, uh, it's just that we don't know him really well, ya know, and plus he's, ya know, old [Monica gives him a glare] -er than some people, but, uh, younger than some buildings.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So what, he's a little older, big deal, I mean he's important to me. Ya know if you ask him, he might take you on his Jag. [walks off]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: How do we say yes now and make it seem like we're not doin' it just to ride in the cool car?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok, this could be tough.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [Monica walks back in the kitchen] Ok ok ok ok. Monica, we'll bring him, but only if he takes the Jaguar.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ooh, you almost had it.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Richard is on the balcony smoking and Monica is on the phone.

scenes.location: Ross's apartment. Ross is holding Ben

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [smells Ben's butt] No no, you're fine, you're fine.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: [enters with Susan] Hello

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Hi honey.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh you guys are not gonna believe what happened.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, we were sitting over there playing on the floor and he grabs the table and he pulls himself up. He pulled himself up. Standing man. I'm sorry you guys missed it but I did tape it so it you guys want to see it.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Uhh, we know, he already did it last week.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: You can watch our tape if you want.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I don't believe this. I miss, I miss the first time of everything. I missed, what, the first time he rolled over, the first time he crawled. What else did I miss? Has he spoken yet, is he driving, does he have a favorite liquour?

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Actually, he is getting closer on the talking thing. He can't quite say mama yet, but once he said yumen.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ooh, I, I'm so sick of missing stuff. Ya know, I want him for more than, than a day, I want him for a whole weekend. No listen , I mean, I feel like-

lines.speaker: CAROL and SUSAN
lines.text: Great. That would be fine.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Really? I mean, I, I had a whole speach prepared.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: Oh shout, that would have been fun.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh look, did, did you just see that? Did you see? He just waved, he just waved, he's never waved before, you've never waved before. Yes he has. Very good.

scenes.scene_description: Ross's apartment. Ross is holding Ben.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Monica and Rachel are there. Phoebe enters carrying video tapes

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey Phoebs, whatcha got there?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok,

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Wow, all you need now is

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, I talked to my grandma about the

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey. Where is he, where's Richard? Did you ditch him?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah right after we stole his lunch money and gave him a wedgie. What's the matter with you, he's parking the car.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So'd you guys have fun?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Your boyfriend is so cool.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, he let us drive his Jaguar. Joey for 12 blocks, me for 15.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Wow, he must like you the best.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh, what about that thing he did when he tipped the guy who showed us to our seats. You never even saw the money, it was like this. [With money in his palm] Hey Chandler, thanks for showing us to our seats [shakes his had and passes the dollar].

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You're welcome. Hey Joey, thanks for parking the car [passes the dollar back].

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No problem. Hey Chandler

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I think they get it.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: There's the man.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: He-he-eyy. [Shakes his had and passes the dollar]

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Hey, you're gettin' better. I'm gonna keep this by the way.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ok. He kept my dollar.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Monica and Rachel are there. Phoebe enters carrying video tapes.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Carol and Susan are dropping off Ben

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So your first whole weekend without Ben, what're you guys gonna do?

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Uh, we're going down to Colonial Williamsburg.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: Yeah, a woman I went to college with just became the first female blacksmith down there.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, ya know, they're a little behind the times in Colonial Williamsburg.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Think I better go before mommy starts weeping.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Buy mommy.

lines.speaker: CAROL and SUSAN
lines.text: We love you. Buy.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Have a good time. Ok, Ben.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ross.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Look. [they look at Joey in the kitchen with a cigar in his mouth, looking at his reflection in a spatula] Joey, do you know we can see you from here?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: How come Richard looks so much cooler with one of these than me?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well for starters, you may want to light it and lose the spatula.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You know what, I think it's cute, you trying to be more like Richard.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Not like him, per-se, just not un-like him.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Look it's the artist formerly known as Chandler.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Just tryin' somethin' here, ya know.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So Joey, why didn't you grow a moustache?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh we flipped for it. I got the cigar, he got the moustache. Figured if we both grew it, we'd look like dorks.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, you really sidestepped that land mine.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey listen, we've gotta go, I promised Richard we'd meet him downstairs.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You're meeting Richard?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, we're goin' to a Ranger game.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, didn't he tell ya?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, he told me he was going out with the guys, I just didn't know that you were the guys.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You hear that? We're the guys.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: We're the guys.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: With that moustache doesn't Chandler remind you of Aunt Sylvia?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Thank you.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Carol and Susan are dropping off Ben.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Rachel is working. Ross enters with Ben

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi, we're visitiing. It's Ben and his da-da. Da-da. Can you say da-da? Look, I'm gonna tell your momies you said it anyway so you might as well try.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No luck huh?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Naa. A while ago I got a sah out of him, which I thought, ya know, might turn into sah-condary caregiver but... Hey, would you uh, would you hold him for a sec, 'cause I, I gotta take this off.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, yeah sure, Ok. [she takes Ben and holds him at arms length]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What're you doing?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Uh, I'm holding Ben.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, well, he's a baby not a bomb.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well just hold him like you'd hold a football.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: This is how I would hold a football.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, here, here. There we go.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, I'm sorry, I'm just not very good with babies. I mean I haven't been around them, I mean, you know, since I was one.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: It's alright, it's no big deal.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, definitely, I'm sure you'll feel totally different when it's our baby.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You think about stuff like that?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uhh, yeah. I mean, actually I kinda think that we'll have, we'll have two babies.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Two, two babies?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah. Ya know, a boy and a girl. Hopefully the girl will come first so Ben here won't feel too competitive.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Then what's gonna happen?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, we won't wanna raise kids in the city so we'll probably move to uh, Scarsdale.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, that way I figure, ya know, we'll be far enough away from our parents that we don't have to see them all the time but close enough that they can come over and babysit whenever we want. And yes, I know, the taxes are a little higher than, let's say, Nassau county but the school system's supposedly great.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Wow. Wow, that's great. Great. Ok, wow, you know what.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I'm off my break now so uh, um here you take this [hands back Ben] and um, I am gonna go pour these very nice people some coffee. Ok. Oh look at that, I don't have a pot. I don't have a pot. Well, hey, maybe I've got one at home, or in Scarsdale. Hey is that a door? [leaves]

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Rachel is working. Ross enters with Ben.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Richard are there. Phoebe enters

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Hey Phoebs, what's happening?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, ok, murder, cancer, soccer teams eating each other in the Andes.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So you watched the movies huh?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Uh huh, what is happening to the world? I mean, no no no, 'cause ET leaves, and and Rocky loses, Charlotte dies.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Charlotte who?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: With the web, the spider she dies, she does. She has babies and dies. It's like ya know, hey welcome home from the hospital, thud.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright, you wanna feel better?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, here, watch this.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So you can't lose, it's there in the title. Wonderfullness is baked right in.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Please, I almost fell for that with, uh,

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Uh, the guy was Lou Gehrig. Didn't you kinda see it coming?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Phoebe, just watch that, I promise it will resotre all your faith in humanity.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [runs in] Hey, big guy, game time.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Hey, be right there.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: There's a game?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Uh, yeah, I just got my pick-up sticks back from the shop. Bring your nerves of steel.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: It's the basketball playoffs.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Listen, um honey, I appreciate this but you don't have to keep hanging out with them for me, I mean, they have each other.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Oh, no, honey, I mean, don't worry, I like hanging out with those guys. It's fun for me. They're different than my other friends, they don't start sentences with, 'You know who just died shoveling snow?'

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright that's great, then just go. Go Knicks.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Uh, it's the college playoffs.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, then go Vassar.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Uh, they're not in it.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, then just go.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Ok. [leaves]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, why does this bother me so much? I mean I don't wanna be one of those people who tells their boyfriend they wanna spend 24 hours a day with them.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: It's just that he doesn't have that much free time, ya know, and I don't know, what do I do?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Does it matter? You're ultimately just gonna die or get divorced or have to blow your pets head off.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: [enters] Aghh.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Me too. [leaves]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I've got a question. Richard made plans again with the guys.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah well, Ross just made plans for the whole century.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ya know what, I think I'm gonna go to my room and read Cosmo, maybe there's something helpful in there. Know what, at least maybe I can learn how to do an at home bikini wax with leftover Cristmas candles.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [enters] Ok, what the hell happened back there?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I don't know, you tell me. One minute I'm holding Ben like a football, the next thing I know, I've got two kids, I'm living in Scarsdale complaining about the taxes.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well I'm sorry, I think about stuff. Ya know, I mean, you're at work, you're assembling bones, your mind wanders.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ross, you have planned out the next 20 years of our lives, we've been dating for six weeks.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: C'mon, what, you never think about our future?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yes, but I, I think about who's apartment we're gonna sleep at tomorrow night and, and where we're gonna have dinner next Saturday night. I do not think about what our childrens' names are gonna be. You know what our childrens names are gonna be.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, no, I mean, ya know, I, I read a book and there was a girl named Emily and I thought, I thought that might be good.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What was the book?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: The big book of childrens' names.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, Ross, Ross, ok listen, what we have is amazing.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: But I do not want to have everything decided for me. I spent my whole life like that. It's what I had with Barry, that was one of the reasons I left. I, I like not knowing right now and I'm sorry if that scares you but if you want to be with me you are gonna have to deal with that.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok fine.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: We're not done.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I didn't know that.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, then you're gonna have to understand that you're with a guy who's not gonna stop planning his future with you because he knows that we're gonna end up together and if that scares you, tough, 'cause you're gonna have to deal with that.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Fine, I will.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Good, 'cause I love you.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well I love you too.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well that's the first time we've said that.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yes it is.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, I'm gonna kiss you.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well you better.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Richard are there. Phoebe enters.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica is in the kitchen

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey. Oh thanks for the great movie tip.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Did you like it?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh yeah. You know, I don't know if I was happier when um George Bailey destroyed the family business or um, Donna Reid cried, or when the mean pharmacist made his ear bleed.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright, I'll give you the ear thing but don't you think the ending was pretty wonderful?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I didn't watch the ending, I was too depressed. It just kept getting worse and worse, it should have been called, "It's a sucky life and just when you think it can't suck any more it does."

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica is in the kitchen, Phoebe enters.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler and Joey are playing Richard at foosball

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Kick save and... denied.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: But... he gets it back, pass to the middle, lines it up and... BAM! Yes! Could that shot BE any prettier?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Man you are incredible.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Well, we had a table in college.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh really, I didn't know they had foosball in the 1800's.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Nice moustache by the way. When puberty hits that thing's really gonna kick in.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [enters] Honey. Uh, not to sound too Florence Henderson but, dinner's on the table.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Ok, just one more point.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [grabs the other two bars on Richard's side and scores] Score! Now can we go?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: See, that's why we don't let her play.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Is everything all right?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Uh-oh.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Oh hey listen, don't be mad at him, it's our fault. I'm sorry we've been hoggin so much of his time.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, he's just really great to hang around with.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Well.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: No no, seriously, Chandler and I were just talkin about this. He is so much cooler than our dads. [Chandler starts kicking him below the table] I mean, you know, our dad's are ok, ya know, but Richard is just- ow, ow. What are you kickin me for? Huh? I'm tryin to talk here.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Uh, you guys see me as a dad?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Nooooo.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Your just, your just clearly not familiar with our young persons vernacular. See, when we say dad, we mean buddy. We mean pal.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Uh-huh, yeah.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No no, seriously, Joey's my dad, Monica's my dad. I've even got some dads down at work.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: That's fine. Well, your other dad and I are gonna go have a romantic evening and I guess I'll just see you kids around.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Nighty-nite.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You're not a dad. You're not a dad.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Not a dad.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I can't believe you got us into trouble. [slaps Joey on the arm. Joey takes exception and slaps him back]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [her and Richard return to her place] So are you ok?

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Yeah, just, I feel like I'm about a hundred. I thought I was just one of the guys.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Come here. I'll make you feel like one of the guys. You know for a really cool guy, you suck at foosball.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: What're you talkin' about, I was killin' 'em.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Yeah, well they suck too.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Chandler and Joey are playing Richard at foosball.

scenes.location: Ross's apartment. Rachel is changing Ben's diaper under Ross's supervision

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, and then you take the poopie diaper and you put it in the poopie diaper pail.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok Ross, just so you know, calling it a poopie diaper doesn't make this process any cuter.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [doorbell buzzes] Hello.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: It's us.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Come on up. I'm gonna get the rest of his stuff together. [walks in his room]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, we can do this now, can't we Ben? Yes we can, yes we can. [finishes the diaper] There. I did it. I did it. Look at that, oh, stays on and everything. Hi.

lines.speaker: BEN
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I'm sorry, what did you just say? Did you just say hi? Oh my God, Ross, Ross, Ben just said 'Hi'.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Wha, what?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ben just said hi.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What, the word hi?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ye-, no, my Uncle Hi.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Great, great, and I miss that too, I miss everything.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry, I guess I just bring it out in him.

lines.speaker: CAROL and SUSAN
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Guess what. Ben just said his first word.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: What did he say?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Something about hi.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: That's so exciting.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Mommy is so proud of you. Hi. Hi.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You know, actually it's more like, hi.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: Ok, this could go on for a while.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: We've got a cab waiting downstairs.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, this was fun. Uh, we should really do it again sometime, wha'dya say? Ok. Alright so I've got him.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: Tuesday.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Tuesday right. Ok, bye you guys.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Take care.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Bye Ben.

lines.speaker: BEN
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Did, did he just, did he, did he just say, he said bye. He said bye. You said, you said bye to me. You said bye to me.

lines.speaker: SUSAN
lines.text: Suddenly I'm seeing him go off to college.

lines.speaker: CAROL
lines.text: We've gotta go, we've got that cab waiting.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Alright, alright, ok. Bye.

lines.speaker: BEN
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: BEN
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: BEN
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Bye.

scenes.scene_description: Ross's apartment. Rachel is changing Ben's diaper under Ross's supervision.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Phoebe is watching Bert and Ernie with Ben

lines.speaker: ERNIE
lines.text: Oh wow, look at this nice deep hole I've been digging. Hey Bert, isn't this a nice hole here. Hey.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [pauses the tape] Ok, Ben, this is the part where Ernie buries Bert in the sand and can't find him. Now, I've looked ahead on the tape and he does find him again. But, ok, before that happens, there's some pretty rough goin' for a while but I think we can handle it. And, there's just the alphabet but we know that ends well so. Ok, here we go. [starts the tape again]

lines.speaker: ERNIE
lines.text: Bert, Bert. Bert. Hey, what happened to my friend Bert? He was here just a moment ago. Oh no, my old friend Bert is lost.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, I'm so glad you're here.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Phoebe is watching Bert and Ernie with Ben.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Phoebe enters to see Monica
scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Phoebe enters to see Monica, Richard, Ross, and Rachel tearfully watching TV.

title: The One With the Two Bullies

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Everyone is there. Monica is watching stock prices on a business channel

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey Monica, why are we watchin' the business channel?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: 'Cause I was going by it the other day and I saw that there was a stock with my initials, MEG, on it and, well, sometimes I have to watch for two or three hours before it comes up again but when it does, it's pretty exciting.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok honey, you really need a job.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Mon, speaking of which, dad says he knows someone you can call for an interview.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Really.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, right there. That, that's the third sign today. Right there.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: On behalf of everyone, I'd just like to say behuh.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No, 'cause you just said dad and everywhere I go today I keep getting signs telling me to go see my father. Like when I was walking over here and I passed a buffet...which is my father's last name.

lines.speaker: EVERYONE
lines.text: Ahh.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: And they were serving franks which is his first name minus the s at the end. And there was a rotisserie with spinning chicken.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: His indian name?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No because I chickened out the last time when I tried to meet him. So I mean coincidences? I don't think so.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Freakish.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Freaky.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Weird, weird.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, so uh, who wants the last hamburger?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, alright, that's it, now I have to go see him.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hamburger. McDonald's. Old MacDonald had a farm, my dad is a pharmacist.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Everyone is there. Monica is watching stock prices on a business channel.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Chandler and Ross enter in sweats carrying rackets

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Man, I am so beat.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey, you just wanna forget about raquetball and hang out here?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah alright.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: [walks back from the counter] Hey you're in our seats.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, sorry we didn't know.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: [walks back from the counter] Hey, we were sitting there.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok, there is one more way to say it, who knows it?

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Is that supposed to be funny?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No actually, I was just going for colorful.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: What's with this guy?

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: What's with you?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Uh, nothing, nothing's with him. Enjoy your coffee.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: What just happened?

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: I just took your hat. See, I can be funny too. My, my joke is that I, I took your hat.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: That, that is funny. Can I have it back?

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, ok, you know what? I think you're very funny. Kudos on that hat joke. But, come on guy just, just give him back the hat.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Why should we?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Stop talking, stop talking now. Let me just get this straight. You're actually stealing my hat?

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: You got a problem with that?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No, just wanna make sure we're on the same page.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Chandler and Ross enter in sweats carrying rackets.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Joey and Rachel are sitting there and Monica walks in

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey, how'd the interview go?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: It bit. It was a 50's theme restraunt. I have to cook in a costume and dance on the counter.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: So don't do it.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: How can I not do it? I have $127 in the bank.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Monica, relax, go get a beer.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I don't want a beer.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Who said it was for you?

lines.speaker: ROSS and CHANDLER
lines.text: [both enter looking down] Hhhiiii.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What's the matter with you?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: The mean guys at the coffee house took my hat.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Noo.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: You're kiddin'.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: It was ridiculous. Ya know, these guys, they were bullies, actual bullies, ya know.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oohhh.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hi. [Ross turns to Rachel and they hug]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ohhh [turns as if to hug someone] Oh no, wait a minute, I have no one.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, woah, let's go down there and get your hat back.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Na, forget it, it's probably stripped and sold for parts by now.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [seeing TV] Hey, I went up.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: My stock, MEG, it went up 2 points. Hey guys, do you realize that if I had invested my $127 in myself yesterday that I'd like have...a lot more than that today. Ya know what, I'm gonna do it.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Do what?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Put all my money in me.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Monica, what are you talking about? You don't know the first thing about the stock market.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: What's to know? Buy sell, high low, bears bulls...[on the phone] Yes Manhattan...yeah telephone number of the stock...selling store.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Joey and Rachel are sitting there and Monica walks in.

scenes.location: Phoebe's dad's house. Phoebe pulls up in the cab with Rachel and Joey in the back

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: See, didn't I tell ya these pillows would be a good idea?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh God, here we go. For the first time in my life I'm gonna say 'Hi birthfather'.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: We love you, we're here for you.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah good luck, good luck.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Thanks. [gets out of the cab]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey Rach, you uh, you want some sandwich?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ohh, what is in that?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Olive loaf and ham spread, no mayo.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No no, 'cause mayo, that would make it gross.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [a little dog starts attacking her leg] Hey, hey, no, oh oh.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Run Phoebe run.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No no no, doggie please. Oh, I do so wanna love all animals, please no.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Get him a bone, get a bone. You gotta bone?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Are you kidding me?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Look kibbles, bits. Oh God, alright, get the hell off my leg you yippity piece of crap. [Flings the dog off and jumps in the cab. The dog keeps jumping up to the window.] Ok, alright, we have a problem.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well why don't you just reach out and take his trampoline.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, here, I know what we can do. [grabs Joey's sadwich and throws it out the window]

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, hey, hey no.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, doggie get the- aahhh. Ok go get the sandwich, get the sandwich doggie. [dog ignores the sandwich] Good doggie get the sandwich, get the...ok, Joey, the dog will lick himself but he will not touch your sandwich, what does that say?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well if he's not gonna eat it, I will.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Are you crazy?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Phoebs, he's just a little dog. [turns back to the car window and the dog is halfway through it.] Ahhh.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe's dad's house. Phoebe pulls up in the cab with Rachel and Joey in the back.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Chandler and Ross are sitting at the couch

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
