lines.text: Do you have to be a Century 21 real-estate agent to get to wear those really cool jackets?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Do you say this stuff to girls?

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Hehehehey, isn't that the guy who used to wear your hat?

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: And look where they're sitting.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You're joking, right? You guys just walked through the door.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Maybe we didn't make it clear enough.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: This couch belongs to us.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Alright, I'll tell you what, you call the couch and then, and then we'll call the couch, and we'll see who it comes to.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: You know what I keep wondering? Why you two are still sitting here.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Alright, that's it. I've had enough of this, alright. Gunther, these guys are trying to take our seat.

lines.speaker: GUNTHER
lines.text: Fellas, these guys were here first.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Oh, sorry, I didn't realize.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: GUNTHER
lines.text: There you go.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Thank you Gunther. We didn't want to have to go and do that.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: He told on us?

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: You told on us?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well pal, you didn't give me much of a choice. [flicks the ends of the big bully's tie]

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Don't play with his things.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Alright, let's take this outside.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Let's, let's take this outside? Who talks like that?

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: The guy that's about to kick your ass talks like that.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You had to ask.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, ok look, see, the thing is we're, we're not gonna fight you guys.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Well then here's the deal, you won't have to so long as never ever show your faces in this coffee house ever again.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I think you played the Gunther card too soon.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Chandler and Ross are sitting at the couch.

scenes.location: Back in the cab in front of Phoebe's dad's house

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey Phoeb's, I think you're good to go.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, I don't know.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I just think that this was a really bad sign, ya know. I mean, like the beast at the threshold, you know. It's just like, I have no family left, ya know. I mean except for my grandmother, you know, but let's face it, she's not gonna be around forever, despite what she says. And I have a sister who I've barely spoken to since we like shared a womb. I don't know, this is my real father and I just, I want things to be like just right.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah Phoebe, I completely understand.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, whatever you need. Hey, you wanna go home?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, thanks. Sorry, again

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [innocently] What was that?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uhh, I'm guessing the threshold's clear now.

scenes.scene_description: Back in the cab in front of Phoebe's dad's house.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Joey is eating breakfast

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I wanna buy 5 shares of SGJ and

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Time is money my friend?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, you missed, 'Takes money to make money,' and uh, 'Don't make me come down there and kick your wall street butt.'

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey, I made $17 before breakfast, what have you done?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well uh, I had breakfast here so technically I saved $3.50.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: How did you make $17.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, my financially challenged friends,

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: How come those?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, CHP because I used to have a crush on Eric Estrada. And ZXY becuase I think it sounds zexy.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What happened to uh, MEG.?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: MEG was good for me but I dumped her. Ya know, my motto is get out before they go down.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: That is so not my motto.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [enters] Hey.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Hey Phoebs. Oh hey, how's the dog?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, I talked to the vet, people are so nice upstate. Anyway, he said that the little fella's gonna be ok and I can pick him up tomorrow.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, thank God.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, but he did have to have a bunch of stitches and he said that only once in a blue moon does a dog's ear grow back so...still hoping.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, so Phoebe, now are you gonna call your dad and let him know that his dog is ok?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I, I don't wanna meet my father over the phone.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey Phoebs, if you want, I'll do it.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok. Listen, just don't say anything about me, ok. [goes over and grabs the phone that's sitting by Monica]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: She'll be a much better friend when the market closes.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [dials the phone] It's a woman.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: So talk to her.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [in a fake voice] Uhh, hello Miss Buffay. I know where your dog is. I want you to know that he'll be returned to you, almost as good as new, within, within 24 hours. Uh, goodbye. [hangs up]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Why the voice.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [in the voice] Hard to say.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Joey is eating breakfast, Rachel has just gotten up, and Monica is on the phone.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Ross is sitting at the bar

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Your cappucino sir.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ya know I think this is much better than the coffee house.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Absolutely.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: How come it's not mixing with the water?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well the package says you have to uh, constantly keep it moving. Stir and drink, stir and drink, never let it settle.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [walks out of his room] Hey, this is ridiculous. I'll tell you what. After I get back from my neice's christening, I'll go down to the coffee house with you and we'll all have a nice cup of coffee alright. No problem, Joey's there.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No. Man I don't wanna have to have Joey with me every time I wanna descent cup of coffee. Ya know, and I don't wanna spend the rest of my life drinking cappucino with a 'K'. I say you and I go back down there and stand up to those guys.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Alright, hang on a second there Custer.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah really, Ross, have you ever been beaten up before?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, sure.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: By someone besides Monica?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No. So what. So what if we get beaten up, maybe that's just something every man has to go through once in his life. Ya know, like a, like a right of passage or somethin'.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, couldn't we just lose our virginities again? Ya know, because I think actually mine's growing back.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's apartment. Ross is sitting at the bar, Chandler serves up two mugs of hot water.

scenes.location: Outside Monica and Rachel's apartment. Rachel is returning

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [Opens the door] I need to borrow a hundred bucks.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hi, welcome home. [pulls Rachel inside] I need to borrow a hundred bucks.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: For what?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I've gotta get back in the game.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Why, when did you get out of the game?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I don't know, I lost it all ok. I lost it.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Hey, I've come to terms with it, you have to too.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok. Look uhh, Mon I'm, I'm really sorry.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah, where are we on the hundred bucks?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I, I don't have it.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: But I need it. Otherwords I'm gonna have to take that horrible diner job. You know, with the dancing and the costumes. I don't wanna have to wear flame retardant boobs.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Nobody does honey.

scenes.scene_description: Outside Monica and Rachel's apartment. Rachel is returning.

scenes.location: Phoebe's dad's house. Phoebe is returning the dog who is bandaged up and has a plastic cone around it's neck

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: MRS BUFFAY
lines.text: Schnoodle. Oh my God, what the hell happened to my dog?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: It was an accident, and, and the woman who did this would never ever hurt a dog on purpose. She's a vegetarian.

lines.speaker: MRS BUFFAY
lines.text: What are these, stitches?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, eight of them. That's 56 to him. You know also, if, if it's raining, you can't let him look up too long 'cause that cone'll fill up really really fast.

lines.speaker: MRS BUFFAY
lines.text: Yeah well, thanks for bringing back what's left of him.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Sure, oh, is, is Frank home.

lines.speaker: MRS BUFFAY
lines.text: How do you know Frank?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Just from a, from a long time ago. Is he here?

lines.speaker: MRS BUFFAY
lines.text: Yeah. Frank.

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Yeah. What? [a young guy comes around the corner]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh, ok, um, I mean Frank senior.

lines.speaker: MRS BUFFAY
lines.text: He went out for groceries.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok so will he be back soon?

lines.speaker: MRS BUFFAY
lines.text: Well he left four years ago so we're expecting him back any minute now.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Alright, I'm, I'm gonna go. I'm sorry about the dog, everything. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Hey lady. Hey wait up. How do you know my dad?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Um well I don't really. Just genetically. He's kinda my dad too.

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Heavy.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah. So um, did he ever talk about me, Phoebe?

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: No but he didn't really talk about anything.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Except stilts.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Stilts?

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Yeah, he loved stilts. One time I was upstairs, I was stealing cigarettes out of my mom's purse, and uh, all of a sudden I look over and there's my dad's head bobbing past the window. He just had this big smile on his face and he was waving 'cause he was always happiest when he was on his stilts.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I don't know what to do with that.

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Me neither. So you're like my big sister.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: This is huge, you can buy me beer.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I'm not gonna. But you know what's cool though? Ok, if you had a friend named Pete, then I could say, 'Oh yeah, I know Pete, he's friends with my brother.'

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: I gotta friend named Mark.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: That'll work too.

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Cool, alright. So maybe, ya know, I could give you a call sometime, we could talk or somethin'.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, that'd be ok.

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, I'm in the book.

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Ok, yeah.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Alright. So um, stilts huh?

lines.speaker: FRANK
lines.text: Yeah hey, you know if you want I can take you around back and show you where he hit his head on the rain gutter.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe's dad's house. Phoebe is returning the dog who is bandaged up and has a plastic cone around it's neck.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Chandler and Ross are sitting on the couch nervously

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well we did it, we're here. We are standing our ground. How long does a cup of coffee take?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Would you come on! Come on! [waitress brings their coffee] Thank you.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ah, there we go.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I think we proved our point.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: You burn your mouth?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Cannot feel my tounge.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Bullies, big bullies.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Oh, look who's here, it's the weenies.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Did we not make ourselves clear the other day.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yes, and that's why we're here.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yes, we're standing out ground...apparently.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Let's do this alright.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Woah, ho-ho, whad'ya got there, a weapon?

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: It's a nice watch, I don't wanna break it on your ribs.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Alright, let's do this.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Question. If I don't care about my watch, can I use it as a weapon?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Whad'ya mean?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, it's sharp, it's metal, I think I can do some, you know, serious damage with it.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: No, you can't use your watch.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok. [reaches in his pocket]

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Or your keys.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Look, here's what we'll do. We'll put all keys and watches in the hat over there. Alright. [they all put their keys and watches in the hat and put it on a mail box] Alright, c'mon man, let's do this.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Before I forget, are we hitting faces?

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Of course we're hitting faces, why wouldn't you hit faces?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well because I have to work on Monday, I have a big presentation.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Actually, you know, uh, I gotta show this apartment tomorrow and uh, you know, this no faces thing might not be a bad idea.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Ok, nothing from the neck up. [everyone gets ready for the fight] Or the waist down. Dana's ovulating.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Oh really, you guys tryin' again?

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok, so let me just get this straight. So we're uh, strictly talking about the middle?

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: C'MON!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey, hey, woah, you want some of this, huh? You want a piece of this, huh? I'm standin here, huh.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey, hey, those guys are takin our stuff! [some guy runs off with the hat]

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Hey.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Chandler and Ross are sitting on the couch nervously.

scenes.location: Central Perk. The four guys are returning after getting the hat back

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: God, that was, that was amazing, that was incredible. You guys, you guys kicked butt.

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: Us, what about you guys? Man you really, bing, gave it to old Mr. Clean back there. He was a big guy.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah he was wasn't he.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, I wouldn't know having missed everything.

lines.speaker: BIG BULLY
lines.text: Don't do that to yourself. Any one of us could have tripped over that little girls jump-rope.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: So, listen guys, are we uh, are we ok here?

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: We're ok.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok so, can I have my hat back?

lines.speaker: LITTLE BULLY
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Huh. [reaches over and grabs the hat and bolts for the door but slips and falls behind the couch]

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. The four guys are returning after getting the hat back.

scenes.location: The 50's theme cafe. Monica is working the grill

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Look at her.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hi Monica.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: He-he-he, how's it goin'?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey nice boobs.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Guys guys, check this out.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Excellent.

scenes.scene_description: The 50's theme cafe. Monica is working the grill, the rest are at a table.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Everyone is there. Monica is watching stock prices on a business channel
scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Everyone is there. Monica is watching stock prices on a business channel.

title: The One With the Two Parties

scenes.location: Moondance Diner. Ross

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So, I'll get candles and my mom's lace tablecloth, and since it's Rachel's birthday, I mean, we want it to be special, I thought I'd poach a salmon.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Ohhh.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Question. Why do we always have to have parties where you poach things?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You wanna be in charge of the food committee?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Question two. Why do we always have to have parties with committees?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Really. Why can't we just get some pizzas and get some beers and have fun?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, I agree. Ya know, I think fancy parties are only fun if you're fancy on the inside and I'm just not sure we are.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright. If you guys don't want it to be special, fine. You can throw any kind of party you want.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Joey they're not real. I start miles beneath the surface of these things, ok, they're fake. See [squeezes her breast] honk honk.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Wow, it's, it's like porno for clowns.

scenes.scene_description: Moondance Diner. Ross, Phoebe, Joey, and Chandler are sitting at the counter, Monica is working. Monica is wearing her costume, including big fake breasts.

scenes.location: Central Perk. Chandler

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I talked to Rachel's sisters, neither of them can come.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, um so, I still have to invite Dillon and Emma and Shannon Cooper.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Woah, woah, woah, uh, no Shannon Cooper.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Why not her?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Cause she uh, she steals stuff.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Or maybe she doesn't steal stuff and Joey just slept with her and never called her back.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Joey that is horriable.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey I liked her, alright. Maybe, maybe too much. I don't know I guess I just got scared.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I'm sorry, I didn't know.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I didn't think anyone'd buy that, ok.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi honey, how did it go?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Agh, it was the graduation from hell.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ya know, my cousin went to hell on a football scholarship.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ya know, I mean this is supposed to be a joyous occasion. My sister's graduating from college, nobody thought she would. It's a true testament to what a girl from long island would do for a Celica.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So what happened?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: My parents happened. All they had to do was sit in the same stadium, smile proudly, and not talk about the divorce. But nooo, they got into a huge fight in the middle of the commencement address. Bishop Tutu actually had to stop and shush them. But you know what, you know what the good news is? I get to serve coffee for the next 8 hours.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, so I guess we don't invite her parents.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Well, how bout just her mom?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Why her mom?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Cause I already invited her.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ooh, ooh, did you ask Stacy Roth?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh no, can't invite her. She also steals.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Chandler, Ross, Joey, Phoebe, and Monica are planning Rache's birthday party.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Chandler

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, here are the birthday candles. Where's the birthday cake?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, we're not having birthday cake, we're having birthday flan.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: It's a traditional Mexican custard dessert.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh that's nice. Happy birthday Rachel, here's some goo.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [answers the door] Dr. Greene. Oh my God it's Rachel's dad. What're you doing here?

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: What? The father can't drop by to see the daughter on her birthday?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No no, the father can, but um, since I am the roommate I can tell you that she's not here and I'll pass along the message, ok. So bye-bye.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Ohhh, you're having a parteee.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No, no, not a party. Just a surprise gathering of some people Rachel knows. Um, this is Phoebe and Chandler and Joey.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: I'll never remember all of that. So uh, what's the deal? Rachel comes home, people pop out and yell stuff, is that it?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: This isn't your first surprise party, is it sir?

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Hi Monica.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Chinese menu guy. Forgot the menus.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: So, basically just a Chinese guy.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uh, hey, Dr. Greene, why don't you come with me, we'll put your jacket on Rachel's bed.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Alright, that sounds like a two person job. [they walk into Rachel's bedroom]

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Well, my goodness, what was that?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Sandra, I am so sorry, I thought you were Rachel and we just weren't ready for you yet.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: You thought I was Rachel?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yes because uh, you look so young.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: And because you're both, you know, white women.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Oh, I missed you kids. Well, should I put my coat in the bedroom?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: NO! No, I'll take that for ya.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Oh well thank you. Such a gentleman. Thank you. [Chandler takes the hot pink coat and grimaces at it] Ahh, it all looks so nice, so festive, all the balloons... [Chandler, remembering that Joey and Mr. Greene are in the bedroom, throws her coat in a cupboard] The funniest thing happened to me on the way here. I was...[Joey peeks out]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [cutting Mrs. Greene off] Ha-ha, that's great, ha-ha. I can't wait to hear the rest of it, ya know, but I really have to go to the bathroom so... Hey, come with me. Yeah, yeah, it'll be like we're gal pals, ya know, like at a restraunt. Oh, it'll be fun, c'mon. [they go in the bathroom]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok, think, what would Jack and Chrissy do?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [peeks back out] Ok, now that your coat is safely in the bedr-, [sees that the coast is clear] oh, ok we can come back out in the living room.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: So uh, Joey and Chanlder, I, I think it's time that you take Dr. Greene over to your place.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Uhh, yes, absdolutely, um. Why again?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Because that's where the party is you goon. See this is just the staging area.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Right this is staging.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, this more than anything else, is the staging area.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: [as they're walking out, Dr. Greene questioningly gestures at the Happy Birthday sign over the door] This is clearly in the wrong apartment. [they all walk across the hall]

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Chandler, Joey, Monica, and Phoebe are setting up for the party.

scenes.location: Later on in the hallway between the apartments. Chandler is showing people to the parties

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Alright you guys are off to party number one [ushers 3 guys into Monica's apartment] and you, you are off to party number two [ushers four women into his apartment. Two guys try to follow and Chandler blocks them and shoos them off to Monica's apartment] Alright fellas, let's keep it movin', let' keep it movin.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Chandler could you at least send some women to my party? [buzzer goes off] Alright that's Ross.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok, they're coming, shhh. [Runs into Monica's apartment and grabs one last girl to take to his apartment]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ohh, thank you for the wonderful dinner.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Thanks for being born.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ohh, thank you for my beautiul earrings, they're perfect. I love you.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, now you can exchange them if you want, ok.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Now I love you even more.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Surprise.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh my gosh, wow. Monica. Oh my god. Mom. This is so great.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Happy birthday sweetie.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Wow you, you. I had no idea.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: No, I knew.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, everybody, there's food and drinks on the table. Go across the hall.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Right now, Joey and Chandler's, go now.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Why.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Just go.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Surprise.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Happy birthday sweetpea.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Daddy.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Both of them are here, both of them, both of them are here?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, we could count again.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I can't believe this is happening.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You know what, this is ridiculous, ok. This is your birthday, this is your party. I say we just put 'em all together and if they can't deal with it, who cares.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: That's who.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Look, are you gonna be ok?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well, I have to be, I don't really have a choice, I mean, you know, I could look at the bright side, I get two birthday parties and two birthday cakes.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, actually just one birthday flan.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: It's a traditional Mexican custard dessert...Look talk to Monica, she's on the food committee.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Joey, Joey. Hey, some girl just walked up to me and said, 'I want you Dennis,' and stuck her tounge down my throat. I love this party.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Quick volleyball question.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Volleyball.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, we set up a court in your room. Uh, you didn't really like that grey lamp, did you?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Joey, a woman just stuck her tounge down my throat, I'm not even listening to you.

lines.speaker: GIRL'S VOICE
lines.text: Dennis.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok, that's me. [runs back]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Listen honey, can you keep dad occupied, I'm gonna go talk to mom for a while.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ok, do you have any ideas for any openers?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Uhh, let's just stay clear of 'I'm the guy that's doing you daughter' and you should be ok.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok people, I want you to take a piece of paper, here you go, and write down your most embarassing memory. Oh, and I do ask that when you're not using the markers, you put the caps back on them because they will dry out.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi Dr. Greene. So, uh, how's everything in the uh, vascular surgery....game?

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: It's not a game Ross, a woman died on my table today.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I'm sorry. See that's the good thing about my job. All the dinosaurs on my table are already dead.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Listen you guys, I don't mean to be a pain about this but, um, I've noticed that some of you are just placing them on. You wanna push the caps until you hear them click. [she demonstrates, Gunther starts to walk to the door] Gunther, where're you going?

lines.speaker: GUNTHER
lines.text: I um, was sorta thinking about maybe...

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: No. No you can't go. No this is fun. Come on we're just getting started. Here, here's your marker.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Listen if you wanna go, just go.

lines.speaker: GUNTER
lines.text: No, she'll yell at me again.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Alright, I can get you out.

lines.speaker: GUNTHER
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Shh. In a minute, I'm gonna create a diversion. When I do, walk quickly to the door and don't look back.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: I think I need a drink.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, here, I, I'll get it for ya. Whad'ya want?

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Scotch.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Scotch. Alright, I'll be back in 10 seconds with your scotch on the rocks in a glass.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Neat.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Cool.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: No no no, no no no, neat, as in no rocks.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Oh hello Ross, where have you been?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Hi. Uh, I have been in the bathroom. Stay clear of the salmon mousse.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Oh, scotch neat. Ya know, that's Rachel's father's drink.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Oh, mine too. Isn't that neat, scotch neat. Would you excuse me? [walks out in the hallway, Mr. Greene is walking out of Chandler and Joey's apartment] Hey, hey, where you uh, sneakin off to mister?

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: I'm getting my cigarettes out of my jacket.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No. no.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Whad'ya mean no?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: No, um, see 'cause that, that is, that is the staging area. If you go in there, it'll ruin the whole illusion of the party. Yeah, I think you take your scotch back in there and I will get your cigarettes for you sir.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Get my glasses too.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: All righty roo. [closes the door] What a great moment to say that for the first time. [goes to get the cigarettes and glasses]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, the first person's most embarassing memory is, 'Monica, your party sucks.' Very funny.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh no, ooh, ooh, did somebody forget to use a coaster?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: What? [she runs over to where Phoebe is, Phoebe signals for Gunther to go] I don't see anything.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Great, I'm seeing water rings again.

lines.speaker: MRS. GELLER
lines.text: Ross, whose glasses are those?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Mine.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: You wear bi-focals?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Um-hmm. [puts them on] I have a condition, apparently, that I require two different sets of focals.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Did you know my husband has glasses just like that?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well those are very popular frames.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Neil Sedaka wears them.

lines.speaker: GUY
lines.text: [to Phoebe] I hear you can get people out of here.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Rachel, you didn't tell me your boyfriend smoked.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah, like a chimney.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Ohh, big smoker. [Packs the cigarettes and flings one on Mrs. Greene in the process. Finally gets one in his mouth and it look really out of place] Big big smoker. In fact I'm gonna go ou into the hallway and fire up this bad boy. [as he walks into the hall, he comes face to face with Mr. Greene]

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Are you wearing my glasses?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yes. [pulls them off and hands them to Mr. Greene] I was just warming up the earpieces for you.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Thank you. Is that one of my cigarettes?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [pulls the cigarette off his upper lip and hands it to Mr. Greene] Yeah, yes it is, I was just moistening the tip.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, ok, she's taking the trash out so I can get you out of here but it has to be now, she'll be back any minute.

lines.speaker: GIRL 1
lines.text: What about my friend Victor?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No, only the three of you, any more than that and she'll get suspicious.

lines.speaker: GIRL 1
lines.text: Alright, let me just get my coat.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: There isn't time. You must leave everything. They'll take care of you next door.

lines.speaker: GIRL 1
lines.text: Is it true they have beer?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Everything you've heard is true.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Could you guys please try to keep it down, we're trying to start a Boggle tournament.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You, and you, you're supposed to be at my party. And Gunther! What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: GUNTHER
lines.text: Um [gestures to dance floor]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [enters with the three people she got out] Ok, welcome to the fu-oh.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Phoebe.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Alright, I'm sorry but these people needed me. They work hard all week, it's Saturday night, they deserve to have a little fun. Go.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ya know, my party is fun. I mean, maybe it's a little quieter, less obvious sorta fun but, you know, if people would just give it a chance... [volleyball hits her in the head from behind]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You want me to see a therapist?

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Sweetheart, you obviously have a problem. You've chosen a boyfriend exactly like your father.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok mom, you know what, fine, I'll make an appointment ok, but you know what, right now, I gotta go, I gotta go do a thing.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Did you know your mother spent $1200 dollars on bansai trees. I felt like Gulliver around that place.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Daddy, daddy, you know what, I really wanna hear more about this, I really do, but I just have, I just have to do a, some stuff.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: You work and you work and you work at a marriage but all he cares about is his stupid boat.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: You work and you work and you work on a boat...

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: He always ridiculed my pottery classs...

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: ...and you sand it and you varnish it...

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: ...but when all is said and done, he still drinks out of the mugs.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: ...and her yoga and her Bridges of Madison County...

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: ...the scotch and the cigarettes...

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: ...and the bansai's and the chiuaua...

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: ...I may have only been in therapy for three weeks now dear but...

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: ...what the hell does she want with half a boat...

scenes.scene_description: Later on in the hallway between the apartments. Chandler is showing people to the parties.

scenes.location: The hallway after the party. Rachel is sitting there

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: [running out of his apartment after a girl] Ok, ok, you can be shirts and I'll be skins. I'll be skins. [sits down beside Rachel] Hey, how you holdin' up there, tiger? Oh, sorry, when my parents were getting divorced I got a lot of tigers. Got a lot of champs, chiefs, sports, I even got a governor.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: This is it, isn't it? I mean, this is what my life is gonna be like. My mom there, my dad there. Thanksgiving, Christmas. She gets the house, he's in some condo my sister's gonna decorate with wicker. Oh, Chandler how did you get through this?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, I relied on a carefully regimented program of denial and, and wetting the bed.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ya know, I just, so weird. I mean I was in there just listening to them bitch about each other and all I kept thinking about was the fourth of July.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Becasue it reminded you of the way our forefathers used to bitch at each other?

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: It's just this thing. Every year we would go out on my dad's boat and watch the fireworks. Mom always hated it because the ocean air made her hair all big. My sister Jill would be throwing up over the side and my dad would be upset becasue nobody was helping and then when we did help he would scream at us for doing it wrong. But then when the fireworks started, everybody just shut up, you know, and it'd get really cold, and we would all just sort of smush under this one blanket. It never occured to anybody to bring another one. And now it's just...

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I, I know. [Hugs her. Ross walks out and Chandler puts her in his arms.]

scenes.scene_description: The hallway after the party. Rachel is sitting there.

scenes.location: Monica's party. She is seeing off the last of the guests

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, thanks for coming, I hope you guys had fun.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Alright, Monica dear, I'm gonna hit the road. Now I've left my 10 verbs on the table. And you be sure and send me that finished poem.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok will do. So glad you came.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: I think I saw Rachel out in the hall.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, let me go check. Your mom want's to say goodbye.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh ok.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Happy birthday sweetie.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ahh, you drive safe.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Ross, what're you doing.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I'm getting ready for the water skiing. [Mr. Greene opens the door which pulls Ross in] How are you doing?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, uh, Dr. Greene, where are you going?

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: To get my coat.

lines.speaker: GUYS
lines.text: No no no.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: Alright, alright, I can get my own coat.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Sorry, we're on a major flan high.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh no, you're not supposed to be here. This is the staging area, you should, it's all wrong, you should leave, ya know, get out. [opens the door, the guys are right there] Or perhaps you'd like a creme d'menthe.

lines.speaker: MR. GREENE
lines.text: I have to be heading to my chateau, thank you.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh all right, then I guess we're going back into the hallway.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Thanks for coming Mrs. Greene. [grabs her and kisses her to distract her. She goes limp in his arms. Mr. Greene leaves.] Well, ok, you take care.

lines.speaker: MRS. GREENE
lines.text: Oh, you kids [she caresses his face and chest] Well, this is the best party I've been to in years.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Thank you.

scenes.scene_description: Monica's party. She is seeing off the last of the guests.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Close up of the flan on the table with birthday candles

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok everybody, it's time for flan.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yup, get ready for the gelatenous fun.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Kinda looks like that stuff you get when you get a bad infection.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, that's enough.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok Rachel, make a special flan wish.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, I've got one. [blows out the candles. Somebody calls out 'heads up' and the volleyball lands in the flan] Wow, those things almost never come true.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Close up of the flan on the table with birthday candles.

scenes.location: Moondance Diner. Ross
scenes.scene_description: Moondance Diner. Ross, Phoebe, Joey, and Chandler are sitting at the counter, Monica is working. Monica is wearing her costume, including big fake breasts.

title: The One With the Chicken Pox

scenes.location: Central Perk. Rachel

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Ok, Chandler, Mon, there's only one bananna nut muffin left.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Oh, I ordered mine first.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Yeah, but I'm, I'm so much faster...

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Give it to me.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Give it to me.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ok, you can have it. [He licks it and offers it to her.]

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [She grabs the coffee cup on the table and licks the rim.] There you go, enjoy your coffee.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: That was there when I got here. [Takes a bite of his muffin.]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: [enters] Hey you guys, you will never guess who's coming to New York.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: [Chandler tries to come back with a smart-ass remark but can't swallow the muffin.] Quick, Phoebe, tell us before he can swallow.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Oh ok, Ryan, that guy I went out with, who's in the Navy.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You went out with a guy in the Navy?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, I met him when I was playing guitar in Washington Square Park. Ryan threw in salt water taffy 'cause he didn't have any change.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, is that when you wrote salt water taffy man?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No. No, he is my submaring guy. He resurfaces like every couple years and we have the most amazing three days together. Only this time he's coming for two weeks. Two whole weeks, which means yay.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: So wait, this guy goes down for like two years at a time?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: That'll teach you to lick my muffin.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: [enters] Hiii.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh no, what happened?

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Well, I just spoke to Carol. Ben's got the chicken pox.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Oh no.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Yeah, so if you haven't already had it, chances are you're gonna get it.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Well I've had it.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, I've had it.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Had it.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Had it.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Well, I've never had it, I feel so left out. [Sees a red bump on her arm.] Oh look!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk. Rachel, Monica, Joey, and Chandler are there.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Richard are in her bedroom

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Honey, you made the bed again. I told you, you don't have to do that. This isn't camp.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Ooh, then I guess the panty raid last night was totally uncalled for. Ok, I am going to take a shower and today I will be singing Jim Crochee's Leroy Brown.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Monica... [He re-enters the bedroom and Monica jumps on the bed, trying to cover it.] Hey Mon, I have a question. Is Leroy the baddest man in the whole damn town or the fattest man in the whole damn town?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Baddest. Otherwise the song would be Fat Fat Leroy Brown.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: What're you doing?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Just waiting for you sweetie.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Are you remaking the bed?

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm sorry. You know what, the way you did it was just fine.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Then, you're redoing it because...

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: If I tell you, you'll think I'm crazy.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: You're pretty much running that risk either way.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Ok, you see, the tag shouldn't be at the top left corner, it should be at the bottom right corner.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Oh, well that's not so crazy.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: I'm just easing you in.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Oh, alright.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Alright, you see these little flower blossoms? They should be facing up, not down, because, well, the head of the bed is where the sun would be. You don't love me any more do you.

lines.speaker: RICHARD
lines.text: Actually, if it's possible, I love you more.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: Really? Wow, well then come on, I wanna show you how to fold the toilet paper into a point.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Richard are in her bedroom.

scenes.location: Chandler's office. Joey is there

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey, look Joey, I'm just saying if you need something to hold you over, I can get you a job right here as an entry level processor.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: But don't you need experience for a job like that?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: It's not that hard to learn. And as for people realizing you have no idea what you're doing, hey, you're an actor. Act like a processor, people will think you're a processor.

lines.speaker: SCOTT
lines.text: [enters] Hey Chandler, here's this morning's projections.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey thanks. Scott Alexander, Joey Tribbianni. Joey is a uh, fellow processor.

lines.speaker: SCOTT
lines.text: No kidding.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh yeah yeah. I process. People want the processing, I'm the one they call.

lines.speaker: SCOTT
lines.text: Where do you work?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Uhh, well, right now I'm in between things. You know how it is. One day you're processing, the next day you're not so much... processing any more.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: I was just telling Joey about the opening in Fleischman's group.

lines.speaker: SCOTT
lines.text: Fleischman's group. Whatever you do, don't touch his sandwiches. Ha-ha-ha...

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Ha-ha. [Scott leaves] Are all you processors dorks?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler's office. Joey is there.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Rachel are doing Phoebe's makeup

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, this lipstick looks just great on you.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: You look fabulous honey, you really do.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah? Are you sure, really. [She picks up a mirror and sees the white splotches all over her face.]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: You see, you look beautiful. For god sakes, dim the lights.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I, I, I'm hideous.

lines.speaker: MONICA
lines.text: It's gonna be ok. Ryan's been under water. He's just gonna be so glad that you don't have barnicles on your butt.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica and Rachel are doing Phoebe's makeup.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Ryan is walks up to the door and knocks

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Come in.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: Hey baby, I'm back... [Phoebe is sitting by the window in a veil.]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Hey Ryan, what's up?

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: What's goin' on?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Well, no no, you have to stay back. I, I have the pox.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: Chicken or small?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Chicken. Which is so ironic considering I'm a vegetarian.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: Why aren't you at home in bed?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: 'Cause my, my grandmother's never had chicken pox. Please, please tell me you have, 'cause oh my God, I forgot how cute you are.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: I'm sorry, I never had 'em.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ohh, ohh.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: If I had one wish, it would be to build a time machine, go back to when I was 7, when Jimmy Hauser had the chicken pox. I would grab that kid and rub him all over my face.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, or you know, you could just wish that I didn't have them now.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: Can I please see your face?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Nope. You don't want to see a face covered with pox.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: Your face could be covered with lochs, I wouldn't care.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: And you hate fish. Oh. That's so sweet, alright. Ok, alright, you can see. This is me... [she unveils herself right as a huge lightning bolt crashes outside. Ryan screams in terror.] Oh, I am scary.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: Sorry, the lightning. Lightning was an unfortunate incidence. You look lovely, lovely.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: I hate this. 'Cause I tell you, I had the most amazing two weeks planned for us, and almost everything I had in mind, we had to be a lot closer than this.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: Phoebe, I have spent the last eight months in a steel tube with men, thinking about this moment. I am not gonna let a bunch of itchy spots stand between us. [He walks to her and kisses her.]

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Ok, this is the most romantic disease I've ever had.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Ryan is walks up to the door and knocks.

scenes.location: Chandler's office. Joey enters

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Hey, how's the first day goin'?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Pretty good. It's like you said. It's mostly just putting numbers from one column into another column.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well there you go.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey and everbody is so nice. I just had a good talk with that lady with the red hair, Jeannie.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Jeannie, the head of east coast operations Jeannie?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah, turns out our kids go to the same school. Small world huh?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Weird world. Your kids?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: I figure my character has kids.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Ya know there isn't a part of that sentence I don't need explained.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Well, see when you're acting you need to think about stuff like that. My character, Joseph the processor guy, has two little girls, Ashley and Brittany. Ashley copies everything Brittany does.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, invisible kids can be that way sometimes.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah. Joseph and his wife, Karen, are thinking of having a third kid... Ya know what? Just did.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Really? Wow. That's some pretty powerful imaginary sperm you must have there.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler's office. Joey enters.

scenes.location: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Phoebe and Ryan are playing Monopoly

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: You know what makes the itching even worse?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: That you don't stop talking about it.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Let's just play, ok. Good, ok. [She picks up the dice.] Here we go, double sixes, here we go... [She starts to rub the dice all over herself.] Here we go, come to mama, just getting ready to roll the dice...

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: What're you doing? Are you scratching?

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No. This is what I do for luck, ok.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: You're scratching. Give me the dice.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: Give me the dice.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: No. Here. [Throws them on the table.] There. Ooh, double sixes.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: We can't scratch. You know we can't, we'll scar.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Uhh, I can't stop thinking about it. It's just so hard. I just wanna grab all these houses and rub 'em all over my body. [Grabs a handful of the houses.]

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Give it.

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Yeah, come on. You know you want it, you know you want it too, come on. Let's just be bad, it'll feel so good. [She starts scratching him.]

lines.speaker: RYAN
lines.text: Oh God help me.

lines.speaker: PHOEBE
lines.text: Now do me, do my back. Oh come on, harder.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, stop that, stop that right now.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: You know, I might have expected this of you Phoebe, but Ryan, you're a military man.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's apartment. Phoebe and Ryan are playing Monopoly.

scenes.location: Chandler's office building. Joey and Jeannie are talking

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: You and Milton have to join us on the boat. Karen'll pack a lunch, you'll bring the kids, we'll make a day of it.

lines.speaker: JEANNIE
lines.text: Oh, that sounds lovely. We're gonna have to set that up. Oh, I better get back. Hope the baby feels better.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Oh, thanks, thanks. Bye bye Jeannie.

lines.speaker: JEANNIE
lines.text: Bye bye Joey.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: What a phony.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, I'm sure you'll teach her a lesson when she steps off the dock onto nothing. Hey Mr. Douglas.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Sir.

lines.speaker: MR. DOUGLAS
lines.text: Uh, listen Bing, I received your memo. So, we're not gonna receive the systems report until next Friday?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well the people in my group wanna spend the holiday weekend with their families.

lines.speaker: MR. DOUGLAS
lines.text: I have a family, I'm gonna be here.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Yeah Bing, what's that about?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: It's about cutting my people a little slack, ya know, for morale. Look, if you wanna see some rough numbers, I can get them to you by Wednesday.

lines.speaker: MR. DOUGLAS
lines.text: Rough numbers?

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: This company was not built on rough numbers. Am I right Mr. Douglas.

lines.speaker: MR. DOUGLAS
lines.text: Have the final numbers on my desk by Tuesday.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Uh, if you say so sir.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Joseph's good, isn't he?

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Well, I'm going to kill you.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey, hey, I just figure Joseph's the kinda guy that likes to mix it up. Ya know, get in there, ruffle some feathers.

lines.text: Come on.

lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: Hey. Mr. Douglas is looking for you.

lines.text: Why? Wh- wh- why is Mr. Douglas looking for me?

lines.text: Becasue at first he thought it was Joseph. But after he asked Joseph about it, turns out it was you. Anyway, I just thought you should know.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Alright, that's it. Look Joey, I'm sorry, I realize this is the role of a lifetime for ya, and if I could just fire Joseph, I would, but unfortunately that's not possible so I'm gonna have to let both of you go.

lines.text: I don't, I hate Joseph, ok. I think he's a brown-nosing suck up.

lines.text: Oh yeah. Well you can't fire Joseph. You know why, 'cause he's not in your department.

lines.speaker: CHANDLER
lines.text: Alright, ok, alright. So I can't fire Joseph but uh, I can sleep with his wife.

lines.text: Karen.

lines.text: Yeah, Karen. I'm thinking about having an affair with her. Oh, you know what? I just did.

lines.text: Ahh. What the hell are you doing to me man.

lines.text: Oh well it's not me, it's my character, Chandy. Yeah the rogue processor who seduces his co-worker's wives for sport and then laughs about it the next day at the water cooler. In fact, I have her panties right there in my drawer.

lines.text: Really?

lines.text: No freakshow, she's fictional.

lines.speaker: JOEY

lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: JOEY
lines.text: It's just that, I, I'm gonna miss Joseph. I liked him. His wife, she was hot. [Chadler pushes him out the door by the face.]

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Why the hell didn't you tell me!

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: I'm sorry. What was I supposed to do stand up and shout 'Hey, Rachel, your butt is showing!'

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh my God this is sooo humiliating. I think the only thing that tops that was, was, was when I was in the eight grade and I had to sing the Copa Cabana in front of the entire school. I think I got about two lines into it before I ran and freaked out. Oh my God, my entire life is flashing before my eyes.

lines.speaker: ROSS
lines.text: Rach, hey look, I remember that, it wasn't so bad.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh Ross, would you stop, you got me, I'm dating you.

lines.text: Rachel!

lines.text: You told me you didn't see anything.

lines.speaker: MR. WINEBURG
lines.text: I tell ya a lot things!

lines.speaker: MRS. WINEBURG
lines.text: Well it's wonderful to have you up and about, again, dear.

lines.speaker: MR. WINEBURG
lines.text: Stay well.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Okay, now that is the third time someone has said something like that to me today.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh, hi!

lines.text: I know.

lines.text: I'm Mrs. Dr. Barry Hunter hyphen Farber.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Oh honey, I'm so proud of you, Min.

lines.speaker: BARRY
lines.text: Min. Oh Rach, you're still here, at our wedding, they were packing up the chopped liver about now.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Yeah, I love that story. Um, I got a question for you guys. Why do people keep is saying that is good to see me up and about?

lines.speaker: MINDY
lines.text: Well uh, after you ran out on your wedding, Barry's parents told people that you were sort of....insane.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: Insane!

lines.speaker: MINDY
lines.text: ...from the syphilis.

lines.speaker: RACHEL
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: BARRY
lines.text: Yeah, what are they gonna say you didn't love me anymore. Come on.

See now, tonight, all I really wanted was to make it though this evening with a little bit of grace and dignity. Well , I guess we can all agree that's not gonna happen.
